title: Fluid Antenna Assisted ISAC Design for Emerging Low-Altitude Scenarios
authors: Junyuan Fan, Jie Tang, Xiaokai Song, Kang Kang, Youjun Xiang, Cui Yang, Beixiong Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The integration of fluid antenna systems (FAS) into integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) networks offers promising reconfigurability for air–ground scenarios in low-altitude economic (LAE) environments. This paper investigates a monostatic FAS-aided ISAC architecture comprising a two-dimensional (2D) FAS-based transmit array and a fixed-position receive array, aiming to enhance the joint performance across multiple ground user equipments (GUEs) and one aerial user equipment (AUE). A joint optimization problem is formulated to maximize a weighted utility metric integrating the communication signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of the AUE and the sensing signal-to-clutter-plus-noise ratio (SCNR), subject to the transmit power constraint, inter-antenna spacing constraint, deployment geometry, and the minimum SINR requirement of GUEs. The resulting problem involves coupled spatial variables and fractional structures, which are addressed via an alternating optimization (AO) framework. Specifically, the receive beamformer is derived in closed form, the transmit beamformer is optimized via fractional programming (FP) and successive convex approximation (SCA), and the antenna positions are iteratively refined on a per-element basis through second-order Taylor expansion to construct convex surrogate upper bounds. Numerical results verify that the proposed FAS-enhanced ISAC system substantially outperforms benchmark schemes.

title: Low-Complexity Unimodular Waveform Design With Good Space-Time Correlation for Multi-Target Detection in MIMO Radar
authors: Xuyang Zhao, Jiangtao Wang, Shihao Yan, Yongchao Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper designs unimodular waveforms with good space-time correlation properties for multi-input multi-output (MIMO) radar systems. Unlike the widely studied approaches that only consider auto-correlation and cross-correlation, space-time correlation is incorporated into the design to reduce the impact of false target echoes on detection performance in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula>-target detection scenario. Based on Riemannian geometry theory, we analyze the geometric structure of the unimodular matrix constraint and construct a customized product manifold that matches the optimization constraints, which lays the foundation for low-complexity algorithm design. On this basis, we propose low-complexity algorithms for both normal-scale and large-scale antenna cases. Specifically, for the normal-scale case, a low-complexity unimodular manifold gradient descent (UM-GD) algorithm with a customized merging strategy and its accelerated version, unimodular manifold accelerated gradient descent (UM-AGD) algorithm, are developed. However, in large-scale case, as the number of antennas <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$M$</tex-math></inline-formula>, the waveform length <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and the number of delay bins <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$|\mathcal {D}|$</tex-math></inline-formula> increase, the merging strategy of UM-GD and UM-AGD introduces a significant storage burden, and the computational complexity remains high. To address this issue, we propose a customized unimodular manifold stochastic variance reduced gradient (UM-SVRG) algorithm. Compared with existing benchmark algorithms whose per-iteration computational complexity is approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(M^{4} + \text{3}~M^{2}~N + 3|\mathcal {D}| K^{2} MN)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, the proposed UM-SVRG algorithm significantly reduces it to about <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(|\mathcal {D}| K^{2} MN)$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Furthermore, with appropriate parameter selection, we provide theoretical convergence guarantees for the UM-GD, UM-AGD, and UM-SVRG algorithms. Finally, numerical results validate the effectiveness of the proposed UM-GD, UM-AGD and UM-SVRG algorithms, showing that UM-GD and UM-AGD are more suitable for normal-scale case, while UM-SVRG performs better in large-scale case.

title: Passivity-Based Active Damping Control Design for Grid-Following Inverters in Weak Grids
authors: Yan Xu, Xianglong Liu, Jun Liu, Yuan Wang, Ji Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Grid-following (GFL) inverters may cause serious stability issues when connected to weak grids. To enhance system stability, the configuration of active damping control (ADC) for GFL inverters is investigated based on passivity theory. Passivity index analysis shows that the control coefficients of the ADC contribute in opposite ways to system stability at low and high frequencies, leading to a trade-off. After careful consideration of the trade-off, design guidelines for ADC are provided. Grid strength and phase-locked loop bandwidth are considered in the design, which enables the GFL inverters to operate reliably under extremely weak grid conditions and with high phase-locked loop bandwidths. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated by simulation and experimental results.

title: Multi-Objective Sizing of an Electric Aircraft Power System Under n-1 Contingency Constraints: Weight–Volume–Losses Trade-offs
authors: Ahmad Naderi, Abbas Mehraban, Michael Terörde
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: A multi-objective sizing framework for the Electric Power System (EPS) of a reference short-range electrified aircraft is presented under n-1 contingency constraints. The workflow integrates database-driven component sizing and selection in MATLAB with steady-state load-flow and contingency evaluation in DIgSILENT PowerFactory, so that the effect of post-outage re-sizing can be quantified consistently. A radial DC architecture is considered together with multiple propulsion distributions and a discrete DC grid voltage sweep. The EPS is evaluated in three states: Before Contingency (BeCon), After Contingency (AfCon), and After Contingency with Loss Refinement (AfConLF). The novelty of this work lies in the integrated contingency-aware and loss-refined multi-objective sizing framework, which enables a quantitatively grounded comparison of aircraft EPS architectures at system level. Multi-objective selection is carried out for the main EPS components and for the overall EPS using total weight, total volume, and steady-state losses at the maximum take-off operating point. The results show that enforcing contingency compliance shifts the feasible trade space toward higher weight, volume, and losses, with the strongest penalty occurring in the DC–DC conversion stage and upstream DC distribution. The AfConLF step preserves the hardware ratings obtained in AfCon while refining the loss estimate by using post-contingency load factors, thereby improving the realism of the steady-state assessment without materially increasing weight or volume. In addition, the influence of DC-grid voltage and motor design on the resulting EPS trade space is quantified, providing practical guidance for selecting contingency-compliant architectures for future electric aircraft.

title: Safe Reinforcement Learning for Nonlinear Multiagent Systems Based on Min–Max DMPC
authors: Yang Peng, Huaicheng Yan, Qiwei Liu, Haining Yan, Yuanyi Zheng, Youmin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: This article presents a safe reinforcement learning (RL) framework for nonlinear multiagent systems (MASs) based on min–max distributed model predictive control (DMPC). The proposed method employs min–max DMPC as a robust baseline to generate control strategies, optimizing closed-loop performance in the presence of disturbances while ensuring interpretability and safety. To mitigate the conservatism inherent in traditional robust DMPC due to its reliance on precise models and fixed disturbance bounds, safe RL is introduced to adaptively update the controller parameters and disturbance sets online. The proposed parameter update mechanism of safe RL formally guarantees the recursive feasibility of the DMPC algorithm during the learning process. Furthermore, theoretical analyses of closed-loop stability are provided. The effectiveness and scalability of the proposed method are validated through two simulation examples.

title: Machine Learning-Based Stress Detection Using Robust HRV Features and Person-Specific Normalization
authors: Maximilian Fecke, Jakob Rehof
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents an artificial intelligence (AI) system for the electrocardiogram (ECG)-based detection of psychological stress, developed as part of a prototypical digital assistance system for nurses. The AI system employs the following key steps: After initial preprocessing including ECG windowing and heartbeat detection, 70 time, frequency, and time-frequency domain features are extracted from each window’s heartbeats. All features are descriptors of heart rate variability (HRV), a well-known stress indicator. They are primarily calculated from sophisticated estimates of NN intervals, NN interval differences, respiration rates, and autonomic nervous system (ANS) activity. Novelties of this step include an increased noise robustness of estimated respiration rates and the derivation of ANS activity features from the integral pulse frequency modulation model of heartbeat generation. Furthermore, the entire feature extraction process is explicitly designed for robustness against irregularities like beat detection errors and ectopic beats, which could severely distort HRV analysis. All features are normalized with a novel, person-specific approach based on short baseline data sections. This lightweight personalization serves to increase classification performance while avoiding costly alternatives like person-specific modeling, and actively prevents normalization data leakage. Finally, stress detection is performed using a machine learning model of type random forest, gradient boosting machine, or multilayer perceptron. Hyperparameters of all steps were rigorously tuned using Bayesian optimization and manual search on the popular datasets WESAD and SWELL-KW as well as their union. The resulting stress detection performances under leave-one-subject-out cross-validation consistently surpassed the comparable state of the art.

title: AdaptGen: A Problem-Adaptive Solution Template Generation Technique for Online Programming Platforms
authors: Guowei Zhang, Weiqin Zou, Xiaowei Zhang, Jingxuan Zhang, Jifeng Xuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract:
Online programming platforms that offer various programming tasks play a crucial role in helping programmers enhance their coding skills. Programming tasks posted for different application scenarios may follow the same or similar programming patterns in their solutions. This leads programmers to repeatedly write not just the core code of problem-solving, but also the same basic framework and some peripheral code (such as variable declarations and input/output handling) that are needed to make their solutions executable. Repeatedly writing boilerplate or well-mastered algorithmic frameworks wastes time and adds little value for programmers focused on skill-specific practice.

Toward this, we propose to develop AdaptGen, a problemadaptive code template generation method for online programming platforms. AdaptGen analyzes and extracts solution patterns from various programming problem solutions (i.e., code accepted by online programming platforms) and generates templates tailored to each problem. More specifically, AdaptGen is built on genetic programming and uses a linear hashing sequence encoding strategy to represent solutions. It incorporates selection, crossover, and de-duplication operators to maintain diversity in the evolution process, and a fitness function tailored to generate solution templates. These templates are then abstracted and structured with a flexible core-code-hiding mechanism, enabling programmers of different experience levels to practice efficiently.

We evaluated AdaptGen using two datasets from LeetCode and NowCoder, containing a total of 997 tasks and over 3,200 solution categories. Results show that AdaptGen successfully generates usable templates for 77%-84% of solution categories, with 80% of the templates performing well in manual evaluations. It also outperforms seven advanced representative large language models (LLMs), achieving the best overall performance in template quality, consistency, and generation efficiency. To validate Adapt- Gen’s effectiveness in real-world programming environments, we further conduct a user study involving live coding practice by programmers in online programming platforms, which effectively demonstrated its utility in practical application scenarios.

title: HEAL Lite: A Smart Wearable Tremor Monitoring and prediction System with AI and Hybrid Storage
authors: Jenyfal Sampson, S. P. Velmurugan, Karuppasamy T, M.Y.Mohamed Irfan, A.P.Vijaybaskar, Dhanushkumar S
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This work is about a device called HEAL Lite. The HEAL Lite device is a system that is made to detect and monitor tremors in people with neurological problems like Parkinsons disease. The device has a small computer called an ESP32 microcontroller and a sensor called an MPU6050 that measures movement. This sensor gets information about hand tremors in time. The device can look at this information. Decide if the tremor is normal or not. Then it can show this information on a screen. At the time the device sends this information to a special website where doctors can look at it from far away. The device also has a part for doctors. This part is called the Doctors Monitoring and Medical Station. It is also based on the ESP32 microcontroller. It has a card to store information and a clock to keep track of time. This means that the device can store information about patients in a safe way even when it is not connected to the internet. Doctors can look at this information on a screen to see how their patients are doing. When we tested the device it worked well. It could correctly identify tremors than 90 percent of the time. It could also respond quickly in less than 200 milliseconds. The device can store information for a time. The HEAL Lite device is a solution, for monitoring tremors and helping doctors understand neurological problems. It is not expensive. Can be used by many people.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for QoS-Aware Adaptive Routing in IoT Manets
authors: Umamaheswari M, Annamalai Saravanan R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) has accelerated the deployment of Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) as a foundation for pervasive connectivity in dynamic, infrastructure-less environments. However, ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) in IoT-driven MANETs remains a significant challenge due to node mobility, resource constraints, and unpredictable traffic patterns. Traditional routing protocols such as AODV and OLSR struggle to adapt to dynamic topologies while meeting stringent QoS requirements such as latency, throughput, packet delivery ratio, and energy efficiency. Recent advances in deep learning offer promising solutions for adaptive routing by enabling predictive intelligence, context-aware decision-making, and real-time optimization. This paper presents an in-depth exploration of deep learning approaches for QoS-aware adaptive routing in IoT MANETs. Various architectures, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTMs), Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), are examined for their ability to optimize routing under QoS constraints. The work highlights key challenges such as scalability, real-time execution, data scarcity, and security vulnerabilities while outlining emerging directions involving federated learning, edge intelligence, and explainable AI. By synthesizing current advancements and identifying open issues, this paper contributes to advancing next-generation adaptive routing strategies for IoT-enabled MANETs.

title: Evaluating LLMs for Operational Technology (OT) Cybersecurity Risk Assessment Across Structured and Unstructured Inputs
authors: Padma Iyenghar, Jonny Messerknecht, Christopher Zimmer, Claudio Gregorio, Elke Pulvermüller
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Operational Technology (OT) cybersecurity risk assessments are mandated by the upcoming EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA), yet they remain largely manual, expert-driven, and based on unstructured technical documentation. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer potential for automation, prior studies mainly examine isolated reasoning steps under prestructured inputs, leaving LLM behavior under realistic industrial conditions insufficiently understood. This paper presents a systematic empirical study of LLMbased OT cybersecurity risk assessment for industrial devices in factory communication systems. Two input regimes are evaluated: document-driven assessment using unstructured device manuals and scenario-driven assessment using structured benchmark inputs. The evaluation follows the IEC 62443-3-2 Zone–Conduit Requirement (ZCR) 5 workflow and applies MITRE EMB3Dbased threat modeling with a consistent risk matrix across multiple proprietary and open-weight LLMs and prompting strategies. The results show that LLMs reliably support EMB3D-based threat identification and achieve moderate agreement with expert baselines for impact estimation, while likelihood estimation exhibits systematic underestimation and instability, particularly for document-driven inputs. Further, input modality has a stronger influence on assessment quality than model choice, and few-shot prompting improves accuracy more consistently than rule-based prompting. Overall, the findings indicate that LLMs are currently best suited as decision-support components within IEC 62443- and CRA-aligned OT cybersecurity workflows, improving scalability and consistency while preserving human control over final risk decisions.

title: A Novel Hybrid GWO-FIS: An Optimized Fuzzy Inference System Tuned by Grey Wolf Optimizer for Real-Time Intruder Risk Assessment in Video Surveillance
authors: Parupkar Singh, Law Kumar Singh, Vishan Kumar Gupta, Tushar Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: For intruder detection traditional automated surveillance systems are often rigid, relying on binary thresholds that lead to high false-positive rates and significant "alarm fatigue." These systems lack context-aware reasoning of a human operator. This paper proposes a novel, hybrid soft computing model (GWO-FIS) for intelligent, real-time intruder risk assessment. The proposed architecture integrates a state-of-the-art deep learning detector (YOLOv8) for high-performance feature extraction with an explainable Fuzzy Inference System (FIS) for decision-making. The YOLOv8 model extracts crucial features such as person-class confidence and precise location. These features are then contextualized by a user-defined polygonal "restricted zone" and fed into the FIS, which evaluates the intrusion risk. To automatically optimize the membership functions of the FIS the primary contribution of this research is the application of a bio-inspired metaheuristic and the Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO). To maximize the system’s Average Precision (AP) on a validation dataset this self-tuning process optimizes the fuzzy parameters. On comparing to static and manually-tuned systems, the resulting GWO-tuned model shows statistically significant improvements in accuracy, adaptability, and explainability, providing a robust and intelligent solution for modern security and surveillance challenges.

title: MANTIS: Towards Multiparty Communication for Bluetooth LE Audio
authors: Theo Gasteiger, Markus Schuß, Carlo Alberto Boano, Kay Römer
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Bluetooth's Broadcast Isochronous Streams (BISes) enable scalable one-to-many audio dissemination, while Connected Isochronous Streams (CISes) support bidirectional communication between two parties via one-to-one connections. Yet neither is suitable for building ad-hoc communication systems in which a multitude of devices can communicate with each other bidirectionally. BISes are inherently unidirectional, and CISes do not scale. We fill this gap by presenting MANTIS, a mechanism that opens a path toward multiparty communication for Bluetooth LE Audio solely using BISes, despite the fact that this is neither supported nor anticipated by the current Bluetooth Core Specification. By introducing the concept of a token and a coordinated token-passing mechanism, MANTIS transforms inherently unidirectional streams into a flexible system where devices can seamlessly alternate between broadcaster and receiver roles, ensuring a single active broadcaster at any time without interrupting the ongoing, underlying isochronous data stream. Additionally, we implement MANTIS on real-world hardware and validate its operation. By bridging the scalability limitations of CISes and the unidirectionality of BISes, MANTIS introduces a new communication paradigm for Bluetooth, enabling ad-hoc, infrastructure-free audio scenarios previously considered impractical or unattainable.

title: Smart Pilgrimage: Integrating IoT and AI for Crowd Management in Chardham Yatra
authors: Mayank Mamgain, Neelam Upadhyay, Bhupnesh Kumar, Siddharth Thapliyal, Jageshwar Nath Singh, Sandeep Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The Chardham Yatra is one of the most crowded and most remote high-density religious pilgrimages that takes place every year in the Indian Himalayas; there are many travelers who journey through a long and inaccessible area. For years, traditional methods of managing crowds did not work well to handle traffic; protect the traveler's health and safety; or quickly respond to emergencies in such a remote location. This study has proposed a framework using Internet of Things (IoT) sensors and Artificial Intelligence (AI) Models to provide real-time crowd density information; real-time predictions about expected flow rates; and real-time decision-making to support crowd managers at the Chardham Yatra site. The framework uses dispersed sensor technologies to collect real-time data from travelers and their surroundings (infrared counters, environmental sensors and mobile tracking systems), and AI algorithms to estimate crowd densities; predict how the crowds will move; and identify when something is going wrong.

title: Emulation of the blood flow in the carotid, brachial, and coronary arteries by driving the pump of an emulator phantom using a system based on the mean flow velocity of arterial curves
authors: Demetrio Fabian Garcia Nocetti, Martin Fuentes Cruz, Ernesto Rubio Acosta, Adalberto Joel Duran Ortega, Juan Antonio Contreras Arvizu, Pedro Jesus Acevedo Contla
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: None

title: Reduction of Episiotomies in the Birth Pathway: Application of the M.A.S.T.E.R. Methodology at the Evangelical Betania Hospital
authors: Francesco Tarsitano, Assunta Piccolo, Cira Palomba, Maria Anna Stingone, Mara Morra, Vincenzo Bottino, Antonio Nardone, Arianna Scala, Giovanni Improta
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: None

title: Latency Forecasting for Real-time Brokerage Data Pipelines Under Market Volatility
authors: Deep Patel
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: In this paper, the forecasting of infrastructure latency in real-time brokerage data pipelines, considering the state of volatility in the market, is examined and assessed. Latency is defined as the time difference from the start of trading to confirmation of trading. Latency is critical to service quality and trading performance. Although previous research has mainly concentrated on market microstructure and optimization of systems independently, there is still insufficient research on the interlinking of market microstructure and infrastructure latency. For this purpose, this study proposes a machine learning method that incorporates high-frequency limit order books to forecast infrastructure latency. The data used in this study is based on 17,109 one-minute ADA/USDT trading activities with 156 features. Three regression models are used to evaluate and forecast infrastructure latency: LightGBM, Random Forest, and XGBoost. From the empirical analysis, LightGBM is observed to be performing better compared to other regression models. The mean absolute error is 5.38ms and has an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.9239. From the analysis and evaluation of infrastructure latency within real-time brokerage data pipelines during periods of market volatility, signals based on market microstructures offer a strong basis for forecasting infrastructure latency.

title: Detection of Fake Social Media Accounts using Machine Learning and Behavioral Analysis
authors: P Celcia, Cynthia J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Social media platforms are rapidly being developed, so they are filled with false accounts that often-spread misinformation and commit fraud which undermines trust in the site and integrity of the platform. The identification of those accounts is challenging with given diverse user behavior and significant amount of data set. Here, we propose a machine learning-based method in terms of profile characteristics along with behavioral trends and graph-based relational features to further accelerate detection. This work developed and evaluated multiple models, including Logistic regression, XGBoost, Autoencoder with Random Forest and Artificial Neural Network with XGBoost. We use a meta-level stacking technique to enhance predictive abilities. The accuracy and the ROC-AUC analysis can be studied to evaluate the models. The stacked ensemble model provides the best performance and accuracy is 99.1%. SHAP-based interpretability is employed to examine the most important influencing features and increase transparency. It proves to be the practical and reliable tools to discover fake and secure social media accounts.

title: Real-Time IoT Monitoring of Battery Packs in EVs using Thermal Imaging and Edge AI Models
authors: Boban Mathews, Bhushan Bharat Shaharkar, Kailash Chandra Panda, N.Karthick, Ramya Maranan, M.SivaramKrishnan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) battery packs are susceptible to non-uniform heat which highly impacts safety, life, and energy efficiency. The traditional types of monitoring are based on point thermocouples that overlook localized hotspots and fast variation of temperatures. This paper presents an IoT-integrated thermal imaging system to detect potential anomalies in EV lithium-ion battery packs in real-time with the use of Edge AI models to map the temperature image accurately and measure the early signs of its potential development. An ESP32/Jetson Nano edge processor with a low-power FLIR Lepton 3.5 thermal sensor can be used to record high-resolution frames of temperature at 9Hz, and therefore can be used to do continuous monitoring without relying on the cloud. A thermal drift detection, hotspots, and abnormal temperature-rise prediction MobileNet CNN model is trained on an edge with a latency less than 80ms. The IoT dashboard is an MQTT system that delivers real-time temperature profiles, averaged temperature of the module, index of the hotspots zone and the forecasted thermal-runaway risk. When a 48 V EV battery module is loaded with drive-cycle loads and experimented, peak deviation of a hotspot is 12.6°C and 96.4 % of anomaly detection. The Communication overhead is also minimized 72 % with edge inference over cloud-based systems. This is a scalable, low-latency solution which can be deployed on two-wheelers, three-wheelers, and light electric vehicles to offer a safety-critical EV battery thermal health monitoring platform.

title: An Automated Domain and Schema-Agnostic Workflow for Ontology Verbalization using AIML and ALICE Bot
authors: Kaneeka Vidanage, Sidath Weerasinghe, Thamini Perera
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Effective ontology construction is highly dependent on the efficiency in collaboration among the domain specialists and the ontologists. Constant and dedicated cooperation of both parties is vital for proper ideology synchronization which leads to successfully achieving a finalized ontological design. Non-technical domain specialists like bankers, farmers, lawyers and medical specialists lack knowledge of semantic concepts like Resource Descriptive Framework (RDF), Web Ontology Language (OWL), SPARQL querying, etc. This hinders the proper glueing of the conceptualizations of domain specialists and ontologists. This research introduces a domain-and-schema-independent verbalization algorithm using Artificial Intelligence Markup Language (AIML) and a converted Alice Bot engine. It can convert knowledge embedded in an ontology into layman-understandable English. This facilitates effective knowledge verification amidst the iterative and incremental process of developing an ontology. The efficacy of the algorithm has been tested across three different domains and yielded an overall accuracy of 82%. Most of the existing verbalization algorithms are dependent on domain and schema. The novel algorithm proposed in this paper has resolved this bottleneck by making verbalization possible regardless of domain.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Class Nail Disease Classification
authors: Boopathi V, Boobalan S, Aswinth M, V. Akisha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Nail diseases are significant clues to the diagnosis of both dermatological and general body conditions, and therefore early and correct diagnosis is essential. However, manual diagnosis is time-consuming and may require that the specialist takes the diagnostic call. To overcome this difficulty, an automated multi-class nail disease classification framework based on dermoscopic images using deep learning techniques is proposed in this research. A comparative evaluation was performed based on four architecture namely Conventional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), DenseNet169, EfficientNet-B2 and MobileNetV2-based model using a Transfer learning and data augmentation strategy. Experimental results show that the proposed MobileNetV2 model can reach overall classification accuracy of 99.2%, which is better than other architectures. In addition, the model performance in terms of precision, recall, specificity, and F1-score is high, and AUC-ROC value is close to 1.00 for most classes of diseases, which indicates the high discriminative ability and generalization performance. In addition, the mobile architecture of MobileNetV2 leads to lower computational complexity, which is also suitable for efficient deployment. These results show the potential of the proposed framework to be a reliable tool for automated screening of nail diseases, which may help dermatologists in the clinical decision-making process and facilitate accessible diagnostic assistance for telemedicine and remote healthcare applications.

title: Random Environmental Artifact Detection in Motor Vibration Signals Using TSA Residual Energy
authors: Robertas Ūselis, Raimondas Pomarnacki, Artūras Serackis
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: Real-world vibration measurements used for motor condition monitoring are often contaminated by random environmental artifacts (e.g., nearby traffic) that are not rotation-synchronous and can bias time-synchronous features or trigger false alarms. This paper presents a lightweight TSA-based method to detect such artifacts in a healthy BLDC motor under near steady-state operation. Accelerometer data (ADXL355) are acquired by a Raspberry Pi Pico 2 (RP2350). The signal is segmented by Hall pulses, phase-normalized via resampling, and a Time Synchronous Averaging (TSA) template is computed as the nominal baseline. Non-synchronous disturbances are quantified using per-revolution residual energy and flagged using a global 3σ threshold with peak consolidation. In a 528 s recording at ∼509 RPM (4,480 revolutions), the method identifies five distinct transient events, matching the expected number of disturbance episodes. The approach is interpretable, compute-efficient, and suitable as a preprocessing step for edge-based monitoring.

title: Design and Comparative Analysis of Ultra-Low Power, High-Speed CMOS Schmitt Triggers in 16nm FinFET Technology
authors: Shilagani Shivaprasad, Rohith Reddy Enugala, Sathwika Kokku, J.Ajayan, Kandukuri Shiva Rao, Godishela Ruthvik
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: In this work, we analyze the performance of 6T, 8T, and 14T nanoscale CMOS Schmitt Trigger (CMOS-ST) circuits implemented using 16nm FinFET technology with varying supply voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>) from 0.5 V to 1.0 V under different temperatures (T). The role of frequency of input signal (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">in</inf>) ranging from 100 MHz to 2 GHz is also studied using Synopsys HSPICE Tool. The best 8T-CST achieves an ultra-low power consumption (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">cons</inf>) of 0.2291 µW at 27°C, though it exhibits high sensitivity to thermal induced leakage in high temperatures. On the other hand, the 14T-CST circuit and 6T-CST circuit exhibit an Inverse Temperature Dependence (ITD) effect that guarantees superior electro-thermal stability. Under 2 GHz, the 14T-CST maintains a remarkably stable P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">cons</inf> shifting minimally from 3.565 µW to 3.624 µW at 27°C-107°C, while leveraging a multi-stage threshold-shifting mechanism for superior leakage suppression and stable logic transitions. Remarkably, the 14T-CST as a highly robust, energy-efficient for high-speed IoT edge devices.

title: AI-Powered Transformer Framework for Intelligent Natural Language to SQL Query Generation
authors: P. Arul Prakash, Shakthi S, Sri Santhiya M P, Shreeja A V, Subash S, Eyamini C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Natural language database interfaces frequently do not generate SQL that is structurally correct when the queries involve complex joins, nested queries, and queries that are ambiguous in terms of schema. The work introduces an AI-powered Transformer framework that supports bidirectional semantic encoding, schema linking, and constraint-aware SQL generation. The model uses a pre-trained T5-Large encoder as well as a relational schema graph made up of 5,752 table-column nodes and 14,986 relationship edges allowing for reasoning over explicit structure. The framework is evaluated on the Spider and WikiSQL benchmarks of 10,181 training queries, 1,034 development queries, and 15,878 natural-language questions. The experiments achieve 84.72% exact-match accuracy on Spider and 92.41% logical-form accuracy on WikiSQL, surpassing the prior best baselines by 6.18% and 4.35%, respectively. SQL generation times were average 1.83 seconds and were brought down to 0.97 seconds for real time deployment. These results confirm that transformer driven semantic parsing leads to improvements in correctness, generalization and execution fidelity in NL-to-SQL translation. These results indicate that the framework proposed yields a gain in exact-match accuracy, reliability of execution and efficiency in latency in all types of database scenarios.

title: Guava Disease Classification Using a Multi-Featured Multi-Staged Transfer Learning Algorithm with Progressive Fine-Tuning
authors: Isaac Angelo M. Dioses, Jennilyn C. Mina, Jeffrey T. Dellosa, Arri Steven P. Dulnuan, Jesusimo L. Dioses, Junrie B. Matias
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Guava diseases pose a significant threat to crop yield and quality in tropical agriculture, underscoring the need for reliable and automated disease detection systems. This study proposes a multi-featured, multi-staged transfer learning framework with progressive fine-tuning for guava disease classification using leaf images. A publicly available Kaggle dataset comprising 2,647 images across three classes, Anthracnose, Fruit Fly infestation, and Healthy Guava, was utilized. The proposed model integrates feature representations from two pretrained convolutional backbones, MobileNetV2 and EfficientNetB4, and refines them through a three-stage training strategy that includes head training and selective fine-tuning of each backbone. Experimental results demonstrate a clear performance progression across training stages, with the final model achieving high accuracy and balanced class-wise performance. Evaluation using precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix analysis confirms the robustness and generalization capability of the proposed approach. The findings highlight the effectiveness of progressive multi-staged transfer learning in improving disease classification accuracy and support its potential application in precision agriculture for early disease detection and improved crop management.

title: Deep Learning Instance Segmentation–Driven Detection of Colonic Polyps in Pediatric Colonoscopy
authors: Bryan Anthony S. Al Shidhani, Chesney Kayzle A. Bumakil, Nicholas Joaquin E. Santos, Angel Grace L. Seballos, Lysa V. Comia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Early and accurate detection of colonic polyps is essential for preventing the progression of colorectal disease, particularly in pediatric patients where lesion characteristics and data availability differ from adult populations. This study presents a deep learning–based instance segmentation framework for automated detection and precise localization of colonic polyps in pediatric colonoscopy images. A pediatric-focused dataset was curated, annotated, and augmented to support robust model training and evaluation. The proposed model achieved strong quantitative performance, attaining a Box mAP@0.50 of 0.966 and a Mask mAP@0.50 of 0.974, with high precision, recall, and a peak F1 score of approximately 0.96, indicating a balanced trade-off between detection sensitivity and reliability. Confusion matrix analysis confirmed effective discrimination between polyp and background regions, correctly identifying approximately 95% of true polyp instances with minimal false positives. The model maintained computational efficiency with a low inference cost of 9.6 GFLOPs and low end-to-end latency, supporting real-time clinical deployment. Qualitative evaluations on unseen images further demonstrated accurate polyp segmentation, while a web-based Gradio application showcased practical real-time inference and accessibility. The proposed approach highlights the potential of instance segmentation–driven deep learning systems to assist pediatric gastroenterologists in early polyp detection and enhance diagnostic confidence during colonoscopy procedures.

title: Auditable Drag and Drop AI Pipelines: OCR LLM Composition with Rubric Scoring, HITL, and Provenance First MLOps
authors: K Ravishankar, Mithran M. V., B. K. Prethika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: We present an auditable, drag-and-drop platform for composing OCR–LLM pipelines with explainability, rubric-based scoring, human-in-the-loop (HITL) review, and provenance-first MLOps. Blocks encapsulate OCR detectors/recognizers, promp table LLM extractors, XAI widgets, scoring policies, and review queues. A Node-RED-style orchestrator records configuration, model/version, inputs/outputs, and human actions to a relational store for replay and audit. We formalize rubric scoring as a constrained, weighted objective and pair it with explanations (Grad-CAM/Score-CAM overlays, token-level rationales) to justify decisions. HITL patterns (escalation, adjudication, exception) close the loop for reliability and compliance. On ICDAR SROIE (receipts) and FUNSD (forms) we report OCR/KIE F1, end-to-end latency, reviewer time per operation, and provenance coverage. Results show competitive accuracy with traceable decision trails, offering a practical template for trustworthy, policy-constrained document AI in production.

title: FPGA Implementation and Comparative Analysis of Chaotic Map Based Pseudo Random Number Generators
authors: K. Rajesh, H.Victor Du John, A. Tamilselvan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Analysis of different chaotic mapping technique for the generation of secured Pseudo Random Number Generator (PRNG) is proposed here. Details of selection on the parameter values suitable in order to obtain the higher entropy value and Lyapunov exponent of the hybrid chaotic mapping technique such as Bernoulli map, Logistic map, Tent map, Zigzag map, Sine map is incorporated. The individual performance is analyzed by using MATLAB R2025a software using the linear equations of the various map respectively. Firstly, Bifurcation diagram is generated to know the possible common peak values of the different map. Secondly, Lyapunov Exponent curve is used to find the best parameters for the randomness. Third, among the different parameters, four peek values are used to generate the scatter diagram and finally, histogram representation for the peak parameter is determined. The increased randomness and unpredictability future of this model will be useful for application like data encryption and decryption, video compression and security purpose. The Spartan-3E FPGA hardware implementation was done to check the utilization efficiency of various chaotic maps. From the result, it has been observed that Bernoulli, Logistic and Sine map utilized minimum hardware resources and it will be used for cryptographic application. In addition, there is a possibility for accuracy and performance with the AI/ML algorithm based PRNG using the chaotic mapping techniques.

title: A Streamlit-based AutoML Framework for Student Academic Performance Prediction
authors: Dina Fitria Murad, Deryan Everestha Maured, Bambang Dwi Wijanarko, Silvia Ayunda Murad, Siti Maryam, Shahnorbanun Sahran
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Accurately predicting student academic performance is a key challenge in educational data mining, with significant implications for early intervention, personalized learning, and institutional decision-making. While previous studies have implemented various machine learning models to support academic performance prediction, most approaches require extensive manual preprocessing, model comparison, and hyperparameter tuning processes that are time-consuming and difficult to scale. This study proposes a Streamlit-based AutoML Framework designed to automate the end-to-end process of modeling and predicting student academic outcomes. The framework processes a multi-sheet Excel dataset of 566 records consisting of 3 years (academic years 2023-2025) of learning transactions for Science (IPA) and Social Science (IPS) majors, applies an integrated data preparation workflow, performs feature engineering, and generates categorical performance labels (High, Medium, Low). Using PyCaret's AutoML capabilities, the framework independently identifies the best-performing model for each major based on a weighted F1 score. The resulting model is implemented in an interactive Streamlit application that enables real-time prediction, feature importance interpretation, and identification of at-risk students. Results show that the AutoML-generated model delivers competitive predictive performance with minimal manual intervention, confirming the suitability of automated machine learning tools for practical educational analytics. Combined with an accessible web-based interface, it offers a scalable, interpretable, and easy-to-implement approach to academic performance prediction.

title: Pruning-Aware CUR Decomposition for ViT Model Approximation
authors: Suguru Mori, Kyoko Sudo
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Processing data in edge devices such as smartphones or IoT devices is effective in minimizing latency and ensuring security. Model reduction is needed to adapt high performance deep learning models to small devices with small memories and disk volumes. Recent research on model reduction includes quantization, distillation, pruning, and matrix decomposition. In this work, we present a systematic evaluation of pruning-aware CUR decomposition for Vision Transformer (ViT) MLP layers. We analyze how CUR decomposition behaves when combined with pruning, and clarify when it outperforms SVD or pruningonly baselines under comparable parameter reduction ratios.

title: Optimized Brain Tumor Segmentation and Detection Using Machine Learning and DeepNeural Networks
authors: T.Boopathy, M.Kavitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Accurately segmenting brain tumors in medical imaging is a crucial step in modern healthcare diagnostics. Traditional manual classification methods, which rely heavily on human interpretation, often result in inefficiencies and errors, particularly when analyzing large datasets. The complexity of brain tumor identification stems from the diverse appearance of tumors and the similarity between abnormal and healthy tissues, highlighting the need for automated segmentation techniques. This study presents a novel approach for extracting brain tumors from two-dimensional magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans. The segmentation process utilizes The Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) grouping approach, succeeded by classification utilizing both traditional machine learning models as well as deep learning-based artificial neural networks (CNNs). Experiments were conducted using a real-time dataset that includes various tumor shapes, sizes, and intensities. Six traditional classifiers—Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Logistic Regression, Naïve Bayes, and Random Forest— were implemented using Scikit-learn. Meanwhile, a CNN model developed with Keras and TensorFlow achieved a classification accuracy of 94.25%, surpassing the performance of traditional approaches. The principal objective of this research is to enhance the distinction between normal and diseased brain tissues through the analysis of texture and statistical characteristics. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of deep learning in medical imaging, providing a more accurate and efficient alternative to traditional segmentation techniques.

title: AI-Enabled Intelligent Monitoring System for Medication Adherence and Lifestyle Management
authors: P. Arul Prakash, Preethi A G, Veerachinnu M, Naveen N, Kabilesh N, Eyamini C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Medication non-adherence and unregulated lifestyle behavior are part of about 41% of avoidable hospitalizations among chronic-disease patients. This study presents an AI-Enabled Intelligent Monitoring System that integrates multimodal sensing, personalized behavioral analytics, and real-time adherence verification. The system incorporates a Bi-LSTM medication-intake classifier trained on 4,567 annotated medication-events and a transformer-based lifestyle assessment model analyzing 312,480 activity-pattern frames over a period of 226 days. An experiment involving 186 patients with high blood pressure and type2diabetes confirms this where our medication-intake detection accuracy improves by 34.7% yielding an overall adherence-verification accuracy of 92.14%. The prediction error for lifestyle-risk was 0.18 RMSE, better than three baseline models. This combined intervention raised general adherence from 63.2% to 87.5% in the trial period. These results support the viability of the system to help ongoing assessment, tailored support, and clinically significant improvements in adherence.

title: GPU-Powered Crop Quality Forecasting: Integrating CUDA-YOLO Detection with RF-XGBoost Classification
authors: Suryanarayanaraju Saripalle, M.Nagamani, V.S.Saranya, Venkatarathnam.K, Siva Kumar Pathuri, Seeniappan Kaliappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Smart agriculture combines drones, artificial intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), and data analytics to solve major problems in the field, including increased food prices, water shortages, climate change, and labor shortages. IoT sensors can be used to track the health of crops in real-time, whereas edge computing can be used to make quick decisions on farms. The AI models can be used to predict diseases of plants, pest attacks, and even the optimal time to plant a particular plant, which will enhance the efficiency of resources and environmental sustainability. The given paper draws a comparison between deep learning and traditional machine learning in terms of crop quality prediction and suggests a fast and real-time CUDA-based hybrid model of RF-XGBoost and YOLO. The result of the experiments reveals that the suggested method has a precision of approximately 96 percent, which is higher than the accuracy of SVM (88 percent) and Decision Tree (91 percent) in precision, speed and scale and can support data-driven and sustainable precision agriculture.

title: A Weighted Ensemble Approach for Enhanced IoT Attack Classification using Optimized Machine Learning Models
authors: A. Tharmalingam, S. Sivaraman, J. Manimegalai, B. Navaneetha, V. Ganesan, M. Sivaraman
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) continues to grow quickly and has caused a paradigm shift in how people live. However, the problem regarding safety is growing along with these technological features. There are many notorious threats in the IoT domain that can impede the services or have potential threats to the users’ personal data. ML can be a potential remedy to identify the IoT threats by analyzing the pattern of the attack. There are many ML algorithms such as Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes and Logistic Regression. Although a single algorithm may have a low detection rate because of high bias or high variance, to overcome this problem, we proposed an ensemble model that sums up the strength of the various ML algorithms such as SVM, naïve bayes, and logistic regression, resulting in a high detection rate. Moreover, each individual model is undergone for parameter tuning with the help of GridSearchCV. Further soft-voting ensemble method is employed to combine these classifiers, which resulting an ensemble model that outperform the individual models across the various metrics. The proposed framework achieved 99% accuracy, demonstrating the effectiveness of optimized weighted ensemble models in reliably identifying IoT security threats.

title: Directional Weight-Aware Scaling Iterative Closest Point (DWA-SICP) for Point Cloud Registration
authors: Xin Lu, Akio Kimura, Mengbo You, Kouichi Konno
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Three-dimensional point-cloud registration underlies many tasks in robotics, computer vision, and metrology, yet remains fragile in the face of noise, outliers, scaling, and partial overlap. Classical ICP and its scaling extension (SICP) are fast and closed-form but treat all directions equally, while globaloptimization and probabilistic methods boost accuracy at a heavy computational cost. We propose Directional Weight-Aware Scaling ICP (DWA-SICP), which for each point builds a small weight matrix from its local covariance, blends source and target information, then embeds these into a single nearest-neighbor search plus SVD-based Procrustes update. On standard benchmarks (Bunny, Happy Buddha, Dragon) with a 25° rotation, a scale factor of 1.1, a small translation, and additive Gaussian noise with standard deviation 0.005, DWA-SICP achieves average RMSE in 4.30e-3 and MAE in 1.20e-2 in about 5 seconds, surpassing ICP, SICP, PC, NDT, and FGR in accuracy, and running orders of magnitude faster than heavyweight global methods such as GO. Our results show that simple directional weighting within closedform updates offers a practical path to high-precision, real-time registration.

title: Quality Consistency in Human–Machine Collaborative Services: Equilibrium Strategies under Incomplete-Information Dynamic Games with Online Calibration
authors: Wu Zhunan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: To address the challenges of frequent service quality fluctuations, unstable strategy interactions, and incomplete information in continuously operating human-machine collaborative service systems, this paper constructs a combined coupled framework of collaborative decision-making and online calibration for quality consistency control from the perspective of incomplete information dynamic game theory. The model treats human service agents and machine intelligence agents as rational decision-makers with heterogeneous types and risk preferences. It characterizes their strategy interaction process in a continuous-time environment and also endogenizes service quality deviations and risk exposures as constraints. Based on this, this paper constructs a quality consistency constraint mapping, an extended Hamiltonian function, and a Hamilton–Jacobi–Isaacs (HJI) equation system. Furthermore, this research proposes a collaborative control algorithm (HMC-QCE) that integrates belief updates, game strategy optimization, and online calibration. This algorithm can be used to achieve dynamic equilibrium approximation and real-time service quality correction. In the experimental section, this paper constructs a nonlinear stochastic differential simulation environment. TTwelve different kinds of service tasks, 240 rounds of parallel experiments, and 500 time steps make up the experimental environment. Five control mechanisms—Static-Coop, Reinforcement Learning-Coop, Bayes-Only (belief-only), Calib-Only (online calibration only), and the suggested HMC-QCE method—are used to compare and assess the algorithm concurrently. In six metrics—Quality Consistency Index (QCI), System Stability Index (SSI), Risk Exposure (RE), Coordination Efficiency (CE), Equilibrium Approximation Error (EAE), and Response Robustness (RR)—experimental results demonstrate that HMC-QCE performs much better than the control approaches. Specifically, the quality consistency error is reduced by an average of 30%–55%, system stability is improved by 27%–48%, and the equilibrium approximation error is reduced by more than 40%. Under strong structural perturbation scenarios, HMC-QCE can restore system stability within 18–24 time steps. Ablation experiments further demonstrate that the game-theoretic coupling module contributes the most to equilibrium approximation and risk control.
