title: Logarithmic Diffusion Model with Structure-Aware Guidance for SAR Image Synthesis
authors: Xinlong Liu, Ting Chen, Xiangli Yang, Hai Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Generative models offer a promising solution for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) image generation to address the few-shot learning problem in automatic target recognition (ATR). Recently, diffusion models have emerged as a powerful class of generative models, which generate images by iteratively reversing a predefined forward diffusion process. However, applying diffusion models to SAR image generation remains challenging, due to the in herent speckle noise and non-Gaussian distribution of SAR images. To address these challenges, we propose SarLogDiff: a diffusion based framework that explicitly adapts to SAR images through logarithmic diffusion and structure-aware guidance. SarLogDiff defines its forward and reverse processes using the logarithmic diffusion, and generates samples conditioned on the structure-aware guidance. The logarithmic diffusion enables SAR image distribution to approach a Gaussian distribution, while the structure-aware guidance preserves the high-level semantics from conditional input (e.g., a reference image) during generation. Extensive experiments demon strate that SarLogDiff can generate high-quality SAR images, and the synthetic samples significantly enhance the ATR performance. Code is available at https://github.com/Kong-CT/SarLogDiff.

title: OTFS based Multi-Numerology Multiple Access with Heterogeneous User Profiles
authors: Hye Yeong Lee, Man Hee Lee, Soo Young Shin
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This paper introduces a framework leveraging orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) based multiple access configurations with multi-numerology (OTFS-MNMA) to efficiently serve users with heterogeneous user profiles. The framework employs numerology superposition coding (NSC) and successive interference cancellation (SIC) to consider both inter-user and inter-numerology interference among users with different numerology and mobility. The key user pairing strategy involves allocating wider subcarrier spacing (SCS) to high mobility users and narrower SCS to low mobility users. An optimization problem is formulated to maximize the sum rate, subject to power allocation constraints and with interferences effect incorporated via the SINR definition. Simulations are conducted to evaluate sum rate and bit error rate across various numerological ratios between user pairing.

title: 3DGS-WM: Robust Watermarking for Scenes and Objects in 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Feng Wang, Wenjing Feng, Yu Liang, Jiayan Wang, Jing Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: With the increasing deployment of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) across research and commercial applications, ensuring copyright protection for 3DGS models has become critical. Existing watermarking techniques primarily focus on full-scene protection, but practical scenarios often require verifying ownership of specific objects within a scene. In this paper, we present 3DGS-WM, the first robust watermarking framework that simultaneously protects both reconstructed scenes and individual target objects in 3DGS. Our method segments protected object regions across multiple viewpoints and jointly trains the watermarking network on both full-scene images and segmented object areas, enabling reliable watermark extraction even under adversarial attacks. To address the challenge of maintaining rendering quality while embedding imperceptible watermarks, we present Frequency Annealing Regularization(FAR) based on Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT), which enhances both global structure and fine details of the scene, effectively reducing perceptual distortions caused by watermark embedding. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both rendering fidelity and watermark robustness, providing a practical and scalable solution for fine-grained copyright protection in 3DGS. Our code is released at https://github.com/wangfeng70117/3DGS-WM.

title: A Physically Based Modification of the Canopy Gap Probability Formula for LiDAR Waveforms over Sloped Canopies
authors: Weihua Li, Wenge Ni-Meister, Yi Li, Xihan Mu, Donghui Xie, Guangjian Yan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Satellite-borne large footprint Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) missions provide key vertical structure metrics, including canopy gap probability, but their behavior over sloped terrain has been underexamined. Building on the foundational formulation of Ni-Meister et al. (2001), this study employs a physico-mathematical derivation to demonstrate that the staircase approximation used in traditional expressions markedly underestimates canopy gap probability relative to a continuous slope (up to 13.3% at a 40° slope for a canopy with LAI = 1). By introducing a slope-dependent cosine correction to form an effective vegetation ground scattering ratio ρv/(ρg cos α), this study establishes a slope-corrected gap probability model applicable to non-horizontal canopies while preserving the analytical strengths of the Ni-Meister framework.

title: DSRAS:Dual-Stage Reasoning and Answer Selection for Video-Text Visual Question Answering
authors: Chengyang Fang, Xiankun Wan, Wenhui Jiang, Yuming Fang, Zhen Tang
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Video text-based visual question answering (Video TextVQA) aims to answer questions by spatio-temporal joint reasoning over textual and visual information in a video. Existing methods have achieved remarkable progress using uniform sampling strategies. However, uniform frame sampling may introduce noisy frames and miss keyframes. Meanwhile, current methods treat all video frames equally, which is suboptimal, especially since video text question answering tasks primarily target questions that include text within the video. To address the aforementioned issues, we introduce a novel Dual-Stage Reasoning and Answer Selection (DSRAS) model, which not only adaptively focuses on and extracts keyframes, but also significantly enhances attention to video frames containing text through an answer selection mechanism. Specifically, we propose a Dual-Stage Reasoning (DSR) module to achieve adaptive frame selection. Then, we introduce an Answer Selection Module (ASM) to guide our model to focus on keyframes containing textual information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our model outperforms existing approaches on the RoadTextVQA and M4-ViteVQA datasets.

title: Finite-Time Adaptive Fuzzy Asymptotic Tracking Control for Quadrotor Unmanned Aerial Vehicle: Theory and Experiments
authors: Yaoqi Jia, Lin Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a novel finite-time adaptive fuzzy asymptotic tracking control framework specifically designed to meet the high-precision trajectory tracking requirements of quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicles (QUAVs) under external disturbances and system uncertainties. First, an asymptotic tracking control strategy is developed to ensure that all tracking errors converge to zero, eliminating steady-state deviations and enhancing operational accuracy. Building on this foundation, a finite-time command-filtered control framework is introduced, which retains the structural advantages of the backstepping approach while guaranteeing rapid convergence within a finite time. Additionally, an error compensation mechanism is incorporated to mitigate filtering errors induced by command filters, further improving control precision. Furthermore, adaptive laws are integrated with fuzzy logic systems to effectively estimate and compensate for the effects from external disturbances and system uncertainties. The effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method are validated through numerical simulations and experiments on a QUAV prototype.

title: Towards Robust Proactive Deepfake Detection Via Orthogonal Moment Watermarking
authors: Chunpeng Wang, Xianqiu Xu, Shanshan Zhang, Bin Ma, Qi Li, Yunan Liu, Zhiqiu Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the growing diversity of face forgery techniques, Deepfake detection models often suffer performance degradation when training and testing manipulations differ. In addition, image distortions such as blurring and noise further obscure forgery cues, making reliable detection challenging. To address these issues, we propose a proactive Deepfake detection framework based on orthogonal moment watermarking. Firstly, watermark information is embedded into the Quaternion Radial Harmonic Fourier Moments (QRHFMs) domain of host face images, ensuring a balance between imperceptibility and robustness. Secondly, a Local-Enhanced State Space (LSS) block is introduced as the core feature extraction module, capturing discriminative representations by integrating local contextual information. Finally, built upon stacked LSS blocks, a dual-branch architecture combines a watermark extractor and a forgery discriminator, where knowledge distillation enables the extractor to guide the discriminator. Essentially, the embedded watermark remains intact under image distortions but is disrupted by Deepfake manipulations. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, with an average accuracy of 92.58% and an AUC of 95.96%, outperforming existing approaches. More importantly, our method exhibits strong robustness to image quality degradation and achieves high-precision detection across diverse forgery techniques, including Deepfake manipulations unseen during training.

title: LLM-Augmented Stiefel Graph Neural Networks for Zero-Shot Spatio-temporal Forecasting_supp1-3689935.pdf
authors: Liang Xie
publish: N/A
abstract: Spatio-temporal time series (STTS) play a crucial role in domains such as traffic forecasting, energy scheduling, and financial analysis. However, accurate and efficient prediction remains challenging due to the complex dynamic dependencies across temporal and spatial dimensions. Existing Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) often struggle to balance effectiveness and efficiency when modeling dynamic spatio-temporal relations. Meanwhile, Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit strong capabilities in modeling long-range dependencies and generalization under few-shot and zero-shot conditions, yet their ability to capture spatio-temporal structures remains limited. To address this, we propose LAD-SGNN, a unified framework that integrates the advantages of graph-based and language-based modeling. Specifically, LAD-SGNN employs Spectral Graph Convolution on the Stiefel manifold (SGSC) together with Linear Dynamic Graph Optimization (LDGOSM) to efficiently extract dynamic spatio-temporal features with nearly linear complexity. Structured prompts and a spatio-temporal alignment mechanism are then designed to fuse the extracted dynamic spectral information with task semantics, which are fed into a lightweight LLM to achieve unified modeling of spatio-temporal structures and semantic reasoning. In this framework, SGSC efficiently represents dynamic spatial dependencies in the spectral domain, while the prompt-based alignment strategy explicitly injects spatio-temporal information into the LLM, thereby enhancing generalization across datasets. Extensive experiments on multiple real-world spatio-temporal datasets demonstrate that LAD-SGNN significantly improves prediction accuracy in both zero-shot and few-shot scenarios.

title: AdaTracker: Learning Adaptive In-Context Policy for Cross-Embodiment Active Visual Tracking_supp1-3692075.mp4
authors: Fangwei Zhong
publish: N/A
abstract: Realizing active visual tracking with a single unified model across diverse robots is challenging, as the physical constraints and motion dynamics vary drastically from one platform to another. Existing approaches typically train separate models for each embodiment, leading to poor scalability and limited generalization. To address this, we propose AdaTracker, an adaptive in-context policy learning framework that robustly tracks targets on diverse robot morphologies. Our key insight is to explicitly model embodiment-specific constraints through an Embodiment Context Encoder, which infers embodiment-specific constraints from history. This contextual representation dynamically modulates a Context-Aware Policy, enabling it to infer optimal control actions for unseen embodiments in a zero-shot manner. To enhance robustness, we introduce two auxiliary objectives to ensure accurate context identification and temporal consistency. Experiments in both simulation and the real world demonstrate that AdaTracker significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in cross-embodiment generalization, sample efficiency, and zero-shot adaptation.

title: A Resilient and Adaptive Control Approach for Enhancing Performance of Grid-Integrated SPV-WES and BES-Based Microgrid
authors: Varsha Yadav, Divyank Srivastava, Bhim Singh, Arunima Verma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Electronic loads significantly affect power quality in electrical distribution systems, creating challenges that must be addressed to ensure reliable operation. Integration of hybrid renewable energy systems further exacerbates these issues, particularly at grid interconnection points. This article proposes a robust control methodology to mitigate such disturbances in systems combining integration of solar photovoltaic, wind power, and batteries for storage solutions. Strategy employs a generalized fourth-order filter with a harmonic decoupling network to isolate positive-sequence fundamental and harmonic components from load currents and grid voltages, while also estimating frequency and phase angle for seamless grid synchronization. These extracted signals regulate voltage source converter currents to ensure compliance with IEEE 1547 standard. Comparative analysis shows that proposed approach outperforms conventional adaptive control in load balancing and harmonic suppression, while enabling smooth transitions between self-sustain mode and utility-tied mode. Effectiveness and reliability of method are validated through both simulation and hardware prototype experiments, confirming its ability to handle complex grid conditions with technical robustness and precision.

title: Efficient Total Causal Effect Computation in Fuzzy Cognitive Maps for Scalable Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Marios Tyrovolas, Nikolaos D. Kallimanis, Chrysostomos Stylios
publish: IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Letters
abstract: Building trust in AI systems requires transparent and interpretable decision-making, underscoring the importance of eXplainable AI (XAI). Fuzzy Cognitive Maps (FCMs) are a promising XAI method because they combine an intuitive causal structure with multilevel interpretability and hybrid intelligence capabilities. However, the traditional approach to causal effect analysis for globally interpreting FCMs is computationally inten sive and impractical, even for moderately sized networks. To ad dress this limitation, this letter introduces the Total Causal Effect Calculation for FCMs (TCEC-FCM) algorithm, which enables the efficient computation of total causal effects in large-scale FCMs for the first time. By combining binary search and graph traversal techniques, TCEC-FCM avoids the exhaustive exploration of causal paths and substantially reduces computational overhead. Experiments on synthetic FCMs with different sizes and densities demonstrate that the algorithm effectively overcomes key scala bility challenges, thereby extending the applicability of FCMs to large-scale XAI scenarios.

title: Key Vehicle Parameters Estimation via Adaptive UKF Informed by Intelligent Tires Equipped with PVDF Sensor Arrays
authors: Zhenqiang Quan, Xiaoqiang Sun, Tianli Gu, Yingfeng Cai, Long Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Key vehicle parameters (KVPs), such as vehicle mass and center of gravity (CoG) position, play a very important role in vehicle control and state estimation. This paper proposes a novel KVPs estimation method based on Adaptive Unscented Kalman Filter (AUKF) and intelligent tire technology. Global sensitivity analysis is used to determine the optimal in-tire installation locations of PVDF sensors for tire vertical load (VL) estimation. Raw voltage signals are collected and preprocessed to construct training datasets. An optimized Gaussian Process Regression algorithm is then employed to establish an accurate tire VL estimation model. On this basis, two key modules are further developed: 1) a VL update rate compensation algorithm for intelligent tires, which mitigates the adverse effects of vehicle speed variations and asynchronous VL estimation among the four wheels on KVPs estimation performance; and 2) an AUKF framework incorporating compensated tire VL information, enabling accurate estimation of KVPs. Both simulation and real-vehicle experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of the estimator. The results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves maximum mean absolute percentage errors of 1.08%, 1.61%, 0.11%, and 2.07% for the estimation of vehicle mass and the longitudinal, lateral, and vertical CoG positions, respectively. In addition, the estimator exhibits strong adaptability to model parameter uncertainties, such as tire-road friction coefficient.

title: Full-Wave Characterization of Reverberation Chambers for Rodent Bioassays: Methodology and Realistic-Loading Impact Analysis
authors: Antonio Faraone, Giorgi Bit-Babik, Kevin Sanderson, Paola Russo, Alfredo De Leo, Valter Mariani Primiani, Valerio De Santis
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive full-wave numerical dosimetry approach for large-scale rodent bioassays in Reverberation Chambers (RCs), improving upon prior methods that rely on idealized Plane Wave (PW) superposition. A “digital twin” of the Università Politecnica delle Marche RC was implemented using Transmission-Line Matrix and Finite Element Method solvers to characterize exposure homogeneity across rodent cages at 900 MHz. Unlike PW-superposition models, loaded-RC simulation accounts for realistic experimental constraints, such as intruding water-supply metal piping, specific antenna designs and placements, and actual mode stirrers. The loaded-RC electromagnetic characteristics were investigated by analyzing the field impedance ratios, yielding discrimination criteria for the probe location and type. Cage-wise instantaneous and ensemble-averaged whole-body specific absorption rate (wbSAR) distributions were evaluated using postured homogeneous rat models. Furthermore, investigations into exposure imbalance mitigation strategies demonstrated significant benefits when spinning cage assemblies. An analysis of mass-dependent exposures revealed a notably weaker correlation between body mass and wbSAR, as well as a much larger wbSAR cage dependence, compared to earlier PW-based predictions, further highlighting the necessity of realistic RC modeling for reliable rodent bioassay exposure design.

title: A Parallelization Strategy for GRAND with Optimality Guarantee by Exploiting Error Pattern Tree Representation
authors: Li Wan, Huarui Yin, Wenyi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Parallelism has become a central concern in modern decoding frameworks aiming to meet stringent throughput and latency requirements. Guessing Random Additive Noise Decoding (GRAND) is a recently proposed decoding paradigm that tests candidate Error Patterns (EPs) until a valid codeword is found. Among its variants, Soft GRAND (SGRAND) achieves maximum-likelihood (ML) decoding but relies on real-time generation and likelihood ordering of EPs, making parallel execution nontrivial under the ML optimality constraint. In this work, we introduce a unified binary tree representation of EPs, termed the EP tree, which formalizes the hierarchical structure underlying SGRAND and Ordered Reliability Bits (ORB) GRAND algorithms, enabling structured organization of EPs and algorithmic-level parallel exploration. Building upon this unified framework, we propose a parallel design of SGRAND that preserves ML optimality while significantly reducing decoding complexity through pruning strategies and tree-based computation. Furthermore, we develop an enhanced ORBGRAND algorithm based on the same EP tree representation, improving decoding performance toward ML while retaining parallel efficiency. Numerical experiments show that the proposed parallel SGRAND achieves a 3.96× reduction in decoding latency compared with its serial counterpart, while the enhanced ORBGRAND achieves a 4.21× speedup, demonstrating the effectiveness of the unified tree-based framework and its strong potential for future algorithmic and hardware optimizations.

title: Parametric Modeling and GPR Imaging of Planetary Lava Tubes Constrained by Terrestrial Analogs
authors: Chunyang Zhao, Ling Zhang, Zhijun Huo, Shuo Han, Rui Gao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Lava tubes are priority subsurface targets on basaltic planetary bodies, and Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) is a primary detection method. However, interpretation is often limited by forward models relying on idealized cavities, which underestimate scattering effects caused by geometric asymmetry and boundary roughness. We introduce a parametric modeling and imaging workflow constrained by terrestrial analogs. Using measured cross-sections from the Pyroduct database, we embedded realistic cavity geometries into a lunar stratigraphic model defined by correlated random permittivity. Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) simulations were performed with a 60 MHz source, followed by Kirchhoff pre-stack time migration. Unlike ideal cavities, analog-constrained models produce asymmetric diffractions and local phase discontinuities. Quantitative analysis shows that realistic morphological complexity severely degrades the signal-to-clutter ratio (SCR) of ceiling reflections, which drops from 80.67 dB in the idealized model to 25.19 dB in the analog-constrained model. Migration analysis suggests that while ceiling location and lateral extent are reliably constrained, the tube floor remains difficult to resolve. Field GPR data from a terrestrial lava tube show similar ceiling-dominant responses, validating this geometric prior model for evaluating performance in future planetary missions.

title: Dynamic Policy Learning for Legged Robot with Simplified Model Pretraining and Model-Homotopy-Inspired Transfer_supp1-3692082.mp4
authors: Hae-Won Park
publish: N/A
abstract: Generating dynamic motions for legged robots remains a challenging problem. While reinforcement learning has achieved notable success in various legged locomotion tasks, producing highly dynamic behaviors often requires extensive reward tuning or high-quality demonstrations. Leveraging reduced-order models can help mitigate these challenges. However, the model discrepancy poses a significant challenge when transferring policies to full-body dynamics environments. In this work, we introduce a continuation-based learning framework that combines simplified model pretraining and model-homotopy-inspired transfer to efficiently generate and refine complex dynamic behaviors. First, we pretrain the policy using a single rigid body model to capture core motion patterns in a simplified environment. Next, we employ a continuation strategy to progressively transfer the policy to the full-body environment, minimizing performance loss. To define the continuation path, we introduce a parametric transition path from the single rigid body model to the full-body model by gradually redistributing mass and inertia between the trunk and legs. The proposed method achieves faster convergence and demonstrates superior stability during the transfer process compared to baseline methods. Our framework is validated on a range of dynamic tasks, including flips and wall-assisted maneuvers, and is successfully deployed on a real quadrupedal robot.

title: EERA: A Hybrid Ensemble Regression Model for Accurate Time Series Prediction in IoT Applications
authors: Ajit Patil, Amol Potgantwar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: IoT systems generate continuous sensor streams that often contain irregular fluctuations, noise, and nonlinear behaviours, making accurate forecasting a persistent challenge. Traditional regression models capture specific characteristics of such data but frequently show unstable performance when evaluated across different IoT environments. To address this limitation, this paper introduces EERA+, a hybrid ensemble regression framework that combines REPTree, SMOreg, and a Multi-Layer Perceptron through a two-stage fusion process involving error-based weighting and a stacking meta-regressor. This design enables the ensemble to balance short-term variability with long-range trends, resulting in predictions that are both accurate and consistent.EERA+ is tested on three real IoT datasets—the UCI Appliances Energy dataset, the Intel Berkeley Laboratory sensor dataset, and the Household Power Consumption dataset—representing smart-home energy usage, environmental sensing, and large-scale power monitoring. Across the datasets, the proposed ensemble records clear improvements over individual models and standard regressors. On the Appliances dataset, EERA+ achieves an MSE of 0.118, RMSE of 0.343, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.986, improving accuracy by 8–32% compared to the base models. On the Intel sensor data, the ensemble reduces MAE by 29% and MAPE by 34%, demonstrating stability under noisy readings and sensor drift. For the power-consumption dataset, EERA+ reaches an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.972, outperforming all baseline regressors while maintaining consistent error reductions across different time windows.These results confirm that combining diverse learning behaviours through a structured ensemble can improve forecasting reliability in practical IoT settings. The study also shows that a single unified model can generalize across distinct IoT domains, highlighting its suitability for real-time monitoring and decision support.

title: Comparative Analysis of Linear Regression and XGBoost for Air Passenger Traffic Prediction
authors: Gangapuram Dhanasree, B. Nandhini, M. Sohan Reddy, T. Prashanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Passenger traffic at airports has shown a steady upward trend over the past few decades, which makes reliable demand forecasting an important requirement for both airlines and airport operators. In this work, we study the problem of predicting monthly air passenger volumes using historical traffic records and compare two supervised learning approaches. The dataset used in this study contains several years of domestic and international passenger counts, flight movements, and load factors. After converting the raw data into a machine-learning-ready format and performing exploratory analysis, we trained a simple Multiple Linear Regression model based on time features, and an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) regressor as a more expressive alternative. Model performance was assessed using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and the coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). Our experiments show that although Linear Regression can reproduce the general growth trend, the XGBoost model achieves substantially lower error and a much higher R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, indicating a better fit to the underlying patterns. Visual comparisons of actual and predicted values further highlight the ability of XGBoost to capture non-linear behavior in air passenger demand. The study presents a complete, reproducible workflow—from preprocessing and exploration to model comparison and forecasting—that can support data-driven planning in the aviation sector.

title: Intelligent Policy Analyzer for Privacy and Legal Documents Using Machine Learning and Intelligent System
authors: R Akiladevi, Anand S, Barathraj V L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Privacy policies, terms of service, cookie policies, software licenses, and employment contracts are critical documents that regulate online services and workplace agreements. However, their extensive length, dense legal language, and intricate structures often render them inaccessible to ordinary users, resulting in low readership and a lack of true consent or awareness. This creates tangible risks, including data misuse, lost rights, privacy violations, and unexpected liabilities, highlighting the pressing need for an accessible solution. The principal objective of this project is to develop an intelligent analyzer that leverages machine learning and advanced natural language processing techniques to automatically interpret, segment, and summarize complex privacy and legal documents. Users can interact with the system through a conversational interface, enabling them to obtain plain-language explanations, receive answers to specific queries, and access key insights about their rights, obligations, and data protection practices. The scope of this work encompasses a broad spectrum of legal and policy documents, not limited to privacy policies but also covering terms of service, software licenses, and employment contracts. While the tool provides comprehensible summaries and interactive explanations, it serves as an assistive platform rather than a replacement for professional legal counsel. The methodology integrates state-of-the-art machine learning models such as BERT, GPT, and domain-adapted Legal-BERT, utilizing labeled datasets like OPP-115, APP-350, and PolicyQA for robust performance. The system workflow includes document ingestion, clause segmentation, context-aware summarization, and chatbot-driven Q&A. Emphasis is placed on usability, accuracy, and data privacy throughout the design. The anticipated outcome is a user centric, time-saving application that democratizes access to legal understanding and policy transparency. This tool has direct applications for individuals, employees, corporate users, compliance managers, and researchers in need of rapid, reliable insights into legal documentation.

title: Machine Learning-Based Early Risk Stratification Framework for Chronic Kidney Disease Progression Using Comprehensive Multi-Domain Clinical and Biochemical Data
authors: Md. Maniruzzaman, Naima Najam Nejum, Musfika Jannat Mamata, Hasebul Hassan Chowdhury, Fahim Ahamed Romit, Sudoy Kumer Ghosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a major health problem in the world today with the need to ensure that risks are stratified at an early age to avoid an irreversible loss of renal functions. The paper provides a machine learning architecture that combines extensive demographic, lifestyle, comorbidity, laboratory biomarker, medication, and environmental exposure information to predict CKD progression. Additional processing included median imputation, categorical encoding, outlier suppression and filtering use of variance. Dimensionality reduction used ANOVA F-test and principal component analysis in order to determine discriminative features such as serum creatinine, serum glomerular filtration rate, albumin-creatinine ratio, blood pressure indices, and electrolyte markers. Four algorithms, including the ridge regression, support vector machines, random forest, and gradient boosting were assessed by means of repeated stratified cross-validation. The gradient boosting had a better performance with an area under curve 0.867, accuracy 93.1, F1-score 96.3 and Brier score 0.048, which shows the best discrimination and calibration. The framework allows specific risk measurement that facilitates timely nephrology referral, individualized therapeutic intervention and evidence-based disease management planning in clinical practice.

title: Data-Centric Privacy Preservation Using Swarm-Optimized Machine Learning Models in Edge Networks
authors: M.Anitha, K.Sivaraman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The increasing volume of data at the edge requires privacy-preserving intelligence without centralized data transmission. The existing systems based on centralized federated learning (CFL) or standard federated learning are subject to communication overhead, slow convergence and risk of private data being leaked. Swarm-Optimized Data-Centric Privacy Preservation, in which DFL is adopted on a decentralized learning manner by particle swarm optimization to enable intelligent client selection and adaptive secure aggregation. These also enables data privacy as nothing gets uploaded online, by bringing the model closer to the local copies, reduces time it takes existing models to converge and finally saves on data transmission costs. Experimental study shows 4.7% increase in global accuracy, 18% faster convergence and 22% less communication cost than compared with previous algorithms. The proposed model also has a 1.1% risk leakage and hamming privacy score of 0.97, showing an effective technique between performance, scale and privacy. These results demonstrate the power of swarm intelligence toward efficient secure collaborative edge learning.

title: Edge-AI Co designed Powered Converter for fault predictive Grid interactive Inverters
authors: M Prabakaran, B Gauthaman, S. Sasikumar, Sripriya Ranganathan, S Kalpana, M Thirumalai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Grid-tied inverters are an essential component of renewable energy systems, but; they are still susceptible to open-circuits, short-circuits and arc-faults, which compromise the reliability of the system. Modern AI based diagnostic approaches such as Convolutional Neural Networks, Random Forest Classifiers, and Sliding Mode Observer achieve an accuracy of 96-98%, but these techniques are computationally expensive and cannot be put to use in real-time. The present work is intended to build on recent AI-based detection techniques including hybrid CNN-RBFNN models, residual observer, and graph attention networks aiming at identifying gaps in methodologies. To overcome these challenges the Edge-Optimized Hybrid Attention Network (EO-HAN) is proposed, a model that can simultaneously represent time-domain residual signatures and frequency-domain S-transform coefficients for use in embedded inference. Experimental results demonstrate that EO-HAN achieves 97.8% classification accuracy, reduces false alarms by 20%, predicts faults 17% earlier, and executes inference within 15–20 ms on Jetson Nano hardware, confirming its suitability for real-time grid-interactive operation.

title: AI-Based Asynchronous Alert Enrichment Framework for Scalable Security Operations Centers
authors: Devika Karki, Virender Ranga, Varsha Sisaudia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Security Operations Centers (SOCs) face increasing operational pressure from growing alert volumes, diverse telemetry, and manual triage demands, while traditional SIEM and SOAR workflows—limited by static rules and sequential processing—struggle to scale. This paper presents an agentic, asynchronous alert-enrichment framework that reimagines enrichment as a parallel evidence-gathering and reasoning task performed by an autonomous agent integrating policy checks, multi-source asynchronous intelligence collection, LLM-based semantic analysis, and confidence-driven decision control with optional human escalation. A prototype built with modular connectors and an asynchronous execution model is evaluated under rising alert concurrency, demonstrating low overhead, stable latency, and reliable operation even under stress. Security analysis further highlights protections against adversarial inputs and unsafe automation. The results show that agentic AI can function as a practical, governance-aware accelerator for real-time SOC alert enrichment.

title: Multi-Layer Hybrid Energy Storage System with Time-Scale Decoupling for Fast-Response EV Charging Stations
authors: Guna G, Pandikumar M, Srihari T, Mohammad Irshad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: High-power electric vehicle (EV) charging stations require energy storage solutions capable of delivering rapid transient response while maintaining long-term system sustainability—a challenge unmet by conventional monolithic control schemes. This work presents a multi-layer hybrid energy storage system (HESS) distinguished by explicit time-scale decomposition and heterogeneous hardware integration. The proposed hierarchical approach leverages supercapacitors (SCs) to manage fast power fluctuations on the order of seconds, orchestrated via model predictive control, and utilizes lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) for slower, large-scale energy delivery through rolling horizon optimization. By decoupling fast and slow dynamic regulation, the system achieves sub-second responsiveness to sudden EV charging events with reduced computational burden. Experimental validation confirms that this architectural synergy yields pronounced improvements in both short-term tracking accuracy and battery longevity compared to existing heuristic or single-layer systems. The unique integration of time-scale separation with technology-specific hardware optimizations sets this solution apart from prior art and renders it highly adaptable to the evolving demands of future ultra-fast EV charging infrastructures. This design paradigm provides a scalable pathway for uniting transient-rich power delivery with sustainable, grid-cognizant energy storage, effectively bridging the divide between fluctuating fast-charging loads and reliable energy provision.

title: Cryptbox 2.0: A Secure Client-Side Encrypted File Storage and Sharing Framework
authors: Krishna Varma, Bharath Teja, Sreeraag G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Cryptbox 2.0 is a secure virtual filesystem that uses client-side encryption to secure data while being stored, synchronized and shared, in untrusted cloud environments. As an improvement upon the original MIT cryptobox project, Cryptbox applies modern cryptographic standards - including AES-256-GCM for authenticated encryption, PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 for strong key derivation, and RSA-4096 for signing digital documents and securely sharing encrypted passwords - to construct a zero-knowledge security model where encryption/decryption takes place on the user’s computer. A FUSE-based virtual mount allows users to easily access their decrypted files in the filesystem while ensuring only encrypted data and integrity metadata leaves their computer. Cryptbox 2.0 also introduces a powerful method for sharing encrypted file passwords, using RSA encryption to create secure bundles of passwords for sharing, along with a redesigned web dashboard for a more intuitive interface for Uploading files, encrypting files, decrypting files, verifying signatures, and managing keys. Instead of his traditional ’involvement in everything the user does’ and holds the user’s information on their own servers and is able to process data privately and collaboratively, Cryptbox combines transparent encryption with strong access control, digital signing, protection of metadata, and cloud agnostic storage into a full-featured privacy-preserving solution for a user to securely handle, share, and backup files and data to the cloud.

title: Computational Modelling and Analysis of Human Electromagnetic Radiation (EMR) Frequency Using Comsol Multiphysics
authors: Hanis Marni Akmal Hamdzah, Siti Zura A. Jalil, Amnani Shamjuddin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Studies shown human emit radiation frequency at certain ranges which reflecting human natural body intercellular processes. This study aims to present a numerical simulation study to explore the human Electromagnetic Radiation (EMR) in the human tissues at specific range of frequency based on experimental datasets. The computational modelling software Comsol Multiphysics 6.2 was used to analyse the human body EMR visualization between computational and experimental study. A model of human torso was constructed within three blocks layer of skin, fat and muscle tissues with specific human dielectric properties was employed in the material properties. The equation was governed with assigned to Maxwells' equation to solve the electromagnetic radiation frequency and desired results. The simulation was generated within frequency range of 10-60MHz spectrum to observe the frequency radiation pattern. The results of magnitude of electric field, magnitude of magnetic field and generated power flow show that the radiation patterns were radiating in line with the frequency input. The mean error analysis between computational and experimental study for four points varies from 5% to 8% indicating good agreement. Analysis of prediction application of human modelling study and experimental study also demonstrate the curve closely followed the experimental and simulated values, proving the feasibility of predictive modelling. This study demonstrate that each point on the human body radiates electromagnetic fields of differing magnitudes and intensities, reflecting spatial variations in the distribution of electromagnetic energy associated with the body's functional system.

title: Kidney Tumor Detection in CT Scans: Combining 3D CNN and Transformer Networks
authors: B. Venkatesu Goud, P. Phanindra Kumar Reddy, P. Sai Varshitha, T. Sruthi, N. Lakshmi Narayana Reddy, K. Harsha Vardhan Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: A novel hybrid deep learning algorithm is proposed to detect kidney tumors on CT scans, which is a combination of 3D convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformer networks capability. In the framework, volumetric processing consists of sequence modeling and thus, the framework is useful in the representation of local texture as well as the global contextual relationship among the multi-slice CT volumes. The model employs the ConvNeXt-inspired 3D backbone to preserve the spatial relations among slices, and then, the transformer encoder is introduced that predicts the long-range dependencies among slices with attention mechanisms, and enhances the accuracy of the slices detection. It has such innovations as a depth-sensitive patch embedding layer that maintains the inter-slice interconnections of 3D convolutions and 2D attention maps, adaptive positional encodings to handle variable slice spacing in CT scans, and a multi-scale feature fusion module, which combines complementary information between the CNN and transformer pathways. The advances improve high sensitivity and specificity of the model on detection of malignant lesions, cysts and kidney stones. Performance of the model training on large-scale data of kidney CT volumes with normal and pathological cases is very high, and inferencing time is just okay in clinical processes. The hybrid methodology is favorable (in comparison with traditional 3D CNNs and pure transformer models) in terms of its capacity to handle slice-to-slice variations without the need to lose fine spatial details. The study shows that hybrid architectures will be able to expedite volumetric medical imaging to result into a major solution that would advance diagnostic AI within the radiology discipline.

title: Proceedings of the 2026 IEEE/ACM 3rd International Flaky Tests Workshop
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: None

title: Neuroimage-Guided Adaptive Deep Learning Model for Accurate and Early Stroke Detection
authors: Resmi A. M, Asfar. S, N. Ramajayam, Umanath R K, S. P. Santhoshkumar, A. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Proper and timely identification of brain stroke is a significant issue in the field of neuroimaging because of its heterogeneous lesion appearances, inadequate annotations, and urgency to make clinical decisions. The most recent developments in deep learning have brought a lot more improvements in stroke diagnosis to automatically and discriminatively learn features out of complex neuroimaging data. This paper is an integrative review that correlates with the theme of Neuroimage- Guided Adaptive Deep Learning Model Accurate and Early Stroke Detection and consolidates the state-of-the-art processes in stroke detection, lesion segmentation, and prediction of outcomes. A total of more than 100 peer-reviewed studies using CNNs, FCNs, hybrid CNNLSTM networks, and transformer-based networks are used to draw insights on CT and multimodal MRI data. Special attention to the adaptive frameworks with the inclusion of spatial-temporal modelling, lesion-aware attention, and weakly supervised learning to counter annotation scarcity is given. Another insight of the review is that multimodal data fusion can be used to predict post-stroke cognitive impairment, and its performance in the task is found to be consistently better than that of the conventional machine learning methods. Critical technical and clinical issues such as generalization, explainability and deployment constraints are called upon. In general, this research proposes a comprehensive deep learning-based platform of intelligent stroke diagnosis and early interventions systems of the next generation.

title: Design and Simulate a CLLC DC-DC Converter for Electric Vehicle Type-C Charging System
authors: Sameer Nayak, A B Raju, Vinod S Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Compact, efficient, and bidirectional power converters will be needed to support the rapid electrification of transportation with next-generation Type-C EV fast-charging systems. High losses and limited bidirectional performance are critical disadvantages of traditional hard-switched DC-DC converters, limiting their suitability for modern EVs. This paper presents an optimum design, analysis, and simulation of a bidirectional symmetric CLLC resonant DC-DC converter, intended for applications in Type-C charging. The proposed resonant tank enables soft-switching, achieving ZVS on the primary side and ZCS on the secondary side to reduce losses and thermal stress. It can handle efficient power transfer at 400/800 V port voltages in both grid-to-vehicle and vehicle-to-grid modes with a closed-loop voltage control strategy that makes the output stable even with different load states. From PLECS simulations, it follows that the efficiency is high, the gain is broad, and the voltage regulation is accurate. A comparison is drawn from analysis that shows the symmetric CLLC converter guarantees superior efficiency, reliability, and compactness, which should make it a good candidate for a future bidirectional EV fast-charging system.

title: Face Off: Adversarial Perturbation-based Defence Against AI Face Recognition
authors: Jenisha J, Jaya Hamsika Patchipulusu, T. Prem Jacob
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Face Off is a lightweight application that is used to ensure the privacy of users in the time of deep learning-based face recognition. As the artificial intelligence increases, people are under surveillance by virtue of biometrics and biometric surveillance and are facing the risk of their facial information being taken and abused in the digital places. The system involves adversarial, minor and unobservable changes to facial images, so that the validity of recognition models is reduced, Though it is potentially invisible to the human eye. Unlike traditional methods, Face Off works minimally with obfuscation methods and computational overhead, making it suitable to real time use. and without interfering with the performance of the system. It is effective against a huge array of general face recognition today algorithms that are being used in open-source as well as commercial systems. This simple and intuitive interface enables the users to have their pictures secured before they can share them online giving them some control over their identity on the internet. Testing indicates that Face Off is successful in evading high rates and allow high image quality. This makes it a practical and available option to defend personal privacy in the time of high-end artificial intelligence surveillance.

title: Leveraging Machine Learning for Digital Transformation Strategies in Healthcare to Enhance Patient-Centered Brand Loyalty and Long-Term Patient Engagement
authors: D Sushma Kiran, Srinivas.D
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The article discusses how machine learning (ML) models could be used to improve patient-centered care and predict patient risk, which in turn can benefit healthcare facilities. Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, and Neural Networks were deployed and tested to use as a machine learning model to predict patient outcomes, such as the likelihood of a disease progression and readmission. These performance metrics in the models are accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score where the Neural Network model was the top achiever in terms of accuracy which is 90.5% showing that it has been able to adapt complex patterns in healthcare data. The effects of machine learning on patient satisfaction and brand loyalty were also tested, in addition to predictive performance, in the study. This study discovered the effectiveness of personalized care and real-time predictive insights, as the patient satisfaction rates rose significantly, 65 to 87 percent within 18 months after the implementation of the ML system. The analysis of feature importance showed that Age, Blood Pressure, and Cholesterol could most impact the patient risk, which is essential information that can help carry out the healthcare provision. Although these are encouraging outcomes, the study shares a number of challenges such as quality of data, ability to generalize the model and its applicability in clinical processes. The research outlines the opportunities and issues of digital transformation as well as provides valuable information to the healthcare providers who can enhance their services and patient relations.

title: Severity Grading and Lesion Segmentation in Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: G.V.Suresh, K. Ranesh, M. GowthamReddy, D. Uday Krishna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the most frequent cause of avoidable diabetes blindness and that is why it has to be identified and treated early. Computer-aided diagnosis must be automated because screening of retinal fundus image by hand is tedious and subjective. The paper categorizes and quantifies diabetic retinopathy lesions with the help of deep learning and retinal fundus images. In order to enhance image quality and model robustness, IdriD dataset scales, normalizes and optimizes retinal images. U-Net can identify imbalance in classes, hard exudates and hemorrhages accurately. The classification models based on ResNet-50 classify DR severity as Mild, Moderate, Severe, Proliferative, or No DR. Severity-classification and segmentation were effective with trials being at 98.27% pixel accuracy. Grad-CAM visualization is further improved by removing clinically significant areas, thereby improving the interpretability of the model. The suggested procedure is effective and is explainable in automated diabetic retinopathy screening.

title: FPGA Implementation of QPSK Modulation Technique for Wireless Communication
authors: Smitha N, Soumya Prasad, Divya Jyothi S, Manushree Naik, Spoorthy Nagaraja Hegde
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This work is focused on the design and FPGA implementation of four basic digital modulation techniques, namely ASK, FSK, BPSK, and QPSK, using Verilog HDL. Each modulator utilizes carrier generation based on LUT, phase accumulation, and a modular architecture for hardware efficiency. All the designs have been verified through behavioral simulations in Vivado and then synthesized, implemented, and tested in real time on FPGAs. ASK scales the amplitude, FSK uses programmable phase increments, BPSK performs 180° carrier inversion, and QPSK generates orthogonal I/Q components using phase-shifted LUTs. Simulation results validated the amplitude, frequency, and phase characteristics correctly, along with accurate symbol transitions. Implementation results have reflected low LUT/FF/BRAM/DSP usage, complete routing, timing closure, zero critical DRC issues, and safe power consumption. Overall, this work will showcase an effective, extendable, and hardware-efficient approach toward digital modulation systems over FPGAs.

title: Machine Learning-based Crop Recommendation Systems: A Comprehensive Survey and Future Research Directions
authors: PalaPala SivaGanesh, Tirunagari MohanKrishna, J. Vijitha Ananthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Crop analysis and prediction is an increasing and significant factor that enhances agricultural practice globally. Crop recommendation assists farmers to choose the most suitable crops that suit their soil type and the climate in the area. Previously, farmers used to depend on professional advice or family-inherited knowledge, which required considerable time and individual efforts and, in many instances, made mistakes. As the global population is projected to reach nearly 9.7 billion by 2050, the demand for sustainable food production that preserves soil health has become increasingly critical. Machine learning approaches can be used as a solution to the problem by automating crop recommendations and detecting the first symptoms of pests or diseases. This assists farmers to raise their yields, maintain land soils, and make the land to remain productive over a long period. This review work focuses on seven popular machine learning algorithms with a large historical dataset of soil nutrient (nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium), pH value, temperature, humidity, and rainfall. The future direction gives explicit crop recommendations that suit every location enabling farmers to both increase production, employ sustainable techniques, make greater profits, make better choices and require professional assistance significantly less often. This approach supports sustainable agriculture and helps address future food demands.

title: Hierarchical Agentic RAG: A Cloud-Agnostic Orchestration Protocol for Multi-Hop Reasoning Over Heterogeneous Enterprise Data
authors: Abhijit Ubale, Siva Kantha Rao Vanama, Rama Krishna Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The organizations working across multiple cloud services encounter many difficulties that impact the integration and reasoning of different sources of heterogeneous data. These hurdles include the interoperability, scalability, and compliance of these systems. To improve upon the current limitations, this research proposes a cloud-independent orchestration protocol based on a Hierarchical Agentic RAG Framework. The framework includes a multi-layered agent structure consisting of agents such as decomposing agents, retrieval agents, and synthesis agents, which work together to enable a more efficient method for reasoning through multiple hops of data regardless of the source (i.e., structured, semi-structured, or unstructured) from within the organization. The proposed protocol allows for the decoupling of the reasoning workflow from the dependencies on any specific cloud platform, which allows for flexibility in the ongoing changes that may happen with any of the service providers that an organization may utilize. The experimental evaluation presented demonstrates that the framework has an accuracy of 93% while outperforming traditional RAG models and existing, cloud-dependent orchestration protocols, in terms of precision, recall, and F1 Score. Furthermore, the system has demonstrated strong levels of scalability, interoperability, and adaptability within a very complex enterprise environment. The proposed protocol is especially applicable in operations/organizations where data governance and cross-cloud intelligence will be necessary; such as finance, healthcare, and government. Collectively, this research provides a reliable, scalable, and efficient means for enterprise knowledge orchestration and accelerating reasoning processes throughout the distributed cloud ecosystem.

title: Multi-Adapter LLMs: Dynamic Mixture of LoRAs for Scalable Personalization
authors: Varun Kotte, Rohit Joshi, Supratim Dutta, Ravindra Rajasekhar Kavuru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable generalization and reasoning capabilities, but their performance in domain-specific or user-personalized contexts remains limited by the inefficiency of fine-tuning full model parameters. This paper explores a modular approach using multiadapter Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) techniques, dynamically mixed at inference time to achieve scalable personalization across diverse tasks. The study examines hybrid adaptation strategies for optimizing latency, parameter efficiency, and model interpretability within large-scale environments. A proposed architecture integrates dynamic adapter selection, prompt-based routing, and attention reweighting mechanisms that enable flexible, task-aware adaptation. The work concludes with case studies and evaluations highlighting substantial gains in personalization quality and compute efficiency across multiple benchmarks.

title: Research on Coordination Technology between Large and Small Models in Transmission Line Defect Detection
authors: Qingping Zhang, Shuang Zhang, Zhenhua Yan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: To address the issue in traditional intelligent detection of transmission line defects where small models have limited recognition capabilities for complex defects, while large models consume significant computational resources and have slow inference speeds, this paper proposes a collaborative technology combining “fast screening with small models, deep analysis with large models, and global decision optimization”. By constructing a reasonable collaborative architecture, this technology integrates the high-precision advantage of large models with the high-efficiency characteristics of small models, aiming to achieve rapid and accurate detection of transmission line defects. The article first analyzes the design ideas and execution processes of three core collaborative architectures: series, parallel, and hybrid. Subsequently, it studies data collaboration, training collaboration, and inference collaboration technologies to address issues such as uneven data distribution, model parameter mismatch, and inference decision conflicts during the collaborative process. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed technology is analyzed through typical application cases. The analysis shows that the collaboration of large and small models in transmission line defect detection can effectively resolve the contradiction between real-time performance and accuracy in defect detection, thereby improving detection efficiency.

title: An Immersive Adaptive AI-Driven Platform for Ayurvedic Health Education
authors: R. Dhanapal, Raghul Raj T, Rahul R, Siddarth R, Priyan G S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The conventional system of medical education does not entail the use of immersive, personalised, and adaptive digital learning platforms that are culturally based to teach. Available XR systems are oriented to visualisation of anatomy and procedural training, neglecting the classical health knowledge systems, which demand constitutional personalisation. PrakritiXR, a browser-native WebXR platform with a novel tri-mode adaptive learning architecture of Ayurvedic education, is described in this study. The architecture uses a closed loop architecture where dynamically changing parameters of immersive scenes through real-time ensemble-based dosha profiling is used to generate interaction patterns that enhance constitutional assessment through ontology-based conversational tutoring feedback. Dosha-specific learning modules are made contextual in culturally relevant 3D landscape, using lotus ponds, havan rites and herbal laboratories etc, and dynamically produced and adapted scenes. An ensemble classifier based on random forest pretrained on 1,247 surveys that have been annotated by a practitioner and cross-validated against 5 folds gives real-time constitutional profiling. The model had 94.1% accuracy, 93.2% precision, 92.6% recall, 93.0% F1-score, and 0.947 ROC-AUC, which were 8.3% and 6.7% higher than the SVM and neural network baselines, respectively. Classification stability in Vata, Pitta and Kapha types of classification was validated using bootstrapped 95% confidence intervals. Scene complexity, educational sequencing and visual aesthetics are automatically altered based on the predicted constitutional profiles using theory-grounded rule-based inference. An Ayurvedic text-pairing BERT model, fine-tuned on 3,200 text pairings, can be used to resolve conversational questions in real-time. With more than 500 teacher-validated interactions among the learners, the model achieved BLEU-4 score of 0.924 and cosine similarity of 0.939. A usability study under controlled conditions was conducted on 42 participants leading to an 86.7 SUS, where 34 percent of the participants had gained knowledge pre vs post-test (p<0.001). PrakritiXR is the digital health education research in which results are reproducible, timed interaction records, standardised assessment modules, and exportable datasets are offered to support the research results.

title: Modulated Reality (MoR) Using Depth Map From Generative AI
authors: Shinki Harada, Kazutake Uehira, Masahiro Suzuki, Hideaki Takada
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: The concept of “Modulated Reality (MoR),” which modulates space through the interaction between a real object in real space and an aerial image displayed using an AR device placed in front of it, has been proposed. However, the optical characteristics necessary for implementation have not been clarified, and at present it is not easy to systematize and utilize MoR as a new 3D display method. In this study, we aim to establish the optical characteristics required for systemization by clarifying the relationship between a real object and an aerial image in terms of luminance, and to implement a simplified MoR display by utilizing depth maps generated with generative AI. Through evaluation experiments, we clarified the relationship between absolute luminance and depth perception, providing the foundation for implementing MoR displays beyond the constraints of time and location.

title: PSO-Optimized Deep Learning Model for ECG-Based Arrhythmia Detection in Wearable Devices
authors: Maheswari. S, M. Venith Vijay, Umadevi. G, N. Hemavathy, Ganapathy. G, Praveen Kumar. A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The identification of heart diseases is an acute issue because the patterns of ECG signals are difficult to interpret, and traditional diagnostic frameworks are constrained. To overcome this, a Walrus Optimization-Assisted LSTM-based model is developed to enhance predictive precision by utilizing clinical and ECG data. It combines the learning of temporal features with LSTM and the adaptive hyperparameter optimization with the Walrus Optimization Algorithm, which guarantees stable convergence and a better model of the cardiac abnormalities. The overall accuracy of experimental assessment shows 98.42%, the sensitivity of 97.85%, the specificity of 98.11%, the F1-score of 98.27%, and the reduction of false positives by 16% relative to conventional deep-learning models. Also, the optimized model has a convergence rate that is 21% faster and is more resistant to noise and data imbalance. These findings validate the ability of the proposed framework to provide reliable predictive heart disease information in real-time, which has high clinical potential for deployment in automated diagnostic and monitoring systems.

title: Reduced-Order Modeling of Thyristor Module Temperature Field Based on Proper Orthogonal Decomposition and Fully Connected Neural Network
authors: Kunpeng Zha, Xiudi Geng, Tianqi Li, Huilian Liao, Mingwei Xue, Tao Wang, Shutao Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: Full-order finite element analysis of the steadystate temperature field of a thyristor module in a converter valve is time consuming. It limits fast evaluation under many operating conditions and online digital twin inference. This paper proposes a reduced-order surrogate model based on proper orthogonal decomposition (POD) and a fully connected neural network (FCNN). A snapshot matrix is constructed by sampling representative operating conditions, and the POD basis is extracted via singular value decomposition. The reduced dimension is determined according to a cumulative energy criterion. Subsequently, an FCNN is trained to learn the nonlinear mapping from operating-condition parameters to POD modal coefficients, enabling fast reconstruction of the temperature field. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces the computational cost, achieving an approximately 34-fold speedup per condition while maintaining a reconstruction error below engineering tolerance. These results meet engineering accuracy requirements and indicate that the proposed surrogate model can effectively support rapid multi-condition thermal evaluation and online condition analysis of thyristor modules.

title: Image Scene-Adaptive Hierarchical Object Detection Method Using Sequence Control and Adaptive Reconstructed Image Generation Units
authors: Ryoga Sugiuchi, Tetsuya Matsumura
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: We propose an image scene-adaptive hierarchical object detection method for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) applications. Conventional hierarchical object detection methods are based on YOLO, and typically combine a global detection model for large objects and a local detection model for small objects in a hierarchical structure to improve small object detection performance. We introduced sequence control and adaptive image reconstruction units that extract optimal detection regions based on the probability of small object presence during local detection after global detection, according to each imaging scene. The proposed method achieves faster processing, lower power consumption, and higher accuracy. Verification using the VisDrone2019 dataset showed a mean average precision (mAP) improvement of approximately 2.49 points compared to conventional methods. Furthermore, we implemented this system on a Raspberry Pi and confirmed a 21.6% speedup compared to conventional methods.

title: Medi Help: AI Driven Medication Information and Assistance
authors: C Sivamurugan, P Gowsalya, Katta Lakshmi Sai Deepika, Pilli Harsha Naga Venkata Pavan, Neelisetty Yasaswini, Mylarapu Abhi Varshini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The rapid advancement of healthcare technologies has led to the development of AI-driven systems that assist with medication information and guidance. MediHelp: AI-Driven Medication Information and Assistance Medication assistance in real-time powered by artificial intelligence. The system makes use of YOLOv9, one of the finest object detection algorithms, for which medication-related information is to be identified within an image. This system will integrate a Gemini AI-powered chatbot that enables the user to interactively query information about medication. The project uses a dataset from Roboflow in several medication classes such as Anarex, Atenelol, Calcium, Lozarsin, and Paracetamol. It is a system that makes use of YOLO v9 for efficient image recognition, whereas the chatbot, supported by Gemini AI, enhances user interaction by responding to an accurate and context-aware response based on the medication data. In this way, this will boost the medication aid process and enhance the user experience while making the health information more accessible. The training of these AI models using Python, TensorFlow, and Roboflow for real-time medication information and assistance has been done. By combining deep learning and natural language processing, the project explores innovative ways to provide AI-driven support for medication management

title: A Multi-Class Personal Protective Equipment Detection Framework using YOLOv8 for Industrial Applications
authors: M. Faris Al Hakim, Hadid Ramadhan, Subhan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Ensuring compliance with personal protective equipment (PPE) is essential for reducing workplace accidents and improving safety in industrial environments. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled real-time vision-based monitoring systems; however, many existing approaches rely on single-source datasets and limited PPE categories, which restrict their generalization in real-world scenarios. This study proposes a YOLOv8-based framework for multi-class PPE detection trained on a combined multi-source dataset covering eight PPE-related categories. The proposed method leverages the anchor-free one-stage architecture of YOLOv8 to enable efficient end-to-end training and real-time inference across heterogeneous data conditions. Experimental results show that the model achieves an overall mean Average Precision at an Intersection over Union threshold of 0.5 (mAP@0.5) of 0.766 and mAP@0.5:0.95 of 0.447, demonstrating strong detection performance across multiple PPE categories. High accuracy is observed for PPE items with distinctive visual features, while challenges remain in detecting context-dependent non-compliance cases. Overall, the proposed framework provides a balanced solution between detection accuracy and computational efficiency, while improving generalization across diverse industrial environments. These findings highlight the potential of multi-source training strategies for developing scalable and practical PPE monitoring systems.

title: Applications of Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT): An Overview
authors: S. Vishnu, Vishnuvarthan Rajagopal, A. Alfred Kirubaraj, Praveena Sirimella, Adnan M. Abu-Mahfouz, S. R. Jino Ramson
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Conventional monitoring systems have been revolutionized with the advancements in capabilities of IoT and AI in terms of data collection and decision making respectively. This is coined as AIoT (Artificial Intelligence of Things). This paper conducts study on various applications of AIoT in diverse domains, underlining the developments in technology, challenges, and future possibilities. The focused application areas are industrial automation, crowd monitoring, disaster management, military, agriculture, waste management, health care, smart grid, and transportation. The detailed review is conducted in the paper depicting the role of AIoT in transforming conventional systems to autonomous and intelligent systems.

title: Hybrid Multi-Expert Fusion Framework for Robust Brain Tumor Classification using MRI and CT Images
authors: Mohammed Ali Shaik, Adupa Vivek
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Precise identification of brain tumors through medical imaging is essential for clinical diagnosis and treatment strategies. Nonetheless, interpreting MRI and CT scans manually takes a lot of time and is subject to variability between different observers. This research introduces MSEG-Net (Modality-Specific Expert Gating Network), a combined multi-expert deep learning architecture aimed at effective brain tumor classification utilizing multimodal MRI and CT scans. The suggested architecture utilizes five advanced backbone networks—ConvNeXt-Tiny, EfficientNetV2-S, Swin Transformer-Tiny, MaxViT-Tiny, and ResNet50—as experts tailored to specific modalities. Rather than using traditional feature-level fusion, a trainable gating network actively combines expert predictions via probability-level fusion, maintaining modality-specific traits and enhancing classification stability. The model underwent assessment on a dataset comprising 9,618 multimodal brain images, utilizing stratified 5-fold cross-validation. Experimental findings show a mean accuracy of 98.12% ± 0.28, F1-score of 97.86% ± 0.31, and AUC of 0.994 ± 0.002, surpassing the performance of individual backbone networks by 0.27–4.8%. Ablation studies additionally validate the efficacy of multimodal fusion, expert gating, and data enhancement. The suggested framework offers a dependable and understandable method for automated brain tumour diagnosis, showing significant promise for actual clinical use.

title: Safety AI Embedded IOT: Real-Time Road Surface Monitoring And Hazard Detection Using Acoustic And Ultrasonic Sensing
authors: Sabareesh K. S., Praveenkumar V., P. Kaladevi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The factors that endanger the safety of the vehicles and transportation qualities are the road surface degradation, potholes, lumpy pavements and abrupt impact conditions. Traditional methods of inspection are either manual surveys or periodic monitoring using cameras and in most cases, these methods are labour intensives, inaccurate and cannot give real-time hazard warnings. In this paper, Safety AI Embedded IoT is an intelligent embedded system, which is intended to monitor the road surface in real-time and warn about hazards. Its system combines ultrasonic pothole depth estimation sensors, gyroscopes and accelerator devices used to note sudden movements and acoustic sensors used to analyze vibration as surface texture. The AI-based classifier is used to analyze the data obtained and classify the road surfaces as smooth, rough, or damaged in real time. When the system notices dangerous situations, the system gives instant warnings to the driver and a buzzer and LCD display, and the GPS-tagged information on hazards is sent to an IoT cloud platform to be monitored remotely and integrated into the smart city dashboard. The design focuses on low power consumption, cost-effectiveness, scalability and easy integration with the existing infrastructure. The experimental outcomes show better hazard awareness, the possibility of accidents decreases, and the priorities of road maintenance are based on the data. The piece is useful in advancing intelligent transportation systems and active urban design, which has the potential of being scaled to a large scale in various settings.
