title: An Overview of the TV 3.0 Application Coding Layer Specification
authors: Joel dos Santos, Li-Chang Shuen Cristina Silva Sousa, Carlos Soares Neto, Guido Lemos, Sérgio Colcher, Marcelo F. Moreno, Débora Muchaluat-Saade
publish: IEEE Transactions on Broadcasting
abstract: This article presents the technical specifications resulting from R&D and standardization efforts in the application coding layer of the next generation of the Brazilian Digital Terrestrial Television System (SBTVD), known as TV 3.0 or DTV+. The architecture evolves the Ginga middleware into an application-oriented platform, specifying APIs and definitions that enable personalized experiences, integration with Over-The-Top (OTT) services, new forms of interaction and immersion, enhanced accessibility, and audience data collection with privacy safeguards. The initiative is the result of collaboration among the SBTVD Forum, its associated companies, the Ministry of Communications, the National Education and Research Network (RNP), and Brazilian universities.

title: Amplitude-Phase-Sphere Block Modulation and Demodulation for Resisting Phase Noise in Millimeter-Wave Single-Carrier Wireless Communications
authors: Guoxing Duan, Min Fan, Wei Xu, Haiming Wang, Xiaohu You
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Phase noise (PN) significantly degrades the performance of millimeter-wave (mmWave) wireless communication systems. To mitigate this challenge, we propose the amplitude-phase-sphere block modulation (APSBM) scheme, which integrates amplitude-shift keying, phase-shift keying, and sphere modulation. Exploiting the Wiener random walk characteristic of PN, APSBM carries information on the relative amplitude and phase between consecutive symbols, thereby counteracting the impact of common PN. Immunity to residual PN in the scheme is further enhanced through a three-dimensional spherical constellation and modulation configurations. We also propose flexible detection strategies (joint, parallel, and cascaded) at the receiver to accommodate diverse application scenarios, minimizing the influence of PN while maintaining a low computational burden. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that in the presence of PN, APSBM outperforms quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM), circular QAM, and spiral modulation, achieving a lower peak-to-average power ratio, a reduced bit error rate, and a higher achievable information rate. The proposed modulation and demodulation scheme is particularly effective under high PN conditions, offering a robust solution for PN mitigation in mmWave wireless communication systems.

title: Survey on Disaster Management Datasets for Remote Sensing Based Emergency Applications
authors: Alain P. Ndigande, Josiah Wiggins, Sedat Ozer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Recent natural disasters have highlighted the urgent need for efficient data-driven approaches to disaster management. Machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques have shown considerable promise in enhancing the key phases of disaster management including mitigation, preparedness, detection, response, and recovery. A critical enabler of successful ML or DL based applications in remote sensing, however, is the accessibility and quality of annotated datasets. With the growing availability of high-resolution imagery from unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and satellites, computer vision and remote sensing algorithms have become essential tools for rapid detection, situational assessment, and decision-making in disaster scenarios. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of publicly available image-based datasets relevant to ML/DL-based disaster management pipelines. Emphasis is placed on datasets that support computer vision and remote sensing tasks across all phases of disaster events including pre-disaster, during, and post-disaster. The goal of this work is to serve as a centralized reference for researchers and practitioners seeking high-quality datasets for rapid development and deployment of remote sensing-driven disaster response solutions.

title: A Physics-Related Parameter Decoupling and Self-Attention-Based Framework for NBTI Aging Prediction
authors: Hao Huang, Qianqian Sang, Chaoming Liu, Le Gao, Jiaming Zhou, Mingxue Huo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Negative bias temperature instability (NBTI) is a key factor limiting the reliability of advanced semiconductor devices. However, accurate aging modeling faces the dual challenges of complex parameter extraction in traditional physical models and the heavy data dependency of pure data-driven models. To address these issues, this article proposes a neural-network framework based on physics-related parameter decoupling and an attention mechanism, named the attention-based physics-related parameter decoupling network (APPD-Net). By structurally embedding the power-law model and utilizing multitask learning (MTL) together with a self-attention mechanism, this framework constructs a high-accuracy prediction model for NBTI degradation. Validated on experimental data from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.18~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m pMOSFETs, APPD-Net outperforms classic neural network models such as the artificial neural network (ANN), convolutional neural network (CNN), and Transformer, even when using as little as 20% of the available training samples. Furthermore, the framework autonomously decouples the intermediate physics-related parameters <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$A$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, which can be used for compact aging modeling, while maintaining strong long-term extrapolation capability. APPD-Net combines the predictive power of deep learning with the structural advantages of physical models, providing a practical approach for developing high-fidelity and explainable aging models for SPICE-oriented circuit simulation.

title: VISTA-LoRa: Task-Oriented Semantic Visual Transmission for Ultra-Low-Bitrate LoRa
authors: Muhammad Toaha Raza Khan
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Long Range (LoRa)’s ultra-low bitrate and strict payload constraints make conventional visual transmission infeasible for embedded Internet of Things (IoT) nodes. This letter presents VISTA-LoRa, a fully deterministic and training-free task-oriented semantic framework that transmits only clarity-selected and morphology-adapted visual regions required for a downstream detector. The VISTA-LoRa pipeline integrates clarity-aware spatio-temporal Region of Interest (ROI) selection, an airtime-normalised morphology-variant optimiser, and a Low-Frequency/High-Frequency (LF/HF)-weighted Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) encoder with Reed-Solomon (RS) protection, followed by multi-scale semantic verification at the receiver. Experiments on real tracked frames show that VISTA-LoRa achieves the highest semantic reliability among the evaluated schemes, obtaining a mean receiver-side confidence of 0.78 and a 4.5–6.4× payload reduction while preserving perfect structural consistency. Such a payload-efficient, high-confidence semantic uplink is particularly valuable for delay-tolerant visual IoT applications such as remote inspection, environmental monitoring, and low-power autonomous systems that must operate within the severe energy, bandwidth, and duty-cycle restrictions of LoRa-class networks. Despite using stronger quantisation and spatial reduction, VISTA-LoRa maintains 91.7% of the original semantic confidence compared to 75.0% for baseline approaches under identical constraints demonstrating its suitability for visual reporting over ultra-low-bitrate LoRa links.

title: High-Precision Stereo Matching Based on Selective Attention and Residual Cost Aggregation for Real-Time Autonomous Driving on Edge Devices
authors: Bifa Liang, Yichao Wang, Ziyang Hu, Zhicong Huang, Haifeng Hu, Jianming Xu, Dihu Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Stereo matching is essential for depth estimation in autonomous driving, often balancing accuracy and efficiency, especially on resource-constrained edge devices. High-precision networks demand heavy computation, while speed-optimized ones compromise accuracy. This work presents SARNet, a real-time, high-precision stereo matching network for autonomous driving, designed for efficient deployment on the edge device NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin. We develop SAFE, a Selective Attention Feature Extractor (SAFE) that integrates patch layers, convolutions, and attention mechanisms for stereo feature extraction. To generate high-quality disparity candidates, we employ a Group-wise L2 Distance to construct a full-range yet compact cost volume. Additionally, the Enhanced Residual Cost Aggregation (ERCA) module enhances cost aggregation accuracy while maintaining efficiency. To further improve precision, we propose a Confidence-weighted Residual Refinement (CWRR) post-processing module that jointly predicts residuals and per pixel confidence, weights residual updates by confidence to suppress erroneous corrections in low-confidence regions, and incorporates a lightweight channel–spatial attention to boost representational capacity. SARNet employs a four-stage selective attention-based residual framework, using a coarse-to-fine strategy and residual computation to balance accuracy and speed. Optimized with TensorRT, SARNet exceeds real-time requirements, making it ideal for autonomous depth perception. Extensive experiments confirm its superior speed-accuracy trade off over existing stereo matching networks.

title: Toward Intrinsically Calibrated Uncertainty Quantification in Industrial Data-Driven Models via Diffusion Sampler
authors: Yiran Ma, Jerome Le Ny, Zhichao Chen, Zhihuan Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: In modern process industries, data-driven models are important tools for real-time monitoring when key performance indicators are difficult to measure directly. While accurate predictions are essential, reliable uncertainty quantification (UQ) is equally critical for safety, reliability, and decision-making, but remains a major challenge in current data-driven approaches. In this work, we introduce a diffusion-based posterior sampling framework that inherently produces well-calibrated predictive uncertainty via faithful posterior sampling, eliminating the need for post hoc calibration. In extensive evaluations on synthetic distributions, the Raman-based phenylacetic acid soft sensor benchmark, and a real ammonia synthesis case study, our method achieves practical improvements over existing UQ techniques in both uncertainty calibration and predictive accuracy. These results highlight diffusion samplers as a principled and scalable paradigm for advancing uncertainty-aware modeling in industrial applications.

title: FBG-assisted no-core/hollow-core hybrid fiber for temperature and strain sensing
authors: Fenglin Xian, Gang Lu, Gaige Zheng
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: We demonstrate a compact hybrid fiber sensor combining a co-located fiber Bragg grating and a no-core-fiber/ hollow-core-fiber in-line modal interferometer for simultaneous temperature and axial-strain sensing. Distinct temperature-to-strain sensitivity ratios of the Bragg resonance λB and interferometric extremum λI enable wavelength-only decoupling via a 2 × 2 sensitivity matrix. The calibrated sensitivities are 0.38536 nm/°C and 0.00105422 nm/με for the FBG channel, and 0.90394 nm/°C and 0.00629945 nm/με for the interferometric channel. The retrieval root-mean-square errors are 0.0091°C and 0.868 με, respectively. This architecture combines the robustness and multiplexing compatibility of FBGs with the high responsivity of modal interference for practical multi-parameter sensing.

title: SenseHD: A 40 μ W 8-bit Accelerator Wake-Up Circuit for Always-on Smart IoT Sensor Monitoring
authors: Andrew Ding, Sungheon Jeong, Xuyang Liu, Hamza E. Barkam, Tamoghno Das, Shaahin Angizi, Nader Bagherzadeh, Mohsen Imani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: IoT sensors and devices heavily rely on cloud-based processing, especially in healthcare monitoring, for sensor data analysis, which incurs high data transfer costs and energy consumption. To reduce constant data transmission, always-on smart sensors can use lightweight brain-inspired Hyperdimensional computing (HDC) models to efficiently classify sensor data that require further resource-intensive cloud processing. The proposed architecture maintains HDC algorithm accuracy with 8-bit integer precision for complex datasets, where 1-bit quantization is not feasible. We propose SenseHD, an all-digital CMOS always-on smart sensor accelerator for energy-constrained smart sensing applications. SenseHD achieves an extremely small power consumption of 39.21 - 92.59 μW depending on design parameterization, supporting 8-bit hypervectors and an energy-efficient near-memory fused item memory + multiply-accumulate systolic array encoder. We introduce a novel systolic similarity search unit that utilizes a small systolic array to calculate hypervector cosine similarity metrics, consuming only 110.89 nJ/inference. By intelligently transmitting only relevant data, SenseHD reduces system-level energy consumption by up to 8.8× compared to a cloud-centric baseline. Notably, the accelerator overhead is negligible, consuming only 1.2% of the total system energy.

title: Fine-grained upper limb force control relies on distributed functional connectivity during motor planning: a scalp EEG study
authors: Chunli Chen, Jiaxin Zhao, Xiabing Zhang, Bin Lu, Dezhong Yao, Jing Lu, Yangsong Zhang, Peng Xu, Xuntai Ma, Fali Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Motor behavior consists of two distinct stages, i.e., planning and execution. However, the underlying brain network patterns that distinguish these two stages remain insufficiently understood. Moreover, the modulation of these network dynamics by different levels of motor force, such as those in daily grasping tasks, has been given limited consideration. In this study, we simultaneously recorded electroencephalogram (EEG) and electromyography (EMG) signals while participants performed upper limb grasping at different percentages of maximum voluntary contraction (MVC). Our results initially indicated that high-force conditions (100% MVC) generated more pronounced movement-related cortical potentials (MRCPs) compared to low-force conditions (20% MVC). Functional connectivity (FC) analysis further demonstrated that motor planning was associated with a more extensive network pattern, especially across the frontal, parietal, and temporal lobes, than motor execution. Additionally, during the planning stage, 20% MVC induced stronger long-range FC, particularly between fronto-occipital and fronto-parietal regions. Conversely, during execution, 100% MVC resulted in stronger FC mainly between frontal and central regions. Based on these network disparities, the classification accuracy for motor stages was 81.73% ± 11.25% under the 20% MVC condition and 84.65% ± 22.48% under the 100% MVC condition. Regarding force strengths, the classification accuracy was 75.58% ± 14.68% during planning and 90.39% ± 7.01% during execution. These results suggest that motor planning necessitates the recruitment of additional cognitive networks to support decision-making processes, especially for fine-grained motor tasks like small force control, whereas motor execution depends more significantly on sensorimotor networks to maintain movement performance. This functional differentiation highlights a hierarchical organization of motor control at the brain network level, thereby deepening our understanding of how distributed neural circuits support distinct stages of motor behavior.

title: WAS-Mamba: 3D Medical Image Segmentation via Windowed Attention State Space Model
authors: Xueren Zhang, Xianghong Wang, Nuo Tong, Peng Jin, Jichen Du, Mingchao Ding, Lequan Yu, Yixuan Yuan, Tianye Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Mamba, the state space model (SSM), has attracted significant attention for its ability to model long-range dependencies with linear complexity, achieving success in medical image segmentation. However, the previous cross-scanning approach in Mamba struggles to capture both long-range and short-range dependencies simultaneously and treats the features of each path equally. This imbalance between local and global modeling capabilities can adversely impact segmentation performance. To address these challenges, we propose WAS-Mamba, a novel method specifically designed for Mamba-based medical image segmentation. WAS-Mamba introduces a cross-channel window scanning strategy (CCWScan) that enables sequences to preserve original local image features during the transformation process. Furthermore, WAS-Mamba employs a weighted state space model (WSSM) to dynamically fuse spatial and frequency domain information, improving the capture of local details and global context for accurate segmentation. We validated the superior performance of WAS-Mamba across five datasets covering different anatomical regions, which include CT and MRI images: Synapse, BTCV, ACDC, BraTS, and Decathlon-Lung. In particular, we achieved a Dice coefficient of 88.09% on the Synapse dataset, with a 33% reduction in computational complexity and inference time compared to the second-best model. The code and model will be released at https://github.com/1605066114/WAS-Mamba.

title: Sat-SFC: Service Function Chain Placement in Battery Supply Varying Satellite Networks
authors: Xiao Chen, Zenghao Hu, Boyu Wang, Chao Zhu, Zhenyu Zhao, Xin Li, Fangxin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: With the rapid deployment of low Earth orbit (LEO) mega-constellations, satellites are increasingly expected to provide Internet access, remote sensing, and telemetry services. Many of these tasks are resource-intensive, requiring both high computational capacity and stable communication links, which are scarce in dynamic satellite environments. Service function chaining (SFC) offers a promising paradigm by decomposing large services into sequential sub-services for distributed processing on satellites, thereby reducing ground communication overhead and improving responsiveness. However, efficient SFC placement in LEO networks remains challenging due to fast satellite mobility, intermittent connectivity, and fluctuating energy availability caused by orbital dynamics. To address these challenges, we propose Sat-SFC, a battery-aware reinforcement learning framework for SFC placement in dynamic LEO satellite networks. Sat-SFC jointly optimizes placement, scheduling, and quality-level selection while explicitly considering computational and energy constraints. The framework extends the PPO backbone with a dual-input encoder that captures both system states and resource features, and introduces a masked parallel action generation mechanism to efficiently handle the multi-discrete decision space. Furthermore, a battery-aware reward design ensures sustainable operation by avoiding resource depletion under varying energy conditions. Extensive simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of Sat-SFC. Compared with baseline algorithms-including PPO, A2C, A3C, DQN, and DD-DQN-Sat-SFC achieves up to 9% higher rewards than PPO under quality-dominant settings, doubles the utilization rate compared to Random and A3C, and maintains significantly lower latency than value-based methods. These results confirm that Sat-SFC can effectively balance the quality-latency trade-off and improve resource efficiency in LEO satellite networks.

title: Feature Smoothing for Generating Transferable Adversarial Examples
authors: Junlin Liu, Xinchen Lyu, Chenshan Ren, Ni Wei, Jingfeng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Transfer-based adversarial attacks generate transferable adversarial examples from a surrogate model to deceive unknown target models. Input diversity is an effective technique to enhance the adversarial transferability by shifting the attention regions of inputs. However, current existing input-diversity-based attacks primarily focus on the input space, neglecting the impact of high-frequency components in the feature space. This limits the adversarial transferability because adversarial perturbations primarily concentrate on high-frequency components. In this paper, we propose a feature orchestration (FO) layer to smooth the extracted embeddings via trained low-pass filters. FO filters out the high-frequency components in the feature space, alleviating the overfitting of adversarial noise to the surrogate model. Based on the proposed FO, we propose a novel feature orchestration attack (FOA). Specifically, FOA apply various transformations and mixups to generate diverse variants in the input space, and smooths the extracted embeddings by FO in the feature space. Evaluations on ImageNet and Caltech101 underscore the superior attack performance of the proposed FOA, compared to the state-of-the-art (SOTA) baselines. In particular, the targeted success rates of FOA outperform the SOTA baselines by about 7%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>65% on ImageNet and about 3%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>33% on Caltech101. Even against defended models, FOA achieves an average targeted success rate of over 53%, exceeding baselines by more than 40%.

title: UniEmo: Unifying Emotional Understanding and Generation with Learnable Expert Queries
authors: Yijie Zhu, Lingsen Zhang, Zitong Yu, Rui Shao, Tao Tan, Liqiang Nie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Emotional understanding and generation are often treated as separate tasks, yet they are inherently complementary and can mutually enhance each other. In this paper, we propose the UniEmo, a unified framework that seamlessly integrates these two tasks. The key challenge lies in the abstract nature of emotions, necessitating the extraction of visual representations beneficial for both tasks. To address this, we propose a hierarchical emotional understanding chain with learnable expert queries that progressively extracts multi-scale emotional features, thereby serving as a foundational step for unification. Simultaneously, we fuse these expert queries and emotional representations to guide the diffusion model in generating emotion-evoking images. To enhance the diversity and fidelity of the generated emotional images, we further introduce the emotional correlation coefficient and emotional condition loss into the fusion process. This step facilitates fusion and alignment for emotional generation guided by the understanding. In turn, we demonstrate that joint training allows the generation component to provide implicit feedback to the understanding part. Furthermore, we propose a novel data filtering algorithm to select high-quality and diverse emotional images generated by the well-trained model, which explicitly feedback into the understanding part. Together, these generation-driven dual feedback processes enhance the model’s understanding capacity. Extensive experiments show that UniEmo significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both emotional understanding and generation tasks. The code for the proposed method is available at https://github.com/JiuTian-VL/UniEmo.

title: Assessing Surgeons' Physiological Fatigue During Robot-Assisted Surgery Tasks Using a Three-Dimensional Visualization System
authors: Hiroki Yamazaki, Takao Yamashita, Naoki Kobayashi
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: In this study, we investigated physiological fatigue experienced by surgeons performing robot-assisted surgery using a three-dimensional (3D) visualization system. Heart rate variability and NASA Task Load Index (NASA-TLX) scores were recorded while surgeons performed a basic peg movement task using a physical training model. In addition to conventional frequency-domain analysis, we determined the motion-corrected LF/HF ratio and normalized LF/HF and biosignal influence time at rest to minimize the effects of postural changes and subtle body movements. The normalized LF/HF and the ratio of biosignal influence time to total task time showed significant correlations with the physical load subscale (PD) of the NASA-TLX. These results suggest that the normalized LF/HF ratio more accurately than conventional LF/HF reflects surgeons' physiological fatigue during static or quasi-static robotic manipulation and is useful for assessing surgeons' workload in 3D robotic surgical environments.

title: Classification and Segmentation Of Eosinophilic Esophagitis Using ViT and FCN-ResNet50 Models
authors: Divya R, Swathi Mogilisetty, Shamil S U, D Prema
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This accurate analysis of gastrointestinal (GI) endoscopic images is essential for the early detection of eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE), a chronic inflammatory condition of the esophagus caused by excessive eosinophil accumulation. Traditional diagnostic procedures are invasive and subject to observer variation. This study presents a hybrid deep learning framework that combines Vision Transformer (ViT) and Full Convolutional Network (FCN-ResNet50) architecture for automatic detection and localization of EoE features. The framework achieved a classification accuracy of 98.2%, an F1 score of 0.86, and segmentation calculations with an IOU of 0.78 and Dice coefficient of 0.84, resulting in an overall combined accuracy of 97.3%. The results confirm that the proposed method provides consistent and interpretable diagnostic results, reduces manual effort and improves reliability. This hybrid model shows strong potential as a computer-assisted tool for accurate and early assessment of EoE in clinical endoscopy.

title: A Hybrid CNN-LSTM Model for Multimodal Biometric Authentication via ECG and Respiration
authors: Ganeshan R, M.Neha, P.Vasudev
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Biometric authentication systems have mostly used physical traits like fingerprints, facial recognition, or iris patterns. But these techniques can be spoofed and attacked by the environment. In order to overcome these problems, physiological indicators like Electrocardiogram (ECG) and respiratory rates have been used as trustworthy and dependable biometrics in tackling these problems. We create a hybrid CNN-LSTM-based deep learning model in this work to identify the person by using a set of ECG and respiration signals. We remove noise and baseline drift with a frequency range of 0.540 Hz, and we remove real breathing cycles with a frequency range of 0.10.35 Hz. The two signals are standardized and divided into 5 seconds windows and then fed into the model. The CNN derives spatial relationships on the signal morphology whereas LSTM derives temporal relationships resulting in robust feature learning. The accuracy and stability of the experimental results is high among different individuals and this confirms that ECG and respiration patterns are distinguishable, just like fingerprints. This paper highlights the opportunities of physiological signal-based multimodal biometrics as a real-time authentication system to be incorporated with hardware in healthcare and security services.

title: A Hybrid Game-Theoretic Framework for Dynamic Pricing and Charging Coordination in Multi-Station Electric Vehicle Networks
authors: Liqing Chen, Dilong Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: This paper addresses the dynamic pricing and charging scheduling problem in multi-station electric vehicle charging networks. We propose a bi-level Stackelberg game framework where charging stations act as leaders optimizing pricing strategies to maximize profits, while EV users act as followers optimizing charging schedules to minimize costs including charging fees, battery degradation, and inconvenience penalties. The problem is reformulated as a Mathematical Program with Equilibrium Constraints by incorporating the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions of the lower-level user problems. A hybrid solution algorithm combining Particle Swarm Optimization for the upper-level pricing problem and Newton’s method for the lower-level user optimization is developed to solve the non-convex bi-level problem efficiently. The algorithm employs domain-knowledge-based initialization and penalty-based constraint handling to improve convergence. Experimental results in four scenarios demonstrate that the proposed framework successfully achieves valley filling by shifting charging demand to off-peak periods, converges within 50 iterations, and balances station profitability with user cost-effectiveness under various operational conditions including high demand, price sensitivity, and reduced charging efficiency. The framework provides a practical solution for coordinated pricing and scheduling in large-scale EV charging networks.

title: Effect of HMD Frame Rate Reduction on Attentional Orienting in a Driving Simulator
authors: Sora Maehara, Wataru HASHIMOTO, Satoshi NISHIGUCHI, Yasuharu MIZUTANI
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: This study examines whether drivers' attention in a simulated driving environment can be directed by temporarily reducing the frame rate of the head-mounted display (HMD) when the participant gazes at specific objects. In our experiment, the frame rate of HMD was dynamically reduced based on gaze fixation, and we assessed its impact by measuring participants' accelerator-release reaction times in response to the sudden appearance of target objects. The results demonstrated that reaction times were significantly reduced when the target appeared within the HMD field of view under reduced frame rate conditions, suggesting that transient frame rate reduction can function as an effective attentional cue without the user consciously noticing it. These findings point to a practical approach for designing VR training scenarios that aim to direct user attention while maintaining a high level of immersion, and they highlight the importance of further research into optimal parameter settings and individual variability.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning–based Adaptive Routing for VANETs with Real-Time Congestion Awareness
authors: P. Janani Durga, V.Mani, R.Suresh, S.Arivumani Samson, S.Saranya, S. Manibharathi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks (VANETs) experience dynamical changes in topology as well as traffic jams, which significantly deteriorates the routing performance. To address this dilemma, the current paper presents a deep learning-based adaptive routing architecture that dynamically chooses the routing paths by exploiting real-time congestion information. The routing problem is modeled in terms of sequential decision making, where the learning agent monitors values of the traffic density, the vehicle speed and the congestion states so as to maximize the routing actions. Realistic congestion patterns have been included in order to add flexibility to the different traffic conditions. As has been shown by the experimental evaluation, the proposed approach achieves a routing accuracy of 95.6%, which is 19.3% points higher than the conventional approaches to routing involving the use of the static routing algorithm. In addition, the model promotes congestion -state discrimination with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.964 thus suggesting effective decision making process in dynamic settings. These findings verify that incorporation of congestion-aware learning significantly enhances the efficiency of routing, dependability and resiliency in the VANET environment that is highly dynamic.

title: Audio Transmission Using Free Space Optical Communication
authors: Selvi. M, Rupesh. J, Kameshwari. M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The use of free space optical (FSO) communication has already become an option that can replace radio-frequency systems as a high-bandwidth, low-latency technology, and the use of real-time audio transmission is underutilized. The reviewed content provides a synthesis of the results of twenty peer-reviewed publications in the areas of FSO system design, atmospheric propagation, multimodal optical transceivers, modulation and signal processing, pointing and tracking mechanisms, visible light and underwater optical communication, and new quantum-secured and photonic technologies. The analysis shows that the most severe causes of perceptual audio degradation are atmospheric turbulence, beam wander, attenuation due to fog and pointing instability, which result in amplitude fading, jitter, and dropout. Modulation schemes and signal-processing methods currently used are not designed to be optimized to meet the continuity and latency requirements of audio signals, even at high rates. Pointing, acquisition and tracking limits are other limitations that limit stability, particularly in mobile or non-terrestrial platforms. Nevertheless, the development of adaptive optics, multimodal FSO, optical computing, and quantum communication can present effective efforts to enhance audio fidelity. On the whole, the review indicates the necessity of audio-focused FSO research and suggests specific future directions, namely, perceptual assessment, low-latency modulation design, sophisticated PAT systems, and safe optical audio systems.

title: Detection and Mitigation of Replay Attacks in CCTV Systems
authors: Mohit Prasad Singh, Shreyas Suresh, Soumya Ranjan Mishra, Suhas Venkata Karamalaputti, Manju
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Video surveillance systems are critically vulnerable to replay attacks, in which the live feeds are intercepted and replaced with pre-recorded loops in order to hide malicious activity. The traditional methods of detection involving deep learning also often remain computationally opaque and very resource-intensive, while simple frame matching usually fails when video content is re-encoded. This paper proposes an innovative, dual-layer detection framework, which fuses probabilistic temporal modeling with deterministic content verification. The system takes as input live Real-Time Streaming Protocol streams and encodes dense optical flow into Sparse Distributed Representations (SDRs) that are analyzed by a Hierarchical Temporal Memory (HTM) model for real-time motion anomaly detection. Concurrently, a secondary Auditor layer uses Perceptual Hashing to identify invariant-to-compression loops. To bridge the latency gap between these layers, we introduce Pulsea novel heartbeat-like synchronization protocol—that ties asynchronous HTM inference with real-time hash verification. This enables a high-confidence Intersection Logic that eliminates static-scene false positives. The experimental results indicate that our system successfully flags replay attacks within 5 seconds with a less than 4% false positive rate, offering a lightweight, explainable hardware-agnostic solution for secure surveillance.

title: Disaster Relief Information Extraction using Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning
authors: Charvi Gupta, Ananya Srivastava, Tarun Jain
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Disaster relief operations depend on information that must be timely and accurate. Twitter and other social media platforms deliver updates that happen in real time, but filtering relevant (on-topic) disaster-related information is challenging. This research uses Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to classify tweets using the CrisisLexT6 dataset. We applied Bag of Words (BoW) and Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) for feature extraction and evaluated six classifiers. For BoW, Logistic Regression achieved the highest accuracy (94.82%), followed by Random Forest (94.52%) and SVM (94.12%). For TF-IDF, SVM achieved the highest accuracy (94.65%), followed by Random Forest (94.59%) and Logistic Regression (93.31%). Naïve Bayes and Decision Tree showed moderate performance but was outperformed by other models. KNN had the lowest accuracy due to its inefficiency with high-dimensional text data. The proposed comparative analysis demonstrates that machine learning-based NLP models significantly improve disaster tweet classification, leading to improved crisis response efforts.

title: A Deep Transfer Vision Framework for Live–Dead Fry Detection in High-Density Largemouth Bass Aquaculture
authors: Pops V. Madriaga, Christopher Cunanan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Accurate monitoring of fish fry populations is essential for effective aquaculture management, particularly during early developmental stages when survival rate and stocking density strongly influence production outcomes. However, automated detection of fish fry remains challenging due to their extremely small size, visual similarity, and frequent occlusion in dense rearing environments. This study presents a deep transfer vision–based detection framework for the simultaneous identification of live and dead largemouth bass (Micropterus salmoides) fry under high-density conditions. A large-scale annotated dataset comprising 45,120 images is constructed, containing 325,799 live fry and 22,819 dead fry instances, with individual images including 20–80 fry and exhibiting complex visual scenarios such as overlap, occlusion, and adhesion. Extensive preprocessing and data augmentation are applied to improve robustness to geometric and photometric variability. Experimental results demonstrate stable training convergence and strong generalization performance, achieving precision and recall exceeding 0.99 and a peak mAP@50–95 of 0.9671. Class-wise evaluation confirms reliable discrimination between live and dead fry, while qualitative testing on unseen dense aquaculture scenes validates the robustness of the proposed approach. The developed framework enables automated fry counting, mortality assessment, biomass estimation, and density management, contributing to the advancement of intelligent and data-driven aquaculture systems.

title: Resilient DevSecOps: Self-Healing Cloud-Native Systems via SRE-Driven AI Threat Detection and Response
authors: Rajesh Lingam, Supreet Nagi, Mourya Chigurupati, Bhargava Teja Myneni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Cloud-native systems operate in highly dynamic environments where continuous deployment, service elasticity, and distributed execution expose platforms to evolving security threats and reliability risks. Conventional DevSecOps practices automate security integration into development pipelines; however, they often remain reactive, depend on manual intervention, and lack runtime resilience when systems experience active attacks or unexpected failures. This creates a need for architectures that can autonomously detect threats, mitigate risk, and maintain operational reliability.This paper proposes a resilience-centered DevSecOps framework that unifies Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) principles with AI-driven threat detection and automated remediation to enable self-healing cloud-native systems. The framework incorporates continuous telemetry ingestion, anomaly and threat inference, reliability-aware risk prioritization, and automated recovery mechanisms embedded into the operational lifecycle. The objective is to preserve service availability, reduce incident response latency, and contain failure impact during security events.Architectural modelling and evaluation demonstrate that the proposed approach enhances reliability posture, improves operational continuity, and supports proactive resilience under adversarial and failure-prone conditions. This work contributes an SRE-aligned DevSecOps paradigm that advances cloud-native security from reactive protection toward autonomous, reliability governed security assurance.

title: A Unified Optimization Framework for Efficient Skeleton Rigging and Deformation Modeling in Interactive Digital Media Environments
authors: XiaoYu Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Skeleton rigging and deformation modeling are core components of character animation generation in interactive digital media. Theoretically, their modeling accuracy, physical consistency, and real-time performance directly impact the system's visual realism and interactive responsiveness. Addressing the problems of fragmented structural modeling, unstable weight fields, insufficient physical consistency, and the difficulty in balancing efficiency and accuracy in existing methods, this paper proposes a unified optimization framework for complex multi-class 3D models (Unified Framework for Skeleton Rigging and Deformation, UF-SRD). This framework, under unified energy function modeling, organically integrates multi-layer mesh aggregation, radial basis function (RBF) reparameterization, bounded biharmonic weight modeling, dual quaternion hybrid skinning, and implicit geometric constraint mechanisms. This organic combination achieves multi-physics coupled solution for skeleton structure generation, weight field construction, and physically consistent deformation. At the modeling level, this paper constructs a structure-adaptive background mesh through a priority-driven multi-layer mesh coarsening and directional aggregation strategy. At the weight level, the introduction of a bounded biharmonic energy functional ensures that the weight field possesses high-order smoothness while satisfying normalization and skeleton coverage constraints. At the deformation level, this study constructs a dual quaternion hybrid transformation operator and introduces implicit field constraints based on HRBF to compensate for local volume loss, joint distortion, and geometric errors in thin-shell regions. This model was systematically validated on a standard test set containing 312 multi-class 3D models. The results show that, compared with mainstream methods such as PhysRig and DDR, the proposed method reduces skeleton topology consistency error by approximately 32.0% and 43.7%, respectively, while keeping joint position errors stably within 8 mm. Simultaneously, the Laplacian energy of the weight field is reduced by approximately 39.5%. Under complex motion sequences, the volume retention rate is close to 98%; in thin-shell structures and fine geometric regions, the self-crossing rate is reduced to 3.4%; and in terms of real-time performance, the single-frame deformation efficiency is improved by approximately 40% compared to PhysRig.

title: Graph Neural Network based Intrusion Detection using Temporal Attention and Encrypted Traffic Modelling
authors: Poornachandra Rao Mada, Srinivasa Rao Pendela, Ramachandran Vedantham
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Current networks produce encrypted traffic at a tremendous rate thus rendering the old signature-based intrusion-detection systems ineffective. Patterns of attack contained within encrypted flows cannot be analyzed with simple statistical models. With the growing adoption of encryption, it will be a huge challenge to spot a malicious activity without breaching privacy. This problem must be resolved since unknown attacks might result in service interruption, information leaks, and intrusion of the system. In this work, the authors offer a proposal of an intrusion detection system designed on the basis of a Graph Neural Networks-temporal attention framework, which can analyze encrypted network flows without decryption. The network traffic is modelled in the form of a dynamical graph, modelling communication patterns and dynamics of relationship adjustments with time. Temporal attention puts the emphasis on the suspicious behavioural deviations and enhances the sequence-level inference. The new method of studying encrypted traffic is because the attack is modelled as a changing graph structure, which makes it possible to have a deeper understanding of the evolution of the attack. With the graph-reasoning and temporal-focus, the system can be able to provide greater accuracy, adaptation to new threats and privacy-sensitive analysis and is therefore applicable to real world enterprise setting.

title: Unified Transformer-Graph Framework for Cross-Platform Misinformation Detection and Knowledge Propagation Analysis
authors: Mohammed Ali Shaik, Abdul Rahim, E Ravithreyini, Shiva Mehta, Masrath Parveen, Afshan Fatima
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The massive growth of online social media networks has contributed greatly to misinformation propagation. The currently used misinformation detection systems are usually single platform and are based on textual semantics or network propagation patterns, which do not adequately cover the interactions at heterogeneous social media ecosystems. In order to overcome these issues, this paper presents a framework of Unified Transformer-Graph Framework (UTGF) which combines a contextual semantic learning task with a Transformer encoder and a relational reasoning task with a Graph Neural Network (GNN). The model semi-simultaneously learns textual data, user metadata, and propagation graphs on several benchmark datasets such as Twitter15/16, Pheme, PolitiFact, and GossipCop, which is a collection of about 100,000 labeled posts. The end-to-end training of the model is done with AdamW optimizer and dropout regularization and early stopping in order to pursue strong generalization. The experimental findings show that UTGF performs better with accuracy of 94.5, F1-score of 0.945, MAE of 0.087 and RMSE of 0.112 and low inference latency of about 25 ms per sample.

title: An Explainable YOLOv8 Framework with DCNv2-based Feature Adaptation for Accurate Recyclable and Household Waste Detection
authors: Shakil Shaikh, Vishal Aher, Swapnil Bangal, Digambar Rane, Vijay Aware, Sandip Udawant
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The rapid increase in household and recycled waste poses significant public safety and hazards to the environment, necessitating the use of modern and independent garbage management solutions. Effective and apparent garbage recognition is essential for modern smart waste management systems, particularly when working with recyclable and household deformable materials that have irregular shapes, occlusions, and different textures. The incapacity of traditional object recognition techniques to decipher such deformable objects limits their practical application. To address these challenges, the present research suggests an Explainable YOLOv8 approach improved with DCNv2-driven feature adaption for precise and comprehensible detection of household deformable garbage and recyclables in order to overcome these difficulties. The proposed design enhances the visual representation of deformed garbage objects and enables adaptive spatial filtering by integrating DCNv2-C2f blocks into the YOLOv8 backbone. While Explainable AI (XAI) techniques such as Grad-CAM and Eigen-CAM provide class-specific activation maps that graphically support model choices, the YOLOv8 detection head performs bounding box regression, objectness estimation, and class probability prediction. The proposed YOLOv8 + DCNv2 model outperforms YOLOv5 and other current waste recognition techniques, according to experimental data. With a high processing speed of 45 frames per second, the model achieves an overall accuracy of 97.81% and a mAP@0.5 of 96.72%, demonstrating its efficacy for precise and real-time waste detection.

title: The Evolution of AI-Powered Recommendation Agents: Taxonomies, Techniques, Applications, and Emerging Frontiers
authors: Dinesh Besiahgari, Naveen Kumar Ramakrishna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Recommender systems have become central to how users interact with digital platforms, yet the field continues to evolve rapidly. In this survey, we explore the development and classification of recommender systems, including collaborative filtering, content-based approaches, knowledge-based and hybrid methods, and more recent innovations such as context-aware and LLM-driven models. Our focus is on the underlying methodologies, ranging from classical matrix factorization to deep neural networks, that shape how these systems function in real-world scenarios.Drawing from examples in e-commerce, entertainment, and social platforms, where adoption rates are notably high (e.g., 90 Beyond surveying current techniques, we consider where the field is heading. Topics like explainability, multi-stakeholder fairness, cross-domain learning, and LLM integration are gaining attention, but many questions remain open. This paper aims to offer researchers, engineers, and product leaders both a grounded overview of current practices and a look at the critical areas driving future innovation in recommender systems.

title: Numerical Simulation of Transmission Tower Collapse Caused by Galloping of Iced Conductors
authors: Junbo Liu, Ji Wang, Zhaoqing Chen, He Liu, Hongda Dong, Jingyao Luan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: In this study, a tower-line system model was established in nonlinear finite-element software. Aerodynamic forces considering variations in wind attack angles were applied to simulate the galloping process of the tower-line system. The extreme stress and displacement responses of the tower at different locations before and after bolt loosening under galloping loads were compared. Finally, the collapse mechanism of the transmission tower caused by bolt loosening during ice-coated galloping was studied. Studies show that without considering bolt loosening, transmission towers can withstand galloping loads induced by ice accretion equal to the conductor’s diameter. After bolt loosening occurs, the internal forces inside the tower undergo redistribution, leading to a steep rise in the stress of neighboring components and a marked enlargement of the tower top displacement. The collapse process follows a chain-like path: single-point bolt, loosening, stress redistribution, aggravated load on adjacent joints, multi-point bolts loosening, structural system instability.

title: Stock Market Forecasting using an Enhanced Temporal Fusion Transformer
authors: A. Sathya Sofia, Poojasri S P, Ramya S, Prisca Carmel B
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Stock market prediction is one of the most important and needed data in the current era. The accuracy of stock market prediction is very complex because of its non linear nature. The models that are used in the previous stock market prediction research were LSTM(Long Short-Term Memory), SVM(Support Vector Machine) and TFT(Temporal Fusion Transformer). The LSTM capture sequential dependencies but it suffers with the long term relationships and integrating multiple external factors. The SVM model improves stock pair matching and feature extraction but it suffers from computational inefficiencies and sensitivity to market shifts. The TFT enhances the advanced approach by providing deep learning mechanisms and multi-variable feature integration, but it has drawbacks like high computational cost, interpretability limitations, and overfitting risks. To overcome these drawbacks, we proposed an Updated TFT model which optimizes prediction accuracy, efficiency, and robustness through model pruning, transfer learning, SHAP-based interpretability, and automated hyper parameter tuning. By using this updated TFT model, the historical and real-time stock data can be handled effectively. It offers higher scalability, reduced overfitting, and better transparency, which provides the valuable tool for traders, investors, and financial analysts. Hence the updated TFT model delivers more accurate, efficient, and interpretable solution for real-world financial decision-making.

title: Eco-Friendly Printed Energy Harvesters Based on Quartz-Gum Arabic Composites for Wearable Selfpowered Sensors
authors: W.L.P.K. Wijesinghe, D. L. Weerawarne, G. C. Wickramasinghe, R.M. Manamendra
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Advancements in printed electronics have enabled scalable fabrication methods for wearable devices. With the rising demand for continuous physiological monitoring in diagnostic and therapeutic applications, there is a significant need for biocompatible, self-powered energy harvesters. While traditional piezoelectric materials often contain toxic elements like Lead (Pb), Barium (Ba), and Lithium (Li), this study explores eco-friendly alternatives. We developed a flexible, biocompatible piezoelectric material using quartz and Gum Arabic (GA). Unlike the commonly used PDMS matrix, Gum Arabic enhances flexibility and supports various printing techniques, including doctor blading, spray coating, and extrusion printing. In this study, three piezoelectric energy harvesters (PEHs) were fabricated with varying quartz compositions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 5 g}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 g}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 g}$</tex>) mixed with a consistent 1 g of Gum Arabic. The PEHs were integrated into a fingertip glove to measure open-circuit voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{V}_{\mathbf{o c}}$</tex>), short-circuit current (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I}_{\mathbf{s c}}$</tex>), and power output across resistive loads during finger-tapping exercises. Results indicated that the 2g-quartz PEH achieved the highest Voc and Isc of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4519 \pm 0.0361 ~\mathrm{V}, 23.0 \pm 5.54 \times 10^{-9} A$</tex>, respectively. However, the 1 g -quartz PEH produced the highest overall power output of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.37 \times 10^{-3} W / m^{2}$</tex>. These results confirm that the quartz/Gum Arabic composite is a viable, better-balanced material for printed, biocompatible energy harvesting in wearable technology.

title: Real-Time Fake Profile Detection using Behavioral Biometrics
authors: T.Sangeetha, N. Deepak, S. Dhanapal, A. Kishore Aravind
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Beyond single identifying factors, the system compares a wide variety of profile features to match user profiles and detect fraudulent accounts. provides for several similarity measures and attribute priority, which improves the accuracy of fake account detection and profile matching. Profile photos, lengths of full names and descriptions, numerical ratios in usernames and full names, external URLs, privacy settings, and social activity metrics (posts, followers, and following) are some of the features that use Logistic Regression and Exploratory Data Analysis (LEDA) algorithms to match and identify user profiles and distinguish between genuine and fraudulent accounts. The framework proposes a feasible solution for intra-social network operations such as data integration, enrichment, information retrieval, and false account detection. The experimental results indicate that the framework performs better than current solutions.

title: Emotion Classification NLP using CNN-Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory Network
authors: Paradesi Mary Swetha, Bikkili Alekya Himabindu, Munni Giri Mahendrappa, Kapuram Lokeshwar Reddy, Shaik Khaleel Basha, Nandyala Divya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The application of automated and reliable techniques of recognizing the emotional state of a human being has since become relevant to such research issues such as the mood of the consumer and the mental health determination. The current methods have abject shortcomings which are in the nature of inability to establish long term contextual correlations and other minor scales patterns of text data words. To address these flaws, the paper will recommend a hybrid deep learning network; e.g. one dimensional convolutional neural network and bidirectional long short-term memory. The forward and backward information is processed by the bidirectional recurrent and the convolutional segment respectively to give the convolutional n- gram information and the local semantic information respectively. It is grounded on the whole network on dropout effects perspective and self-attention based pooling as the operations of both increasing the over-generalization of the performance and preventing the overfitting tendency. According to experimental evaluation, the suggested framework significantly outperforms single-model baselines, such as independent LSTM and CNN configurations, with a classification accuracy of 92.1% and a macro-F1 score of 0.91. Besides being precise, the model is computationally efficient and this fact makes it suitable to apply in the real time.

title: SmartAlert: A Data-Driven Cloud-Native Framework for Intelligent Alert Management in Cloud Operations
authors: Abinaya Mettupatti Sivagnanam, Gayathri Surianarayanan, Srikanth Singireddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Alert fatigue represents a critical challenge in enterprise cloud monitoring systems, where excessive notifications desensitize operators to genuine issues. This paper presents an intelligent alert management framework that reduces alert volume by 73% while improving incident detection by 18%. We implement a multi-tier alerting architecture with dynamic threshold adjustment, contextual suppression rules, intelligent aggregation, and role-based notification routing. The system employs severity scoring algorithms considering resource utilization trends, business impact, time-to-exhaustion, and historical patterns. Email template optimization with actionable recommendations increases alert response rates from 34% to 87%. Evaluation across healthcare and telecommunications organizations over 18 months demonstrates the framework mitigates alert fatigue (reducing daily alerts from 12.3 to 3.4), maintains 99% detection of critical issues, and decreases mean-time-to-response by 64%. Implementation details and lessons learned from production deployment provide practical guidance for building effective cloud monitoring systems.

title: Rakt Setu - An Integrated Mobile Platform for Expediting Blood Donation and Request Management
authors: Ayush Srivastava, Shaurya Jain, Prapti Sharma, Shriyansh Srivastava, Thammali Gangadhar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The critical shortage of timely blood supply in emergency medical situations is a significant global healthcare challenge. This challenge becomes more serious due to poor communication, logistical difficulties, and the absence of real-time coordination between donors and seekers. The primary objective of this research is to present a high-performance, integrated web application – Rakt Setu, that automates the end-to-end blood request management process through real-time geospatial coordination. Rakt Setu's architecture uses MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React, Node.js) stack to guarantee a full-JavaScript, scalable, and maintainable codebase. The website has a dual-role architecture in which seekers create urgent, location-based requests to find suitable donors or blood banks. The smart geospatial matching tool, built into the platform and reliant on the MongoDB in-built geospatial query tool to alert nearby compatible donors immediately, is one of the innovations. While the Node.js backend is used to handle real-time APIs, the frontend is a single-page application (SPA) that is built on React. The entire application is bundled together with Docker, which provides a consistent environment of development and deployment. When one accepts a request, a secure, two-way, exchange of contact information is made, which guarantees direct communication. The application also connects to the Google Maps API to allow travelling and cooperates with certified hospitals to offer safe and authorized spots of donation. The long-term goal of this end-to-end workflow is to significantly lower the response time in emergencies and enhance donor participation as well as overall effectiveness of blood supply chain between request origination and assisted donation.

title: MAGNET: Multi-Scale Attention Graph Network Framework for IoMT Threat Detection
authors: Uthayanathan Priyatharsan, Achintya Singhal, Subbiah Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The rapid growth of devices on the Internet of Medical Things has created life-critical healthcare networks that are increasingly being targeted by sophisticated cyberattacks. Existing intrusion detection systems fail to adequately address two fundamental challenges: modeling the relational topology among heterogeneous medical devices and capturing attack dynamics that span multiple temporal scales. This paper proposes MAGNET, a Multi-Scale Attention Graph Network Framework that addresses these gaps by integrating three synergistic components: a GATv2 encoder for adaptive asymmetric topology-aware message passing, a Multi-Scale Dilated Gated Convolution layer for parallelizable multi-resolution temporal aggregation with a 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</sup>-step receptive field in only $\mathcal{O}\left( R \right)$ layers, and Robust Power Normalization (RPNorm) to stabilize training under heavy-tailed activation distributions induced by diverse attack traffic. Evaluated on CICIoMT2024 and TON-IoT datasets, MAGNET achieves 99.91% and 95.00% accuracy with false positive rates of only 0.4% and 2.0%, respectively, outperforming all compared state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies confirm the independent contribution of each component. Inductive evaluations demonstrate strong generalization to previously unseen device types. These findings highlight the feasibility of deploying MAGNET in real-world clinical IoMT networks for proactive cyber-threat detection.

title: Real-Time Intrusion Detection in IIoT/OT Networks Via Traffic-Feature Forecasting: A Testbed Study
authors: Mhamad Bakro, Andrea Marotta, Walter Tiberti, Ottorino Odoardi, Ivano Salvatore, Piergiuseppe Di Marco
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) and operational technology (OT) networks are increasingly integrated with physical processes, enabling real-time sensing and actuation while widening the cyber-physical attack surface. Enterprise intrusion detection systems (IDS) often rely on payload inspection, signatures, or high-dimensional engineered features that are difficult to deploy in OT and may yield high false-positive rates while missing some anomalies. This work presents a lightweight IDS for IIoT/OT environments that relies only on passive, per-second traffic telemetry and residual-based forecasting. From mirrored traffic, we extract low-cost statistical features, forming a dataset representation without payload parsing. We emulate denial-ofservice (DoS) flooding and injection/replay manipulations, and use simple rule-based thresholds to label traffic and construct a supervised benchmark dataset. We evaluate two forecastingbased detectors: (i) a supervised Random Forest (RF) forecaster trained on rule-labeled data, and (ii) a one-class Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) forecaster trained exclusively on normal traffic (label-free). Real-world experiments on a prototype PLC-to-motor-drive control loop with an external attack platform show that an LSTM-based, residual-driven temporal forecasting approach enables a robust and scalable IDS suitable for timingconstrained IIoT/OT deployments, achieving 100% detection with zero false alarms in the evaluated scenarios.

title: AI-Powered Liver Disease Prediction Using Random Forest and Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN)
authors: Shivam Kumar, Arjun Kumar Yadav, P.J.Beslin Pajila
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: In this project, we threw together a machine learning app that will enter in normal blood test results and immediately provide a risk prediction of liver disease with a confidence score. The platform picks up the standard clinical data of regular check-ups and processes them through machine-learning algorithms to determine whether an individual is at risk of liver disease.We tested five classifiers: a baseline of logistic regression, a tree ensemble of random forest, XGBoost boosting, a small neural network, and a Kolmogorov -Arnold Network that is easier to interpret.We tested it on more than 1,500 patient records of Indian Liver Dataset and other Indian medical repositories. Data cleaning was done to address missing values. Characteristics were age, sex, total bilirubin, direct bilirubin, alkaline phosphatase, ALT, AST, total protein, albumin, and the albumin -globulin ratio.Among the rest of them, random forest achieved the highest accuracy, exceeding 90%. The Kolmogorov-Arnold Network was cool in that it presents individual transformation curves of individual features.To create the final product, we created a web app using Fastapi as the back-end and React and Material-UI as the front-end, hence it is easy to enter data and user-friendly.

title: Context Graph: Conversational State Tracking for Knowledge Graph Construction
authors: Shah Manan Vinod, Dulan Dissanayake, Randeep Wilkhu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The volume of new medical research is growing too fast for humans to read and organize manually. To solve this, researchers use Knowledge Graphs (KGs) to turn text into structured data (connecting drugs, diseases, and proteins). However, existing computer programs struggle to read complex medical text because they often process one sentence at a time, forgetting the context. The primary objective of this study is to introduce Context Graph, a novel Conversational State Tracking system that resolves implicit entity references by treating the reading process as a stateful conversation with a Large Language Model (GPT-4o). To ensure data fidelity, we enforce strict structural validation via Pydantic and standardize entities using the Unified Medical Language System (UMLS). Key findings demonstrate that Context Graph achieves a state-of-the-art accuracy of 74.92% on the MINE benchmark, significantly outperforming the previous best method (KGGen at 66.07%). Furthermore, a real-world pilot study on 378 drugs from DrugBank yielded over 130,000 structured relationships, proving the system’s viability for high-density, context-heavy biomedical text extraction.

title: A Universal Harmonic Discriminator for High-quality GAN-based Vocoder
authors: Nan Xu, Zhaolong Huang, Xiao Zeng
publish: 2025 IEEE Automatic Speech Recognition and Understanding Workshop (ASRU)
abstract: With the emergence of GAN-based vocoders, the discriminator, as a crucial component, has been developed recently. In our work, we focus on improving the time-frequency based discriminator. Particularly, Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) representation is usually used as input of time-frequency based discriminator. However, the STFT spectrogram has the same frequency resolution at different frequency bins, which results in an inferior performance, especially for singing voices. Motivated by this, we propose a universal harmonic discriminator for dynamic frequency resolution modeling and harmonic tracking. Specifically, we design a harmonic filter with learnable triangular band-pass filter banks, where each frequency bin has a flexible bandwidth. Additionally, we add a half-harmonic to capture fine-grained harmonic relationships at low-frequency band. Experiments on speech and singing datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed discriminator on both subjective and objective metrics.

title: AI-Enhanced Career Guidance System for Personalized Career Pathways
authors: Ramchandra. H. V, Pragathi M, Srishti P Desai, T Mythri, Chittiboina Sai Niharika Yadav, Gagana V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) idea is changing education and the work sphere, and thus the career guidance system needs to be developed on a high level. The traditional methods of counseling that can be subjective and have a limited scope are incapable of fitting the range of available academic, behavioral, and labor-market data. We conduct studies on the AI-supported methods of career advice, such as rule-based, machine learning models, and deep learning architectures as well as hybrid ML-NLP frameworks. These systems are based on structured and unstructured data in production of personalized recommendations. Nevertheless, there are still limitations which include limitations in interpretability, data set(s) variety, semantic reasoning, and adaptive feedback systems. The emerging career guidance systems are to have multimodal inputs, semantics as well modeling and the application of iterative learning to suit the changes in the skill requirements.

title: FPGA-Based Hardware Accelerator for Number Theoretic Transform in Post Quantum Cryptography using Nexys-A7
authors: Saravanaperumal R, Nirmal Kumar P, Deepa Jose
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Number Theoretic Transform is an essential element of the CRYSTALS-Kyber algorithm and helps with polynomial multiplication to support post-quantum security. Nevertheless, the hardware realization of the algorithm is resource heavy due to the presence of complex arithmetic logic and inefficient data processing. This paper aims to design and implement an efficient NTT accelerator using an FPGA that overcomes the aforementioned issues with the help of an optimized algorithm designed to support modular multiplication and reduction. This work is accomplished using the Xilinx Artix-7 component. A Finite State Machine is utilized to process the data. An important novel feature in the design is the inclusion of a dual butterfly unit, parallel processing configuration, utilized to perform NTT calculations with odd and even data paths simultaneously, for 256-point NTT calculations. In addition, through the application of the proposed modular reduction method, instead of standard arithmetic logic, and the dual butterfly processing configuration, the design reduces hardware complexity. Simulation results for 16-point, 128-point, and 256-point NTT modules validate those results, proving that it offers considerable improvements in Look-Up Table and Slice usage, while maintaining precise functionality, over existing FPGA-based implementations. These results validate its relevance in post-quantum cryptographic applications.

title: AIoU: An Adaptive Bounding Box Loss with an Edge Alignment Penalty
authors: Wenhui Chen, Ziyao Lin, Xinyu Jiang, Chi Man Vong
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Intersection-over-Union (IoU)-based losses, such as IoU and CIoU, have become the de facto standard for bounding box regression in modern object detectors. However, these losses mainly emphasize center distance and aspect ratio consistency, while providing limited supervision on explicit boundary alignment, which is particularly critical for small, dense, and overlapping objects. In this paper, we propose AIoU, an enhanced IoU-based loss that introduces an explicit edge alignment penalty together with a dynamic weighting mechanism. By directly penalizing boundary misalignment and adaptively balancing different geometric factors during training, AIoU provides more stable and accurate localization supervision. AIoU is simple to integrate into existing detection frameworks and introduces no additional inference overhead. Extensive experiments on COCO and VisDrone benchmarks across multiple YOLO variants demonstrate consistent performance improvements, validating the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method.

title: Evaluation of Category-Agnostic Image-to-3D Models for Eyeglass Frame Reconstruction
authors: Ervinas Gisleris, Artūras Serackis, Dalius Matuzevičius
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: Recent advances in category-agnostic image-to-3D generation have enabled reconstruction of textured 3D models from a single RGB image across diverse object categories. However, specialized product domains such as eyewear present structural challenges due to thin elements, symmetry, transparency, and reflective materials. This study evaluates seven recent image-to-3D pipelines for the reconstruction of eyeglass frames from monocular RGB images. A test set consisting of fourteen images representing seven distinct frame designs was prepared. Each model was provided with identical input images and required to reconstruct textured 3D objects. The evaluation was qualitative and was based on a structured visual inspection of geometric plausibility, thin-structure reconstruction, lens handling, and texture fidelity. The results indicate that, while overall reconstruction quality has improved, consistent modeling of thin temples and accurate handling of absent or transparent lenses remain a challenge. No single model consistently outperformed others across all samples. The findings highlight both the progress and the current limitations of category-agnostic 3D generation systems when applied to eyewear reconstruction.

title: Design of FIR-Enhanced Convolutional Neural Networks through Hardware-Aware NAS with Hybrid Vedic Multipliers
authors: P.Esther Jebarani, A.Amir Anton Jone, M.Preethi, Ben.M.Jebin, Kummaramsetty Sainath
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: The present research proposes a hardware-aware neural architecture search (HW-NAS) model of FIR-enhanced convolutional neural networks (CNNs with hybrid Vedic multipliers. The suggested solution solves CNN topology and arithmetic hardware simultaneously by using latency, power and area constraints as a part of the search space, allowing it to be effectively deployed on resource-constrained hardware systems. It is a hybrid Vedic multiplier architecture based on Urdhva Tiryagbhyam, Nikhilam Sutra and Karatsuba algorithms performed on the FIR preprocessing and CNN convolution layers in order to speed up multiply accumulate. The HW-NAS mechanism chooses the best filter lengths, kernel sizes and multiplier settings in order to reduce the power-delay product without impairing the accuracy of classification. The use of biomedical EEG data has shown that experimental evaluation with reduced delay and energy consumption needs and can be achieved with high diagnostic accuracy compared to more traditional CNN designs. The suggested architecture is highly applicable in low-power biomedical signal processing applications in real-time.

title: An Augmented Reality-Based Disaster Information Visualization System for Foreign Tourists in Japan: Design and Preliminary Validation
authors: Chanaken Gowit, Keisuke Utsu, Osamu Uchida
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: This study examines the difficulties foreign tourists face in understanding disaster-related information in Japan, which often limits effective situational awareness during emergencies. To address this issue, a mobile disaster information system using Augmented Reality (AR) is proposed to transform abstract and text-based disaster data into intuitive visual representations in real-world environments. The study adopts a design-based research approach, focusing on conceptual system design and initial prototype evaluation. Technical feasibility was assessed through simulated API testing to examine the retrieval and processing of disaster data in JSON format. In addition, the system's user interface and user experience (UI/UX) were evaluated by four experts in relevant fields based on usability principles. The findings suggest that the proposed system architecture is technically feasible and that the AR-based interface has potential for practical application. Future work will involve full implementation and usability testing with foreign tourists to evaluate real-world effectiveness.
