title: Advance IoT in the Era of Artificial Intelligence and Edge Computing: A Comprehensive Literature Review and Comparative Study
authors: Sharra Mae B. Fernandez
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The swift expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) has exposed limitations in conventional cloud-centric systems, especially in latency, scalability, and data security. This research clarifies how integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and edge computing transforms IoT into intelligent, decentralized systems called the Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT). This research uses a comprehensive evaluation and comparison of peer-reviewed articles published between 2019 and 2025. The papers center on models for buildings, methods for implementing AI, and their uses. Consistent with previous research, the results point to a move toward continuum designs for the IoT-edge-cloud. These solutions provide distributed intelligence via TinyML at the device level, edge inference, and cloud training. Increased system resilience, reduced latency, and improved bandwidth efficiency are among the many benefits that industrial systems, smart agriculture, and healthcare can reap from the Internet of Things (IoT). There are still major obstacles in governance, security, interoperability, and thorough validation. To create future digital ecosystems with AIoT systems that are scalable, safe, and robust, this study synthesises and compares relevant literature to identify research requirements.

title: An Efficient Diffusion-based Approach for Text-to-Video Generation using LTX-Video
authors: B. Ashreetha, Gajula Vedhanth, Batthala Sai Uma, Jada Hemanth, Chedubavi Kalyan, N.Anil Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The research introduces video synthesis that is guided by language via diffusion models and takes temporal samples that are integrated to form a coherent clip that is semantically aligned with the natural language prompts. Once text processing is completed, the embeddings are employed to construct a context video utilizing a diffusion model incorporated within the ComfyUI pipeline, executed using PyTorch 2.6.0. Video streams are created with a rate of 768 × 512 pixels in 49 frames per clip with 24 frames in 1 second (fps). The latent representations are decoded to RGB frames with the help of a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and post-processed and exported to video with FFmpeg/imageio. The framework is also evaluated using standard video generation metrics, namely FVD and CLIPScore, to evaluate the quality of perception and semantic predictability. It is experimentally executed on a Google Colab environment using NVIDIA A100 (24GB VRAM+) or more. The suggested implementation offers a modular and reproducible foundation of research based on the diffusion-based video generation, and its applications can be found in creative media, education, simulation, and AI-assisted content creation.

title: Deep Learning-based Prominent Feature Identification for Advancing Gait Recognition
authors: Sachin Bhimraj Mandlik, Rekha Punjaji Labade, Sachin Vasant Chaudhari, Balasaheb Shrirangrao Agarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Gait recognition has emerged as a powerful biometric technique thanks to its capability to identify individuals from afar, eliminating the need for physical interaction or high-resolution imagery. However, the performance of gait recognition models largely depends on the quality and discriminative power of the features extracted from gait patterns. This paper presents a comprehensive study aimed at identifying the prominent features that most substantially enhance accurate gait recognition. Both traditional handcrafted features and modern deep-learning-based representations are examined across appearance-based, model-based, and spatio-temporal approaches. Through a systematic review and comparative analysis of state-of-the-art methods, this work highlights key gait attributes such as silhouette shape cues, joint–angle trajectories, limb motion dynamics, periodicity of gait cycles, and deep spatio-temporal embeddings. The findings reveal that robust gait recognition is typically achieved by combining multi-level features—capturing both structural and temporal characteristics—while ensuring invariance to covariates such as view angle, clothing, and walking speed. This study provides a consolidated understanding of the most effective feature categories and offers insights for developing next-generation gait recognition systems with improved accuracy and robustness.

title: Impact of RGB-D Camera Selection on Human Pose Estimation Accuracy and Temporal Stability
authors: Emilijus Pocius, Laura Venckutė, Kristina Skrebutenas, Rytis Maskeliūnas, Vytautas Abromavičius
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: Human pose estimation (HPE) plays a crucial role in numerous fields, including healthcare, sports analysis, and human-computer interaction. While deep learning-based algorithms like MediaPipe, YOLO, and MoveNet have made real-time pose tracking increasingly accessible, their performance is often constrained by the quality and capabilities of the input hardware. This study presents a comparative evaluation of three Orbbec RGB-D cameras: Gemini 2, Astra 2, and Femto Mega—in combination with state-of-the-art pose estimation algorithms. We assess each camera’s effectiveness in terms of keypoint localization accuracy, temporal stability, and overall suitability for pose estimation tasks under controlled indoor conditions. Our evaluation reveals that the Gemini 2 camera consistently outperformed other models when paired with MediaPipe and YOLOv11 architectures, while MoveNet variants demonstrated poor performance across all camera configurations. RGB imaging proved superior to monochrome for lower body tracking, with approximately 40% lower errors. Our findings aim to guide researchers and developers in selecting the most suitable camera-algorithm combination for HPE applications, striking a balance between performance and practical deployment considerations.

title: Automated Detection of Pleural Effusion in Chest X-Rays using EfficientNet-B0 and Multi-Channel Fusions
authors: K. Kavitha, Hari Kishan Reddy K, Sai Sankar Reddy Challa, Sneha Priya K, R Gayathri, Dileep Ruthin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Pleural effusion is a clinically significant condition commonly identified in chest radiographs and is associated with diseases such as heart failure, infection, and malignancy. Early and accurate detection is essential for effective clinical management; however, automated identification remains challenging due to anatomical variability and localized manifestations in the lower lung regions. Although deep learning methods have demonstrated promising performance in thoracic disease detection, many existing approaches analyze entire radiographs without incorporating anatomical guidance, which may reduce detection reliability. In this study, we propose a Lung-aware deep learning framework (LADL) for automated pleural effusion detection using frontal chest radiographs from the CheXpert dataset. The LADL framework integrates lung field segmentation with an EfficientNet-based convolutional neural network to focus analysis on anatomically relevant regions. To enhance model interpretability, Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) is used to visualize attention in clinically meaningful lung zones. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a classification accuracy of 93.85%, indicating reliable detection performance and improved robustness compared to conventional whole-image approaches. The proposed framework highlights the potential of anatomy-guided deep learning for supporting automated pleural effusion detection in clinical practice.

title: Photovoltaic-Charging Pile Coordinated Scheduling Method Based on Power Balance
authors: Jianfei Shi, Kexi Li, Daoxiang Meng, Jianbing Yao, Hao Jiang, Kaihao Bai
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: The intermittency of photovoltaic (PV) output and the randomness of electric vehicle (EV) charging load are prone to cause power imbalance in the integrated PV-storage-charging system. To address this, a PV-charging pile collaborative scheduling method based on power balance is proposed to achieve multi-objective optimization operation of the system. A PV output model considering time-series coupling characteristics and power balance constraints is constructed. A weighted multi-objective comprehensive optimization model is established with system operation cost, grid interactive power fluctuation, and PV absorption rate as objectives. An improved multi-objective particle swarm optimization (IMOPSO) algorithm is designed, which enhances global search capability through adaptive inertia weight and mutation operations, and optimizes scheduling weights to adapt to differentiated scheduling strategies. Experimental results show that the maximum fluctuation of grid interactive power is only 65 kW, and the longest response time of the charging pile is only 1.0 ms. This method can mitigate the impact of source-load fluctuations, providing technical support for the efficient operation of distributed PV -storage-charging systems and PV absorption.

title: Design of a Multiple Piezoelectric Energy Management Circuit Based on MPPT and SSHI
authors: Yunyang Wei, Weijie Dong
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: To address application scenarios involving the coordinated operation of multiple piezoelectric energy harvesters (PEHs), this paper proposes an extensible synchronous switching harvesting on inductor (ESSHI) circuit that integrates parallel synchronized switch harvesting on inductor (P-SSHI) and series synchronized switch harvesting on inductor (S-SSHI) architectures. By exploiting the switching characteristics of diodes and transistors, the proposed circuit enables coordinated operation among multiple PEHs. Its effectiveness is validated through both simulation and experimental results. To maximize power transfer from the piezoelectric energy harvesters (PEHs) to the load via the ESSHI circuit, a control strategy based on fractional cumulative peak voltage (FCPV) is introduced. This approach enables maximum power point tracking (MPPT) in a topology where multiple PEHs jointly charge a single energy storage capacitor. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a peak MPPT efficiency of up to 96.14%. The proposed MPPT-ESSHI approach provides an effective and scalable solution for the coordinated operation of multiple PEHs and demonstrates strong potential for high-efficiency self-powered sensing systems.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Approach for Maize Leaf Disease Classification using CNNs and Vision Transformers
authors: Ganesh B. Kote, Purushottam R. Patil, Pawan R. Bhaladhare
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Detecting and identifying plant diseases accurately and in time is significant to enhance crop production and food security. Maize is among the other cultivated cereal crops in the world and is prone to various leaf diseases, which seriously lower productivity unless they are detected at an early stage. Over the last several years, deep learning-based methods have shown promising results in automated classification of plant diseases; however, single-model methods in most cases cannot adequately cope with the issue of class imbalance and lack of generalization. This paper suggests an ensemble deep learning model, which can include several convolutional neural networks (CNN) and a Vision Transformer to classify maize leaf disease effectively. The suggested ensemble model is based on ResNet50, DenseNet121, EfficientNet-B3 and Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16) through a weighted probability fusion approach. Here, a class-weighted loss function is used to eliminate dataset imbalance, and test-time augmentation (TTA) is used to increase the strength of prediction. A stratified split is used to run experiments on curated maize leaf disease dataset which has four classes: Blight, Common Rust, Gray Leaf Spot and Healthy leaves. The proposed ensemble model using CNN and Vision Transformer with TTA demonstrates a better performance in all evaluation indicators. It outperforms the existing techniques, with an accuracy of 98.10%, a precision of 97.83%, a recall of 97.22%, an F1-score of 97.50%, and a ROC-AUC of 99.76%. This achievement has contributed to the performance benefits through an effective combination of complementary local and global feature representation and an improved decision-level ensemble method. The architecture lowers the bias of individual models and enhances their ability to identify the difference between the various types of maize leaf diseases. This results in an effective and reliable system of maize leaf disease classification. Specifically, the ensemble has almost perfect ROC–AUC values within all the classes, whereas the minority Gray Leaf Spot category is significantly improved. Those findings show that the integration of convolutional model with transformer-based model can significantly improve discriminative ability and generalization ability in plant disease classification.

title: Inequality-Constrained Emotional–Style Decoupling Optimization for Automatic AI-based Electronic Keyboard Music Generation
authors: HaiYang Qu, DaHai Xing
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: To address the issues of high coupling between emotion and style, lack of physical performance constraints, and insufficient granularity of emotion control in current automatic music generation models, this paper proposes a groundbreaking Inequality-Constrained Emotion-Style Decoupling Optimization Framework (IC-ESDO) for automatic music generation on electronic keyboards. This framework achieves unified optimization among emotion expression, style control, and performance feasibility through the collaborative design of multimodal emotion perception, latent space decoupling modeling, and inequality constraint decoding. Methodologically, this study first constructs a multimodal emotion perception module. This module integrates EEG signals, textual emotion descriptions, and user historical preferences, learning continuous and differentiable emotion embeddings through a cross-modal attention mechanism. Subsequently, in the generative model based on OctupleMIDI extended representations, multi-channel posterior modeling of latent emotion, style, and content variables is introduced. Simultaneously, a dual decoupling mechanism is constructed using mutual information suppression terms and an adversarial decorrelation discriminator. Furthermore, the physical and logical constraints of electronic keyboard performance are formalized into a set of computable inequalities. The model embeds constraint filtering and renormalization operations at each generation step, ensuring that the generated results satisfy performance conditions such as finger spacing limits, polyphony constraints, pedal logic, and legato rules. On the experimental level, this paper constructs the E-StyleKeys-45K dataset, which contains 45,312 MIDI tracks and features multi-style and multi-emotion continuous annotations. It also conducts systematic comparisons with models such as Music Transformer, EmotionBox, Diff-BGM, Hyper-Curve Transformer, and Calliope. The results show that the proposed method reduces the Emotion Consistency Error (ECE) by approximately 34.2% compared to the best comparable model. Significant improvements are also achieved in Style Consistency (SCA) and Emotion-Style Decoupling Index (ESDI). In cross-style emotion generalization experiments, IC-ESDO maintains stable performance even with unseen emotion-style combinations. Constraint Satisfaction Rate (CSR) experiments show that the proposed method achieves over 95% across all constraint dimensions.

title: Research on Electric Vehicle Charging and Discharging Scheduling Optimization and Power Grid Scheduling Based on NSGA-III Algorithm
authors: Xiaomin Cao, Hong Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: With the rapid global proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs), their impact on the power grid is becoming increasingly significant. To address the multi-objective optimization problem balancing grid load fluctuations, electricity operating costs, and vehicle owner benefits arising from high EV penetration, this paper proposes an EV charging and discharging scheduling optimization method based on an improved NSGA-III algorithm. This method constructs a multiobjective optimization model, incorporating EV travel behavior, load demand, and electricity price fluctuations to optimize grid load balancing, electricity purchase cost control, and vehicle owner economic benefits. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed NSGA-III method excels in grid load balancing and cost control. Compared to MOPSO and NSGAII, NSGA-III significantly improves grid scheduling efficiency, reduces electricity purchase costs, and enhances vehicle owner economic benefits. Analysis of hypervolume and diversity indices verifies the method’s fast convergence and solution set diversity, indicating that NSGA-III is an efficient algorithm suitable for EV scheduling optimization. This research provides an effective optimization scheme for power grid scheduling systems in the context of high EV penetration.

title: Hybrid Residual-Attention Framework for Interpretable Multi-Feature RGB Malware Classification
authors: L. Selvam, A. Sangeetha, R. S. Achsah, G. Vinoth Rajkumar, J. Relin Francis Raj, P. Bharathi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Malware detection faces escalating difficulty due to the rapid evolution of polymorphic, metamorphic, and packed variants that evade conventional signature-based mechanisms. Visualization-based approaches have attempted to improve robustness by transforming executable binaries into grayscale images and applying convolutional neural networks for classification. However, single-channel grayscale representations discard essential structural and entropy-related characteristics embedded within Portable Executable files. High-entropy regions generated by packing or encryption appear as indistinguishable noise, reducing classification reliability. Additionally, many learning-based systems operate as opaque models, producing predictions without interpretable evidence, which limits operational adoption in security-critical environments. An interpretable multi-feature RGB malware classification framework is introduced to address these limitations. Executable binaries are encoded into three complementary channels: raw byte intensity, block-wise Shannon entropy, and Portable Executable section structure. This representation preserves low-level byte distribution, randomness patterns, and structural boundaries within a unified spatial format. The resulting RGB images are processed through a hybrid residual learning architecture augmented with a spatial-channel attention module to emphasize discriminative regions. Gradient-weighted class activation mapping generates heatmaps highlighting influential areas, which are subsequently mapped back to original executable sections such as .text, .data, and .rsrc. The integrated design enhances robustness against obfuscation while enabling section-level attribution of malicious predictions. Improved information retention, structural awareness, and transparent decision visualization collectively establish a reliable and interpretable malware classification pipeline suitable for advanced cybersecurity analysis.

title: DenseNet121-based Intelligent Recovery Prediction and Personalized Rehabilitation Framework for Upper Limb Fractures
authors: N. Pandeeswari, P. Sunil Kumar, M. Sivasubramaniyan, P. Sundar Pandi, V. Tarun Balaji
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Upper limb fractures affecting the humerus, shoulder, elbow, forearm, wrist, hand, and fingers constitute a significant portion of orthopedic injuries worldwide. In current clinical scenarios, fracture management primarily focuses on diagnosis and stabilization, while structured rehabilitation guidance often remains limited. Shortage of physiotherapy resources, inconsistent follow-up monitoring, and absence of individualized recovery planning tools create significant gaps in patient care. Generalized exercise instructions and lack of personalized nutritional guidance may lead to improper rehabilitation practices, delayed healing, joint stiffness, and functional limitations. Furthermore, recovery duration is commonly estimated using broad assumptions rather than data-driven analysis tailored to individual health conditions. To address these challenges, an intelligent recovery prediction and personalized care system is proposed. The framework integrates deep learning-based fracture severity classification from upper limb X-ray images with machine learning-driven recovery duration estimation using patient-specific attributes including age, weight, calorie intake, fitness level, injury type, and gender. Predicted severity and recovery timelines are utilized to automatically generate structured day-wise diet plans and progressive physiotherapy schedules designed to support safe and effective bone healing. The system unifies fracture analysis, recovery forecasting, nutritional planning, and rehabilitation scheduling within a web-based platform, enabling structured documentation and guided monitoring. By providing objective severity assessment, individualized recovery estimation, and stage-wise rehabilitation support, the proposed framework enhances clinical consistency, promotes adherence to medically appropriate routines, and improves overall recovery outcomes.

title: Optimally Coordinated Planning of Combined Cooling, Heating and Power System for Microgrid
authors: Wei Wang, Yuan Zhu, Aibibule Saitar, Enju Zhao, Jihao Sun, Yuli Qi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: An optimally coordinated planning method with economic objectives is proposed for combined cooling, heating and power (CCHP) equipment to enhance the overall economic performance of the system. The approach is applied to the planning of CCHP microgrids that include wind power and photovoltaics. First, a comprehensive optimization planning mathematical model is established considering wind power, photovoltaic systems, CCHP equipment and heat storage devices, where the objective function is to minimize the cost of typical days. Second, aiming at the established mixed-integer linear programming model, the CPLEX solver is adopted to solve the configuration scheme of cooling, heating and power equipment under both grid-connected and off-grid scenarios. Finally, the results of numerical examples indicate that the proposed model can effectively reduce the total cost of the CCHP-based microgrid, improve the energy efficiency, and efficiently prove the advanced nature of the multi-energy cooperative optimization system, which has certain guiding significance for the planning of multi-energy microgrid.

title: Quantifying Cognitive Divergence Between Humans and Artificial Intelligence with Unified Markov-Chain Framework
authors: Vincent Gnanaraj T, Nandini M, Nhesika S G, Nivedha A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This study compares human and artificial intelligence (AI) cognition using a unified seven state model consisting of Information Processing, Learning, Memory, Pattern Recognition, Reasoning, Adaptation and Goal Optimization. Human cognition is flexible and exploratory, whereas AI behaves with stable, optimization driven consistency. A synthetic 100×7 dataset was used to construct correlation-based transition matrices, and a Markov-chain simulation of 300 human and 300 AI agents over 300 steps captured dynamic features such as state occupancy, dwell time, and switching behavior. Results showed a major divergence in which humans exhibited high cognitive switching (0.446) compared with AI (0.120) while AI concentrated strongly in the Reasoning state (0.385). Goal Optimization differences were statistically significant but small (Human = 0.185956, AI = 0.177548; t = 3.3943, p = 0.0007247). The findings quantify exploratory human cognition versus persistent AI processing, providing a practical framework for understanding cognitive alignment, improving adaptive AI design and strengthening human vs AI collaborative systems.

title: Knowledge-Driven Service Quality Management: A Paradigm Innovation for Decision Making Integrating Generative AI and Causal Inference
authors: Wu Zhunan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: To address the high-dimensional sparsity and causal uncertainty challenges in quality management within highly networked and spatiotemporally coupled service systems, this paper proposes a knowledge-driven service quality management framework (KDSQM) that integrates generative modeling and causal inference mechanisms. First, this model constructs a structured causal generative model for intermittent service processes. This model embeds latent variables of user behavior, service state, and spatiotemporal environmental factors into a unified structural equation system. This operation enables a unified probabilistic representation of the QoS tensor. Second, this study designs a hierarchical variational inference-driven multimodal data fusion mechanism. This mechanism achieves joint estimation of textual, numerical, and spatially labeled data by constructing hierarchical ELBO lower bounds. Based on this, a spatial causal propagation kernel function and a temporal modulation function are introduced. This function extends the classical CP tensor decomposition causally and constructs a generative counterfactual inference framework. Simultaneously, a gradient-aware hybrid negative sampling strategy is proposed. Experimental results show that compared with the CP decomposition model, the proposed method reduces RMSE by 20.9% and improves NDCG@10 by 17.2% in the D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> scenario. In the highly sparse D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> scenario, the RMSE reduction expands to 24.3%. Spatial causal kernel ablation experiments show that the counterfactual prediction error (CF-Error) is reduced by 27.4%. Furthermore, the model remains stable when the spatial decay coefficient β ∈ [0.8,1.2]. In generative counterfactual evaluation, CF-Error decreases to 0.082, SCM is 0.121, and VAE is 0.138. Causal intervention gain (CIG) increases to 8.9%. The gradient-aware negative sampling strategy improves NDCG@10 from 0.782 to 0.823, Hit@5 from 0.734 to 0.781, and reduces the number of convergence rounds from 41 to 29.

title: Observability-Driven DevOps for Distributed Systems Using Tracing and AI-Ops Based Anomaly Detection
authors: Nagarjuna Nellutla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Fintech platforms such as payment gateways, digital banks, and trading systems increasingly adopt micro services and cloud-native architectures, which makes traditional monitoring approaches insufficient for ensuring reliability and regulatory compliance. This paper examines how observability-driven DevOps, powered by distributed tracing and AIOps, can improve reliability and incident response in Fintech systems. We focus on three contributions. First, we characterize observability requirements for Fintech workloads and describe an end-to-end reference architecture using Open Telemetry for vendor-neutral distributed tracing, logging, and metrics collection. Second, we propose an AIOps-based anomaly detection pipeline that leverages trace- and metric-level features to detect performance and reliability anomalies in CI/CD stages and in production runtime. Third, we relate these techniques to relevant standards and best practices, including the NIST AI Risk Management Framework, SRE error budget concepts, and emerging IEEE/ISO guidance on trustworthy AI and operational resilience. We illustrate the approach with TikZ-based architecture diagrams and a conceptual Fintech case study, and we discuss challenges around data volume, sampling, model drift, and regulatory expectations. Our analysis suggests that combining Open Telemetry-based observability with AIOps-style analytics can transform DevOps in Fintech from reactive firefighting into proactive, evidence-driven operations.

title: Immersive Virtual Reality Flood Survival Training: Enhancing Survival Skills Through Simulation
authors: Kemachart Kemavuthanon, Osamu Uchida
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Thailand has faced rising flood risks, especially during 2024–2025, with severe events occurring in both northern and southern regions. This study addresses the lack of flood preparedness among Thai learners caused by limited disaster experience and reliance on lecture-based instruction. Japan's continuous disaster education and drills illustrate how experiential learning can build resilience. Based on these insights, this research presents a virtual reality (VR) flood survival simulation that guides learners through preparedness, monitoring, and survival stages in a gamified environment. Scenario tasks, branching decisions, and real-time feedback reinforce safe actions and assess changes in awareness and behavior. The system provides an immersive alternative to passive learning, suited to Thailand's context. Results suggest the potential of VR tools to improve flood-risk education and offer insights into curriculum integration and adaptive disaster simulation design.

title: Towards Consistent Management of Integrated 5G and TSN Networks
authors: Arne Neumann, Krithiga Ramesh, Dominik Welte, Manuel Schappacher, Axel Sikora
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: The integration of Fifth Generation Mobile Network (5G) and Time Sensitive Networking (TSN) is a key enabler for future industrial communication networks, combining deterministic Ethernet with dynamic wireless connectivity for use cases such as modular manufacturing. While standardization efforts lay the foundation, the actual realization of 5G as a logical TSN bridge requires consistent configuration models and Quality of Service (QoS) enforcement across both the wireless and the wired domains. In particular, challenges arise from the collection and generation of metrics that enable configuration, the transport of these metrics through the entire system, and the mapping of TSN parameters to 5G-specific resource allocation mechanisms. In addition, the architectural characterisitics and constraints within current tool chains provided for these network management purposes need to be considered. From a research standpoint, such integration is a challenge for the scalability and orchestration of hybrid networks in real-world deployments. Building on this analysis, the paper proposes a configuration concept leveraging standardized data models and existing solution modules. For the validation of the concept a dual testbed is presented which combines Centralized Network Controller (CNC)-based TSN configuration with Open5GS/ UERANSIM-based 5G simulation.

title: XAI-Driven Hybrid Deep Learning and XGBoost for High-Accuracy Cybersecurity Anomaly Detection
authors: N.Prakash, C. Viji, C.A. Kandasamy, A.Yoganathan, E.Nithyakala, P. Kalyanasundaram
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: In past years, cybersecurity attacks have increased so anomaly detection plays an important role in the research area. The normal traditional machine learning models failed to detect the complex attack patterns in large network traffic data. To solve this problem, this proposes an Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) driven hybrid anomaly detection model that combines Deep Neural Network (DNN) and XGBoost algorithms. In the proposed approach, the DNN model is used to extract the important features from network traffic data and the XGBoost classifier is used to classify the normal and malicious traffic. The proposed model was evaluated using a cybersecurity dataset containing 175,341 network traffic samples. In the experimental results, the hybrid model achieved an accuracy of approximately 98%, which is higher than the standalone neural network model that achieved 91.83% accuracy. The Proposed hybrid model also achieved an improved precision of 98.63%, recall of 99.52%, and F1-score of 98.58%. SPSS software is used to test the independent sample t-test where it is proved that the performance improvement is statistically significant (p < 0.001). The result finally shows that combining the Deep learning (DNN) feature extraction + XGBoost classification models improved the performance of cybersecurity anomaly detection.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Model for Helmet Detection in Real-World Scenarios
authors: Suman, Adithya G, Arvind G, G Raviteja, Goutham R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Motorcycle and workplace accidents frequently result in severe head injuries due to the absence of helmets. Automated helmet detection has emerged as a critical research area for enhancing safety compliance in traffic surveillance and industrial environments. Existing methods have utilized deep learning frameworks such as CNNs, YOLOv3–YOLOv8, and hybrid approaches combining convolutional networks with object detection architectures. In this paper, we propose an advanced helmet detection framework using YOLOv11, the latest evolution of the YOLO family. YOLOv11 integrates dynamic input scaling, transformer-enhanced backbone layers, and adaptive loss functions for high-precision detection in complex real-world scenarios. The proposed approach builds upon prior work in YOLOv8-based safety helmet detection, YOLO+CNN hybrid models, and real-time CNN-based helmet detection systems. Our experiments demonstrate that YOLOv11 achieves superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 99.3%, precision of 99.1%, and recall of 99.4% on benchmark datasets, surpassing existing models. This paper highlights the contridbutions of YOLOv11 toward practical, real-time, and scalable helmet detection systems.

title: Simultaneous Multi-Function Aggregation in Industrial Networks Oriented Multi-Group NOMA
authors: Yinfei Li, Hussein Abrahim, Weiyan Hou, Shijie Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: To address the massive connectivity and low-latency challenges in the Industrial Internet of Things, over-the-air computation has emerged as a promising technique for fast data aggregation. In this paper, a simultaneous multi-function aggregation scheme based on multi-group non-orthogonal multiple access is proposed. This architecture allows for the parallel computation of distinct functions from different sensor groups, effectively supporting multiple active production cells within the same time-frequency resource. Specifically, we formulate a joint optimization problem of power control and scaling factors to minimize the computation mean square error, subject to individual transmit power constraints and minimum data rate requirements for digital metadata transmission. Considering the practical industrial environment, the robustness of the system against imperfect channel state information is explicitly investigated. At the receiver, successive interference cancellation is employed to separate the superimposed signals from different groups. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme and analyze the trade-off between computation accuracy and the number of supported industrial groups, providing insights for deploying efficient aggregation in dense factory networks.

title: Hybrid XGBoost and FaceNet-based Framework for Secure Credit Card Fraud Detection with Biometric Verification
authors: K.Umamaheswari, A. Suganthana Rishi, P. Yuvaraja, S. Vishal Kumar, R. Sharvesh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Rapid expansion of digital payment systems has increased dependence on credit card transactions in online and offline financial activities. Large volumes of electronic transactions create significant security challenges due to rising cases of credit card fraud. Unauthorized access to card numbers and authentication details enables fraudulent transactions that cause financial losses and reduce trust in digital payment environments. Conventional security mechanisms based on static authentication procedures often fail to identify fraudulent behavior when sensitive card information becomes compromised. Absence of continuous monitoring mechanisms limits the ability of existing systems to detect abnormal transaction activities before transaction completion. Increasing sophistication of fraudulent techniques allows attackers to bypass traditional authentication procedures. Static verification processes cannot effectively monitor behavioral patterns associated with transaction activities such as spending amount, geographical location, transaction frequency, and device information. Lack of real-time behavioral analysis delays identification of suspicious transactions. Identity verification methods applied in many systems do not ensure physical user presence. The proposed work introduces an intelligent fraud detection framework integrating behavioral transaction analysis with biometric identity verification. Transaction data undergo continuous monitoring to identify abnormal behavior. Detection of suspicious patterns activates facial verification and eye detection to confirm user presence. This layered mechanism prevents unauthorized transactions and strengthens transaction security.

title: Simultaneous Reconstruction of Attenuation and Activity Images in TOF-PET Based on Adaptive Multi-Scale Median Root Prior
authors: Yang Ankang, Liu Zixiao, Wan Lin, Xie Qingguo
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: None

title: Reducing Time-to-Surgery in Elderly Patients with Proximal Femur Fractures: A Quality Improvement Initiative Using the M.A.S.T.E.R. Framework
authors: Arianna Scala, Giacomo Negri, Rodolfo Nasti, Elvira Baiano, Vincenzo Bottino, Antonio Nardone, Maria Triassi, Giovanni Improta
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: None

title: A Transformer Based Fault Detection Approach and Its Application in Tandem Cold Rolling Process
authors: Zhen-Lei Ma, Xiao-Jian Li, Jie Sun
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Empowered by the abundant data in the Internet of Things, deep learning models, especially transformers, have achieved great success in the field of industrial process fault diagnosis. However, most existing transformer-based detection methods mainly rely on the temporal information of data while just implicitly considering the correlation between process variables. Moreover, the characteristic of ‘black box’ of transformer models often results in poor interpretability. To handle these problems, an interpretable dual-attention transformer (IDAformer) based fault detection method is proposed, which can explicitly capture both the temporal and variable dependencies. Specifically, an adaptive activation function and a gating mechanism are designed to allocate different activation strengths and weights for the time attention and variable attention, which can enhance network performance. Meanwhile, an interpretable attention mechanism and an attention visualization method are introduced to improve the interpretability. Moreover, state-space model is also integrated to guide the learning of network such that it can further enhance network performance. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through a tandem cold rolling process.

title: SHFGAN: SARImage Harmonization Fusion with Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Junyu Yang, Wenzheng Wang, Chenwei Deng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Although significant progress has been achieved in synthesizing and simulating SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) images using generative models, the random generation of target characteristics and target disappearance during the synthesis process remain major challenges due to the unique nature of SAR images. To address these issues while ensuring harmonious fusion of target patches and backgrounds, this paper proposes a generative model named SHFGAN (SAR Image Harmonization Fusion Generative Adversarial Network). Built upon a multi constraint generative adversarial network (GAN) framework, the generator of SHFGAN employs a ViT (Vision Transformer) model. By introducing attention mechanisms to enhance feature interaction and integrating a semantic embedding module to enforce external semantic constraints, the model effectively resolves unnatural target-background fusion and random generation of target characteristics. Additionally, a multi-task discriminator design, including a realism discriminator and a mask segmentation discriminator, is adopted to respectively constrain the authenticity of generated images and their semantic segmentation results. Through adversarial training and multi constraint loss functions, the problem of target disappearance is mitigated. Quantitative and qualitative experiments on the SARDet-100K, SSDD and AIR-SARship datasets demonstrate that SHFGAN achieves remarkable performance in SAR target patch-background fusion tasks, outperforming common generative models and reaching state-of-the-art levels.

title: Mercury: Multipath Spraying for Joint Congestion and Reordering Control in RDMA
authors: Yuxiang Wang, Jiao Zhang, Leixin Cai, Tao Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Due to the low entropy traffic characteristics of LLM (Large Language Model) training, existing load balancing mechanisms such as Equal-Cost Multi-Path (ECMP) fail to fully utilize the redundant bandwidth between computing nodes in RDMA over Converged Ethernet (RoCE). Packet spraying mechanism has become a typical solution to the load balancing problem in RoCEs. However, it has a negative effect on congestion control mechanisms and suffers severe out-of-order problems. In this paper, we propose Mercury, a host-driven spraying scheme that synergizes congestion feedback and reordering control. Mercury selects paths by leveraging ECN, RTT, and reordering metrics, adjusts rates via multi-metric window. It also employs receiver-side buffers with priority-based dropping to mitigate out-of-order penalties. Evaluations in ns-3 under AllReduce and All-to-All traffic show that Mercury consistently outperforms the ECMP-based baselines, including DCQCN, TIMELY, HPCC, SWIFT, and BOLT, with the largest reduction in Max FCT reaching 63%. Under multi-path load balancing, Mercury delivers the lowest Max FCT for large messages in AllReduce and for most message sizes in All-to-All. It outperforms STRACK and MP-RDMA by up to 28% and 35% in AllReduce, and by up to 25% and 30% in All-to-All.

title: A Novel High-Precision Network Auto-Generation Approach for PMSM Thermal Analysis via Pixel Information
authors: Jinming Xing, Bo Ma, Sheng Huang, Jingyu Zheng, Yu Li, Shuo Wang, Anouar Belahcen, Jianguo Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: High power density is a key design specification for modern permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs), which imposes strict requirements on effective high-precision temperature prediction methods. This paper proposes a pixel information-based method for generating multi-node thermal networks to enable efficient thermal analyses of PMSMs. This method takes cross-sectional motor images as input and automatically generates a high-node-density thermal network by structured grid discretization and pixel-level material information extraction, enabling fully automated thermal network construction. Based on the multi-slice three-dimensional network, the ATNG method achieves high computational efficiency while maintaining accurate temperature prediction, allowing effective representation of internal temperature distributions and transient thermal behavior. Experimental validation on stator modules and a PMSM prototype under various operating conditions shows that the ATNG method can reliably predict temperature trends in both steady-state and transient regimes, with a maximum prediction error within 5%, while offering clear advantages in modeling and simulation efficiency.

title: MPAN: A Multi-Prototype Adaptive Network for Few-Shot EEG-Based Biometric Recognition
authors: Jing Tang, Honggang Liu, Xuanyu Jin, Xuchao Shan, Wanzeng Kong
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Biometric recognition based on electroencephalography (EEG), which captures intrinsic neural dynamics via scalp-recorded electrical activity, has shown great promise. Most existing studies leverage deep learning to enhance recognition performance. However, they typically rely on large amounts of subject-specific data or require retraining to accommodate new users, thereby limiting scalability and generalization. To address these issues, we propose the Multi-Prototype Adaptive Network (MPAN), a dual-branch framework tailored for few-shot EEG-based biometric recognition, enabling rapid adaptation to new subjects. Specifically, a Dual-Path Pretraining Network (DPPN) is first employed to learn discriminative and robust EEG representations through supervised contrastive and mutual distillation losses. Building upon the pretrained global and local metric subnets, the subsequent MPAN integrates complementary metric information via a multi-prototype fusion module, yielding reliable decision-making under few-shot conditions. Evaluated on two public EEG benchmarks, MPAN consistently surpasses state-of-the-art baselines, achieving identification accuracies of 91.86% and 94.37% under the 16-way 5-shot setting. The results highlight its potential as a practical solution for efficient new-subject enrollment in scenarios with limited labeled EEG data.

title: Addressing Data Imbalance in Medical Imaging: A Simulation Study Using Ensemble Transfer Learning for Skin Cancer Detection
authors: Ricardo F. Rocha, Fernando H. A. Moraes Neto, Paulo H. Ferreira, Hellen O. Paz, Adriano K. Suzuki, Francisco Louzada
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Data imbalance is a critical challenge in classification tasks, especially in the medical field, where accurate disease identification is essential. This study investigates the impact of imbalance in medical image datasets and proposes the use of transfer learning models combined with resampling and data augmentation to improve skin tumor detection. Images from ISIC (International Skin Imaging Collaboration) 2016, 2017, and 2018 were used, and simulations were performed varying the proportion of the minority class between 10% and 50%, considering data augmentation and resampling; we used a threshold-based ensemble of 13 convolutional neural network models.We evaluated the performance of combinations with standardized metrics, placing emphasis on the F2-score. The results showthat individual models have difficulty identifying malignant tumors when the imbalance is higher (10% malignant and 90% benign samples). However, the use of resampling and data augmentation and ensemble improves the outcome of these models to predict the malignant class.We conclude that the combination of these techniques is effective in dealing with imbalance, providing improvements in predictive performance.

title: Enhancing Detection of Anomaly-based Intrusions in Smart Health using Explainable AI
authors: Saad Shakeel Abbasi, Saba Mahmood, Fatima Khalique, Mohammed Ali Alshara, Tariqullah Jan, Ruhul Amin Khalil
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Anomaly-based threat detection is crucial for organizations. Anomalies are deviations from the norm and can sometimes be hard to identify in contextual cases. These anomalies may arise from an insider-based malicious intrusion. Anomalies are mostly unsupervised and sometimes require expert validation to confirm their occurrence. The black-box nature of unsupervised machine learning models offers little insight into the origin and details of the anomaly. Thus, there is a need for an explanation layer in anomaly detection systems to aid decision-makers in tracking anomalies. Anomaly-based threat detection is critical for smart health scenarios. In this research, we have utilized the electronic patient record to identify unsupervised anomalies.We have suggested adding an explainability layer that utilizes unsupervised learning. The results indicated that Isolation Forest outperformed the other methods on the clustering metrics, as validated by the explainability layer. Lime and Shap are used to visually explain the anomaly’s cause using feature contributions. User-friendly, explainable captions are generated for each anomaly instance, enabling non-technical users to easily understand the results from Lime and Shap. Three metrics, fidelity, sparsity, and stability, are used to validate the explanation layer, which explains each instance of an anomaly: fidelity requires R2 ≥ 0.70, sparsity favors a smaller number of features that provide user-friendly results, and stability is high. Finally, a Lime-Shap agreement is used to validate the results for each anomaly instance across all Explanations (all seeds). The proposed scheme adds trust to unsupervised learning results, thereby increasing user confidence in the anomaly detection algorithm’s outcomes and aiding decision-makers.

title: Proceedings of the 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream) : Organizers Foreword
authors: Dalius Navakauskas, Artūras Medeišis, Justina Anulytė, Darius Plonis, Tomyslav Sledevič
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: We are very glad to welcome our colleagues – young scientists, researchers and practitioners to the 13-th IEEE Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream’2026), held in Vilnius Gediminas Technical University (VILNIUS TECH), Vilnius, Lithuania, on 23-24 April 2026. The eStream conferences aim to disseminate the research achievements between worldwide groups of scientists and engineers working in different areas of science to reach more tight relationships and generate new ideas for joint projects or other means of collaboration.

title: Variable-Reference Discontinuous Pulse Width Modulation Method for Four-Level Neutral Point Clamped Inverters
authors: Yu Wang, Jianfei Chen, Mengyuan Wang, Chengzhi Li, Jiaqi Chi, Hongda Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Four-level neutral-point-clamped (4L-NPC) inverters are widely used in low- and medium-voltage applications. However, existing variable-reference PWM and carrier-overlapped PWM methods often lead to increased switching losses and uneven loss distribution among power devices. To address this issue, this paper proposes a unified variable-reference discontinuous PWM (VRDPWM) framework that enables two clamping modes: full-clamping VRDPWM-I method and partial-clamping VRDPWM-II method. In the full-clamping method, the phase leg is clamped to the DC-link voltage levels during the discontinuous interval, thereby reducing the number of switching transitions. In contrast, the partial-clamping method selectively suppresses the switching activity of the inner switch pair to redistribute switching losses by exploiting the switching redundancies of the 4L-NPC topology. Furthermore, the influence of different clamping states on capacitor voltage dynamics is analytically investigated, and a compatible capacitor voltage balancing method is developed. Experimental and simulation results verify that the proposed VRDPWM-II method effectively reduces the switching loss of the inner switch pair while maintaining high output current quality and stable DC-link capacitor voltages.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: Development of the Fully Automatic Robotic Storyteller – Comparing Manual, Semi-Automatic, and LLM-Based Emotional Story Annotation for Multimodal Robotic Storytelling_supp1-3691816.pdf
authors: Sophia C. Steinhaeusser
publish: N/A
abstract: Storytelling has accompanied mankind since its beginnings and receiving stories is still a popular leisure activity. Today, new storytelling media are emerging. For example, social robots can act as storytellers, using different modalities such as facial expressions or body postures. With the goal of automating robotic storytelling, we annotated stories with emotion labels which a robot can use to automatically adapt its behavior to. Doing so, three different approaches and a baseline are compared in two studies in this paper: 1) manual labels by human annotators (MA), 2) software-based word-sensitive annotation using the Linguistic Inquiry and Word Count program (LIWC), 3) a LLM approach using ChatGPT (GPT), and 4) random annotations as baseline (RBL). In an online study showing videos of a storytelling robot, the annotations were validated using a backwards annotation task, with GPT closely followed by MA achieving the best, and the RBL the worst results. In a subsequent lab user study, all versions of the story were compared regarding storytelling experience, emotions, and robot perception, revealing no significant differences for any of these variables based on the annotation approach, but a significant decrease of negative affect and fatigue over the storytelling in all conditions. Taken together, the results indicate suitability of both the manual and GPT approaches. On this empirical basis, the Automatic Robotic Storyteller was implemented using GPT-based annotations and bodily expressions. The reduced programming effort of our system makes robotic storytelling accessible to a broader audience, strengthening its deployment in the field of affective computing.

title: Study on the Performance of Epoxy Resin Insulating Materials Based on Plasma Surface Modification of polydimethylsiloxane
authors: Haoqin Chen, Xuefeng Ye, Chenjie Li, Chang Liu, Zhiyuan Zhou, Xiaoxing Zhang, Yunjian Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: To enhance the hydrophobicity and insulation performance of epoxy resin (EP), the impact of polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) modification on the surface structure and properties of biphenyl-based EP is systematically investigated via atmospheric-pressure argon plasma modification (input voltage: 16 kV, current: 2.5 mA, Gas flow rate: 100 ml/min). Experimental results demonstrate that surface properties are enhanced through the introduction of silicon-containing groups onto the resin surface, where a uniform and dense micro-nano spherical granular structure is formed. Consequently, optimal hydrophobicity is achieved with a contact angle increase of 22.7 ° compared to the unmodified EP, while dielectric properties are maintained with excellent stability. Furthermore, the surface resistivity exhibits a trend of increasing initially and then decreasing with modification time, peaking at a value 150.8% higher than the untreated EP control sample, and tracking resistance is significantly improved as carbonization width and erosion depth are reduced by 55.6% and 48.0%, respectively, compared to the untreated EP control sample. It is observed that insufficient modification duration results in limited performance enhancement, whereas excessive duration causes performance degradation due to surface damage induced by thermal ablation. This study not only provides targeted and reliable experimental data and theoretical support for resin surface modification but also offers a replicable technical path and scientific reference for the engineering promotion of high-stability EP in high-end applications, including electrical insulation and electronic packaging, from the perspective of synergistic optimization of surface structure design and performance.

title: Geranium: Multimodal Retrieval of Genomics Data Visualizations_supp1-3683429.mp4
authors: Nils Gehlenborg
publish: N/A
abstract: Effective visualization is essential for interpreting genomics data, yet researchers often face challenges in finding relevant, reusable examples. Existing tools offer limited support for searching the vast landscape of genomics visualizations, making the process of authoring new visualizations time-consuming and inefficient. To address this gap, we introduce Geranium, a data visualization retrieval system for searching and authoring genomics visualizations. Geranium supports multimodal retrieval, enabling users to query with images, text, or grammar based specifications. Retrieved examples serve as scaffolds for authoring, providing templates that researchers can adapt with their own data, thereby streamlining the mechanics of visualization construction. Geranium integrates three embedding methods to combine specialized and general knowledge: grammar-based embeddings tailored to genomics visualizations, multimodalem beddings from a biomedical vision-language foundation model, and text embeddings from a fine-tuned large language model. For each visualization, we construct a multimodal representation that includes a Gosling specification, a pixel-based rendering, and natural language descriptions. We evaluate embedding strategies to maximize top-k retrieval accuracy and conduct user studies with domain collaborators to gather feedback on usability. Our collection comprises 3,200 visualizations across 50 categories, ranging from single-view to coordinated multi-view designs and supporting applications from single-cell epigenomics to structural variation analysis.

title: A Web-Enabled Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Proactive Ambulance Pre-clearance
authors: Saran Raj Sowrirajan, Mohan Koduri, Shaik Mansoor
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Congestion and excessive delays due to inefficient and inflexible control of the traffic signal are usual in urban road networks, which are particularly critical in the case of emergency vehicles, such as ambulances. The challenge lies in how to provide timely priority to ambulances, while keeping acceptable conditions for other traffic within an urban traffic management system. In this paper, we propose a web-enabled, Deep Reinforcement Learning-based traffic signal control framework for proactive ambulance route pre-clearance in urban environments. The traffic signal controller is implemented using a DQN, and the state space consists of observed traffic states obtained from the SUMO simulator, and a route-priority factor, calculated using the ambulance’s route endpoints. The action space consists of all feasible selections of the traffic signal phases at signalized intersections. A lightweight web application provides the interface to submit the details of the ambulance route in advance, to enable route-aware signal control along the ambulance corridor. Simulation experiments are conducted using the SUMO traffic simulator on a realistic urban road network derived from OpenStreetMap. Various traffic scenarios, such as peak congestion, incident-induced blockage, event surges, mixed traffic flow, and weather-affected traffic conditions, are considered for evaluation. The results show that the proposed Web-enabled DQN framework effectively reduces ambulance travel time compared to a reactive DQN baseline, while also improving overall traffic flow.

title: Design and Implementation of the Gate Driver Based SiC Power Module
authors: Kavitha S, Kumar C
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: In this paper, the design of a CMOS gate driver module designed and its performance analysis at LTspice is provided. Traditional gate driver circuits, which can be made with bipolar or discrete MOSFET stages, have disadvantages in that they consume more power, have lower switching speed and require more design effort. These limits limit their application in high performance, and low power applications where efficiency and rapid response is critical. To address these, the proposed CMOS-based gate driver is architecturally simplified and optimally sized in all transistors to so as to achieve reduced propagation of the gates, reduced switching losses and a higher energy efficiency. The design is proved effective as fast rise and fall times, low levels of static power dissipation and constant operation under different loads are validated by LTspice simulations. The findings affirm that the CMOS gate driver module proposed is very appropriate in the current power electronic system, converters, inverters and motor drives.

title: Optimizing Bug Bounty Recon Workflows using Automated Asset Discovery and Vulnerability Correlation
authors: Seema Lavhate, Shakil Shaikh, Swapnil Bangal, Vedant Borawake, Krushna Shinde, Kunal Patil
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Bug bounty hunting has become a critical component of modern cybersecurity programs; however, the reconnaissance phase remains time-consuming, repetitive, and highly dependent on manual effort. Large attack surfaces, dispersed tools, irregular workflows, and a high likelihood of overlooking crucial assets due to human mistake are some of the difficulties faced by today's bug hunters. These limitations reduce efficiency and delay vulnerability discovery, especially in large-scale web applications. To address this problem, this project focuses on the automation of the bug bounty reconnaissance workflow by integrating multiple passive and active reconnaissance techniques into a unified and systematic framework. The proposed automation tool streamlines tasks such as subdomain enumeration, service discovery, endpoint identification, and data aggregation, minimizing manual intervention. By automating recon processes, the workflow ensures better coverage, faster execution, and improved accuracy while maintaining scalability. The project demonstrates how an automated recon workflow can significantly enhance productivity for bug bounty hunters and security researchers, enabling them to focus more on vulnerability analysis rather than repetitive data collection. This approach helps overcome current reconnaissance challenges and contributes to more effective and reliable vulnerability discovery in modern bug bounty programs.

title: Non Invasive Knee Pressure Monitoring System
authors: L. Dhiviyalakshmi, S R Harsini, R Hima Sri, N Guruprasad, Deepa B. Prabhu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Continuous monitoring of knee joint pressure is essential for early diagnosis of degenerative joint conditions, rehabilitation assessment, and injury prevention. Conventional evaluation techniques are either invasive, laboratory dependent, expensive, or unsuitable for long-term monitoring. This paper presents the design and implementation of a wearable, non-invasive knee pressure monitoring system integrated with an IoT-enabled embedded platform for real-time data acquisition and transmission. The proposed system employs an array of force-sensitive resistors positioned strategically around the knee joint to capture dynamic pressure distribution during flexion and extension movements. The embedded controller performs signal conditioning, analog to digital conversion, and wireless data transmission to a remote monitoring interface. Experimental validation demonstrates stable signal response, repeatability, and low latency transmission. Comparative analysis with existing approaches highlights improvements in portability, affordability, and continuous monitoring capability. The proposed system provides a scalable and cost-effective solution for orthopedic rehabilitation and preventive joint health monitoring.

title: Smart Portable IoT Monitoring for Indoor Air Quality with Cloud Intelligence and Instant Insights
authors: Swathi Paapanna Gari, Sowjanya D, Nirmala M B, Chandan B Ram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The continuous monitoring of indoor air quality (IAQ) becomes necessary as quality of air directly affects human health. This project presents a IAQ monitoring system that uses an ESP32 with BME680 sensor to monitor indoor air conditions in real time while maintaining low costs. Machine learning techniques are employed to classify air quality into three categories: Good, Moderate, and Poor. The three models Random Forest, SVM and KNN received live data for testing procedures. The Random Forest Classifier produced the highest results among all models by achieving 97.89% accuracy and 98.21% precision and 97.55% recall and 97.88% F1-score. The system includes a Flask web dashboard which displays current sensor readings and prediction results and provides automated email alerts with built-in spam protection. It includes a secure cloud storage module which transfers historical reports to AWS S3 for extended data preservation. The hardware and communication tests demonstrated that data moves without interruption from the sensor to the dashboard. This delivers a dependable and user-friendly solution for real-time intelligent indoor air quality monitoring.

title: An Effective One-vs-All Bidirectional GRU Framework for Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Sudhanwa Bokade, Sampada Thigale
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: There are currently three major challenges for effective Speech Emotion Recognition (SER): a lack of dataset variety, unequal amounts of data from different emotion classes, and overlapping acoustical qualities for different emotions. This paper proposes an ensemble of Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units (BiGRUs) in a One-vs-All (OvA) classification architecture trained on a large corpus of labelled datasets. The dataset was developed by combining four established datasets (i.e., CREMA-D, Emo-DB, RAVDESS, and SAVEE) that were equalised with respect to the number of samples taken from each class. For each audio file, an 85-dimensional feature set was created using audio analysis methods (i.e.,MFCC, Zero-Crossing Rate, Teager Energy Operator, and Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio), and the datasets were processed through a systematic modification pipeline (i.e., pitch shifting, adding random gains, injecting white noise, and time-stretching). Using six independent OvA classifiers, we concluded the emotion class using a maximal-confidence selection strategy. The performance of this framework was demonstrated through AUC scores ranging from 0.974 to 0.989 and macro-averaged F1-Scores ranging from 0.85 to 0.94 for all emotions. A real-time inference pipeline was implemented for microphone-based predictions, demonstrating the potential for implementation in practical applications. Overall, results show that combining multiple features of each emotion with a specialised OvA architecture improves the reliability and robustness of Speech Emotion Recognition.

title: Examining the Role of Facial and Gesture Emojis in Context-Aware Sarcasm Detection
authors: Shi Jie Mak, Chuk Fong Ho, Ching Pang Goh
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Sarcasm detection remains a complex challenge in natural language processing due to the absence of nonverbal cues in text and the difficulty of interpreting nuanced roles of emojis, particularly distinguishing between facial and gesture types. This study addresses these gaps by developing a multimodel comprehensive analysis focused on the distinct roles of facial versus gesture emoji categories in sarcasm detection framework, utilizing Global Vectors for Word Representation (GloVe) and DeepMoji embeddings to evaluate the contribution of facial and gesture emojis through a benchmarking analysis of three architectures: Random Forest (RF), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM), and Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT). Results show that BERT achieves the strongest performance, particularly with facial emojis, reaching an F1-score of 0.98 and a Matthews Correlation Coefficient of 0.9655 -substantial gains over baseline models. While BERT benefits less from gesture emojis than RF and Bi-LSTM, the findings demonstrate that emojis serve as informative cues of sarcastic intent. Overall, the study proves that the effectiveness of emojis depends on both the model architecture and emoji type, highlighting that facial, gesture, and other emojis should not be treated equally in sarcasm analysis, and necessitating more nuanced, emoji-aware approaches for sentiment classification and content moderation.

title: Uncertainty-Calibrated Multi-Fidelity Graph Transformer for Integrated Circuit Parametric Performance Prediction and Hotspot Localization
authors: Chenxu Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: Integrated circuit (IC) design and verification workflows frequently depend on corner-aware circuit simulation and post-layout signoff analysis, which incur substantial computational cost and limit rapid design space exploration. To address data scarcity and expensive high-fidelity simulation, this paper presents an uncertainty-calibrated multi-fidelity learning framework for parametric performance prediction and hotspot localization at the circuit level. A heterogeneous circuit graph is constructed to encode device attributes, net connectivity, and optional layout-aware context, enabling topology-preserving representation learning. A graph transformer is then employed to capture long-range dependencies across devices and nets, while a gated multi-fidelity fusion module models high-fidelity targets as a combination of low-fidelity trends and learnable residual corrections. To improve engineering reliability, conformal prediction is adopted to produce statistically valid prediction intervals, providing calibrated uncertainty alongside point estimates. In addition, attribution-based analysis on graph nodes and edges is introduced to identify performance-critical devices and interconnects, supporting hotspot localization. Experimental results on representative circuit benchmarks demonstrate improved accuracy and uncertainty calibration compared with conventional regression and graph neural network baselines under limited high-fidelity samples, while reducing the required number of expensive simulations for comparable predictive performance.

title: Efficient and Interpretable Spam Email Filtering using SVM and Decision Trees
authors: V.Vadivu, V.Ganesan, R. Prasanth, Anjana A, R. Anitha, A. H. Abdul Kather
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Electronic mail isa primary mode of communication, it’s usage leads to increase spam communications, which causes security and productivity problems. This research framework provides a hybrid spam email classification framework that combines Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Decision Tree (DT) classifiers toward increase detection precision besides robustness. The proposed method integrates text-based features developed using Term Frequency Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) which capture both linguistic then structural patterns of spam emails. Feature fusion and classification are executed using a weighted probability-based decision-making method. The research findings are based on a composite dataset of the email corpora from Enron and SpamAssassin. The proposed hybrid system performs significantly better than individual classifiers. Further, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques like SHAP and LIME are combined toward expand the representation's interpretability too transparency. The research method provides a scalable, effective result used for detecting spam emails.
