title: Learning Spatial-Temporal Coherent Correlations for Speech-Preserving Facial Expression Manipulation_supp1-3687518.pdf
authors: Tianshui Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Speech-preserving facial expression manipulation (SPFEM) aims to modify facial emotions while meticulously maintaining the mouth animation associated with spoken content. Current works depend on inaccessible paired training samples for the person, where two aligned frames exhibit the same speech content yet differ in emotional expression, limiting the SPFEM applications in real-world scenarios. In this work, we discover that speakers who convey the same content with different emotions exhibit highly correlated local facial animations in both spatial and temporal spaces, providing valuable supervision for SPFEM. To capitalize on this insight, we propose a novel spatial-temporal coherent correlation learning (STCCL) algorithm, which models the aforementioned correlations as explicit metrics and integrates the metrics to supervise manipulating facial expression and meanwhile better preserving the facial animation of spoken content. To this end, it first learns a spatial coherent correlation metric, ensuring that the visual correlations of adjacent local regions within an image linked to a specific emotion closely resemble those of corresponding regions in an image linked to a different emotion. Simultaneously, it develops a temporal coherent correlation metric, ensuring that the visual correlations of specific regions across adjacent image frames associated with one emotion are similar to those in the corresponding regions of frames associated with another emotion. Recognizing that visual correlations are not uniform across all regions, we have also crafted a correlation-aware adaptive strategy that prioritizes regions that present greater challenges. During SPFEM model training, we construct the spatial-temporal coherent correlation metric between corresponding local regions of the input and output image frames as additional loss to supervise the generation process. We conduct extensive experiments on various datasets, and the results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed STCCL algorithm.

title: DOA: A Degeneracy Optimization Agent With Adaptive Pose Compensation Capability Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yanbin Li, Canran Xiao, Hongyang He, Shenghai Yuan, Zong Ke, Jiajie Yu, Zixiong Qin, Zhiguo Zhang, Wenzheng Chi, Wei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Particle filter-based simultaneous localization and mapping (2-D-SLAM) is widely used in indoor localization tasks due to its efficiency. However, indoor environments such as long straight corridors can cause severe degeneracy problems in SLAM. In this article, we propose a new paradigm of degeneracy optimization and demonstrate its effectiveness through mathematical analysis and extensive experiments. We use proximal policy optimization (PPO) Schulman to train an adaptive degeneracy optimization agent (DOA) to address degeneracy problem. We propose a systematic methodology to address three critical challenges in traditional supervised learning frameworks: 1) data acquisition bottlenecks in degenerate dataset; 2) inherent quality deterioration of training samples; and 3) ambiguity in annotation rules design. And we design a specialized reward function to guide the agent in developing perception capabilities for degenerate environments. Using the output degeneracy factor as reference, the agent can dynamically adjust the process of sensor fusion in pose optimization. Specifically, the observation distribution is shifted towards the motion distribution, with the step size determined by a linear interpolation formula related to the degeneracy factor. In addition, we employ a transfer learning module to endow the agent with generalization capabilities across different environments and address the inefficiency of training in degenerate environments. Finally, we conduct ablation studies to demonstrate the rationality of our model design and the role of transfer learning. We also compare the proposed DOA with state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods to prove its superior degeneracy detection and optimization capabilities across various environments.

title: ULTRA-MACE: A Unified Low-bit Trans-precision Reconfigurable Multiply-Accumulate Compute Engine for Accelerated Computing
authors: Vijay Pratap Sharma, Shekhar Venkatpurwar, Mukul Lokhande, Ratko Pilipović, Santosh Kumar Vishvakarma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The rising demand for energy- and resource-efficient edge-AI platforms calls for compute engines that balance throughput, flexibility, and run-time efficiency under tight bandwidth constraints. This brief presents ULTRA-MACE, a unified low-bit trans-precision reconfigurable multiply-accumulate compute engine (MACE) that supports FP4 (E2M1), FP8 (E4M3), Posit-(8,2), and Posit-(16,3) through a six-stage vectorized pipeline with 128-bit quire accumulation. ULTRA-MACE employs a high-precision split mantissa multiplication scheme and an integrated gated bypass for ReLU, achieving 4×/2×/2×/1× throughput (FP4/FP8/Posit-8/Posit-16) without increasing bandwidth demand. Post-route results in 28nm CMOS demonstrate a peak energy efficiency of 12.8 TFLOPS/W at 0.9 V and a compute density of 8.2 TFLOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Integrated into the TREA accelerator with 64 MACE, the FPGA prototype achieves 89% of theoretical peak performance and maintains practical accuracy with only 1.5% degradation relative to the FP32 baseline. Overall, these results demonstrate an efficient trans-precision compute engine for edge-AI acceleration.

title: Joint MBMP-CMSE and NC-MVDR Technologies for FDA-MIMO SAR Mainlobe NMM-ISRJ Suppression
authors: Hanqing Zhang, Guodong Jin, Hongbo Zhang, Shilin Niu, Jie Lv, Yuan Cheng, Jing Ding, Zhenyu Guo, Shaohua Ye, Daiyin Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The mainlobe jamming suppression constitutes a persistent challenge in contemporary radar systems. The mainlobe noise multiplication modulation interrupted sampling repeater jamming (NMM-ISRJ) exhibits dual characteristics of deception and dense masking, making it difficult to effectively distinguish the real targets in radar detection. Frequency diverse array multi-input multi-output synthetic aperture radar (FDA-MIMO SAR) utilizes its additional controllable degrees of freedom (DOFs) to effectively distinguish between the real targets and mainlobe jamming in transmit spatial frequency domain. However, the traditional blocking matrix preprocessing (BMP) and improved BMP (IBMP) methods will cause the desired signal to lose array DOFs, leading to issues such as the mainlobe broadening of the antenna pattern and sidelobe level amplitude rise. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a joint multistage BMP covariance matrix symmetric extension (MBMP-CMSE) and norm constrained minimum variance distortionless response (NC-MVDR) to suppress mainlobe NMM-ISRJ for FDA-MIMO SAR. Firstly, the mathematical model of MBMP is derived based on the traditional BMP to suppress the desired signal. Then, the CMSE algorithm is applied to avoid the array DOFs loss. Finally, a robust beamforming is achieved using NC-MVDR algorithm to suppress mainlobe NMM-ISRJ. The effectiveness of the joint MBMP-CMSE and NC-MVDR in suppressing mainlobe NMM-ISRJ is validated by integrated experiments, including quantitative analysis, point-like targets and distributed scene comparative simulation experiment.

title: Coded Modulation Hopping with Group Adaptive Modulation and Coding for Secure Communications
authors: Jingya Yang, Yiqing Zhou, Yihao Wu, Zhijun Han, Ling Liu, Jinglin Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Modulation-based communication security is promising. However, most existing modulation hopping-based physical layer security methods select modulation schemes arbitrarily, which is not practical. To this end, this paper proposes a coded modulation hopping scheme based on group adaptive modulation and coding (CMHop-GroupAMC). First, a group modulation and coding mapping table is constructed, where several modulation and coding schemes with the same spectral efficiency are selected to satisfy a target block error ratio. Then, under a given channel condition, coded modulation hopping can be performed within the group. Moreover, according to different prior information available to the eavesdropper, different classifiers such as classical single modulation classifier (SMC), cluster-based modulation classifier (CBMC), and one symbol-based modulation classifier (OneSymMC) are considered. Assuming that the eavesdropper has no prior information, the classification probability of an eavesdropper (CPE) using SMC is analyzed based on the likelihood function. Moreover, assuming that the eavesdropper has some prior information (e.g., the received symbols are from multiple modulations), the CPE with CBMC or OneSymMC can be evaluated via simulations where deep learning (DL)-based intelligent classification algorithms are employed to carry out classification. Analytical and simulation results demonstrate that, when CMHop-GroupAMC is employed, the classification performance of the eavesdropper is effectively degraded under SMC, CBMC and OneSymMC.

title: Satellite-Collaborative Computation Federated Learning Optimization in LEO Edge Intelligence Networks
authors: Junyi Yang, Yafeng Ma, Zhenyu Xiao, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Satellite edge federated learning (FL) is an important application paradigm of the integrated artificial intelligence and communication in the future sixth-generation (6G) system. However, achieving efficient FL tasks within highly dynamic, computationally constrained low-earth orbit (LEO) satellite networks while balancing delay and energy consumption remains a significant challenge. Therefore, we explore a satellite-collaborative computation federated learning (SCCFL) system in LEO edge intelligence networks. To this end, we formulate a multi-metric trade-off optimization problem weighted by delay and energy consumption, by jointly optimizing computation satellite (CS) offloading, aggregation satellite (AgS) selection, and computation resource allocation. We propose a multi-metric intelligent alternating (MIA) optimization method, in which each independent agent is responsible for providing decision reference Q-values of specific variables (such as CSs or AgS) for specific metrics (such as delay or energy consumption). The final decision is made based on weighted Q-values. During training, each agent is trained alternately. We also propose a multi-metric computation resource allocation strategy that minimizes delay while reducing the energy consumption as much as possible. Simulation results validate the superiority of our proposed method in terms of FL accuracy and the trade-off between delay and energy consumption.

title: Optimized Pulse Patterns Design for Electrical Drives: A Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Mohammad Abu-Ali, Felix Berkel, Maximilian Manderla, Markus Pietschner, Daniel Görges
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society
abstract: Optimal modulation techniques such as optimized pulse patterns (OPPs) are essential for minimizing power losses and hence achieving efficient operation of electrical drive systems. Conventional OPPs are designed offline to minimize different steady-state performance criteria and require switching angles-based analytical models suitable for gradient-based optimization. Such models are however not always available and can be complex and difficult to validate. Hence, a data-driven design approach that can utilize any form of models, or even be used for direct OPP learning, e.g., from a real-world drive, without requiring any modeling effort, is of significant interest. In this paper, we present a first proof of concept for a data-driven design of steady-state OPPs using reinforcement learning (RL). We explore the feasibility of training an end-to-end neural network policy that maps desired working points (WPs) to reference OPPs by interacting with an electrical drive environment. We introduce a novel parametrization of switching angles that inherently incorporates their constraints, and we utilize an efficient simulation set-up to simplify the training for this proof-of-concept investigation. Through extensive simulation and real-world experiments, we demonstrate the potential and trade offs of the proposed RL-based approach for designing loss-optimal pulse patterns for a traction permanent magnet synchronous motor drive. The performance is benchmarked against a conventional, indirectly loss optimized, model-based OPP design. Test bench results show a potential for power loss reduction in learned OPPs compared to conventional ones, despite the simulation model bias, and motivate learning OPPs directly from a real-world drive environment.

title: Longitudinal Passability and Energy-Feasibility Screening of Mountain Routes for Powered Off-Road Wheelchairs
authors: M. Staniek
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: his paper presents a scalable first-order framework for screening mountain routes for powered off-road wheelchairs by combining dense route geometry with a one-dimensional longitudinal energy model. Dense traces sampled at approximately 1 m resolution (~10,000 km of routes in Poland) were reprojected to a metric coordinate reference system, smoothed in elevation, split at discontinuities, and analyzed with 3-, 10-, and 30-m grade windows. The resulting descriptors capture maximum uphill and downhill grades and the length of continuous steep sections, which are interpreted relative to wheelchair-specific screening profiles to assign segment-level passability classes. The same segmented representation is processed by an energy-feasibility layer that includes gravitational and rolling loads, aerodynamic drag, drivetrain losses, controller current and power limits, and a Thevenin-equivalent battery with open-circuit voltage and state-of-charge tracking. In this framework, controller constraints are not treated only as diagnostic flags: a feasible-speed check allows current, power, and voltage saturation to affect the route-level decision directly. Because the available route traces do not directly include cross-slope, surface roughness, traction, or surface-dependent rolling resistance, these factors are handled as explicit limitations and as sources of scenario-based uncertainty rather than inferred deterministically from longitudinal geometry alone. The preprocessing yielded 1,024 valid routes and 1,055 continuous sections in the database. On a length-weighted basis, the green passability share ranged from 44.26% to 69.75%, and the mean predicted energy demand under the adopted baseline vehicle parameter set was 40.56 Wh/km. The integrated output distinguishes geometrically blocked routes from geometrically passable but energetically risky cases. The framework is intended as a transparent screening and prioritization tool for route recommendation and future field calibration, not as a substitute for field inspection or fully validated safety assessment.

title: Performance and Efficiency Trade-Off Analysis of Modern CNN Architectures for Automated Stem Cell Colony Quality Assessment
authors: Hatice Hilal Gurleyen, Nejat Yumusak
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Quality assessment of stem cell colonies plays a critical role in regenerative medicine and cell-based therapies. Performing this assessment manually by experts is both time-consuming and prone to inter-observer variability. In addition, the limited availability of high-quality labeled stem cell colony images increases the risk of overfitting in data-driven approaches and complicates model generalization. In this study, a systematic comparison of multiple CNN architectures is presented for the binary classification problem of stem cell colony images as good or bad, within a multi-dimensional evaluation framework incorporating performance, calibration, and efficiency. On an open-access dataset previously reported in the literature with an accuracy of approximately 89%, eight modern CNN architectures were evaluated using standard preprocessing and data augmentation strategies under a five-fold cross-validation protocol, including VGG13, VGG16, ResNet50V2, DenseNet169, InceptionV3, MobileNetV3-Small, EfficientNet-B0, and EfficientNetV2-S. The models were analyzed using conventional performance metrics such as accuracy, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and Average Precision, as well as calibration metrics including the Brier score, Expected Calibration Error, and Negative Log-Likelihood. Post-hoc calibration via temperature scaling was applied using a split-validation protocol. In addition, the models were compared in terms of computational cost including GFLOPs, VRAM consumption, number of parameters, training time, and inference latency, and data-efficiency was assessed via learning curve analysis. The results demonstrate that the EfficientNetV2-S model achieves the most balanced performance in terms of accuracy, generalization, and calibration, reaching an accuracy of 95.2%, while the EfficientNet-B0 model provides competitive performance with substantially lower computational cost. Calibration performance improves notably after post-hoc scaling, highlighting the importance of reliable confidence estimation. These findings emphasize the critical importance of balancing performance, efficiency, and reliability in model architecture selection for data-limited biomedical imaging scenarios and present a practical deep learning framework for the automated quality assessment of stem cell colonies.

title: Advanced AI Platforms for Securing Wireless Communication Using Real-Time Threat Detection
authors: Arun Prasath N, B. Harchana, K. Suganya, Avinash Amaranayani, R. Sujitha, Athiraja A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The growth of wireless communication systems creates critical security issues for real-time threat detection because security has become a major concern. The current security protocols demonstrate insufficient capability to both recognize and eliminate dynamic security threats throughout wireless networks. This document investigates how advanced AI platforms can improve wireless communication systems security by enabling real-time threats identification. The proposed solution uses Random Forest together with Support Vector Machine and Deep Neural Networks under a machine learning paradigm to detect anomalies. The system runs through network traffic data containing attack and regular traffic patterns from a training dataset. This study reveals that the AI based system has a detection accuracy of 97.5% with precision of 94.2%, recall of 92.8%, which outperforms traditional method. The finished system offers superior capabilities to find complex threats including DDoS attacks and unauthorized intrusion attempts. It becomes evident that AI-based threat detection systems bring powerful security capabilities to wireless network protection because they adapt to variations and provide reliable security measures. The future project concentrates on enhancing performance and implementing real-time edge deployment for operational purposes.

title: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis using Text and Emoji: A Comparative Study of Classical and Transformer-based Models
authors: Javer C. Borngo, Maureen Villamor, Veldin A. Talorete
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: This research seeks to explore the effectiveness of multimodality in sentiment analysis using textual content and emoji characteristics to categorize the sentiment found within social media data. Traditional sentiment analysis approaches mainly depend on textual elements and frequently overlook emojis, which have become important carriers of emotional expression in digital communication. Thus addressing this limitation, this paper proposed a multimodal sentiment analysis framework which utilizes demojized emoji representations in the text parsing pipeline. This paper assesses the performance of classical machine learning models Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine and transformer-based models BERT and RoBERTa. We tested a large amount of social media posts (circa 45,000 training samples and 12,000 testing samples). Our results indicate that using emoji features enhances the sentiment classification performance across all models. Classical models demonstrated substantial gains when emojis were incorporated, with Logistic Regression reaching an accuracy of 0.99. The transformer-based models performed the best, as BERT achieved near-perfect accuracy and macro F1-scores approximately 0.99. These results emphasise the role of emoji-based emotional cues and confirm the effectiveness of transformer architectures for multimodal sentiment analysis in informal social media environments.

title: A Warm-Started CP-SAT Framework for Resource-Constrained Project Scheduling
authors: Mohamed Riyaz A, E. Grace Mary Kanaga
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Project scheduling faces delays because construction materials are unavailable and adverse weather conditions prevent work and there are not enough workers which results in higher expenses for the project. The existing methods which CP solvers use to reach their highest performance require users to pay for complete system re-optimization after every system disruption because the methods do not support warm starting. Our solution implements a hybrid framework that combines Serial Generation Scheme (SGS) heuristics with Google CP-SAT optimization to perform K=200 parallel SGS evaluations which assess various evaluation methods for multiple schedule creation. The PSPLIB benchmarks show our method achieves a mean APD of 0.46% on J30 which optimally resolves 473 of 480 cases within 120 seconds while the J60 test achieved a 5.66% success rate with 413 optimal results out of 480 and the J120 test achieved 26.72% success with 23 optimal results out of 60 at 300 seconds. Serial SGS achieved an 8.04% success rate while the two remaining success rates reached 17.87% and 43.28% respectively. Domain-bound tightening method with best-of-200 restart diversity achieved 5.3-times APD enhancement from cold-start (20.21% to 3.79%) while hint injection provided slight benefits based on test results from 50 J120 instances. The framework creates a certified-optimal base which enables ML-based delay forecasting and GNN task encoding for RL-driven adaptive rescheduling extensions.

title: Characterization of Myocardial Structural Heterogeneity Using Radiomic Texture Features and Clinical Biomarkers
authors: Arina Peržu, Žygimantas Abramikas, Sigita Glaveckaitė, Giedrė Balčiūnaitė, Nomeda Rima Valevičienė, Darius Palionis, Povilas Treigys, Jolita Bematavičienė
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: Quantitative characterization of myocardial structure and function from cardiac MRI remains a major challenge in cardiac imaging. This study investigates radiomics-based texture analysis for myocardial phenotyping using cardiac MRI. Texture features aggregated across the full cardiac cycle were used to construct a global myocardial representation in a cohort of 87 subjects. This representation provided the most effective discrimination between pathological and healthy myocardium. Unsupervised clustering revealed distinct myocardial phenotypes associated with significant differences in global longitudinal strain and native T1, while extracellular volume fraction and collagen volume fraction showed no systematic variation. Cluster stability analysis confirmed robustness to cohort restriction and low agreement with clinically driven clustering, highlighting the complementary role of radiomics in myocardial characterization.

title: Deep Learning based Techniques for Bias Detection & Mitigation : An Extensive Review of Challenges, Effects and Future Enhancements
authors: Milind Shah, Sandipkumar R Panchal, Sweta S Panchal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: In the rapid, emerging and evolving field of Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Deep Learning concerns have arisen about the opacity of certain models and their potential biases. Bias in Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems has emerged as one of the most pressing concerns in ensuring fairness, accountability, and transparency in automated decision-making. This paper provides a structured exploration of bias in AI by categorizing its types and outlining the sociotechnical consequences of biased systems across diverse application domains. Building on a systematic review of contemporary literature, we analyze state-of-the-art approaches for detecting and mitigating bias, with particular emphasis on their applicability to deep learning models used in real-world contexts. The comparative evaluation highlights the strengths and limitations of existing bias-handling techniques and underscores the trade-offs between accuracy, fairness, and interpretability. Furthermore, this review examines practical mitigation strategies implemented in deployment scenarios, bridging theoretical advances with industrial practices. Finally, the challenges and unresolved research gaps are discussed to provide future research directions aimed at developing ethically robust, bias-resilient AI systems. This research offers both a consolidated perspective on the current landscape and a roadmap for advancing fairness in next-generation AI architectures.

title: Efficient Simulation Methods for SAR System Design and Analysis
authors: Renuka Gunturu, Nagajyothi Aggala, Chandrasekhar Maloth, Malasri.T
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Understanding Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) complexities and the impact of its signal parameters is important for advancing radar technology. This paper focus in the development of MATLAB-based SAR simulations and utilizes a Vector Signal Analyzer (VSA) to evaluate range and cross-range imaging performance in various environments. The research focused on three signal types: sinusoidal pulses, linear frequency modulation (LFM) chirps, and band-limited white Gaussian noise. Results demonstrated that while all signals successfully produced functional images, the LFM chirp yielded the highest resolution and detail due to its superior bandwidth characteristics. Notably, Gaussian noise performed comparably to sinusoidal pulses, suggesting unexpected utility for noise-based signals in SAR applications under specific conditions. These findings underscore the inherent flexibility of SAR systems and highlight how strategic signal selection directly influences imaging quality. Ultimately, this work provides a framework for optimizing radar signal processing and motivates further exploration of non-traditional emission parameters to improve SAR data acquisition and accuracy.

title: AI-based Cognitive Load and Drowsiness Detection using Eye Images
authors: Ayushi Vinod Telrandhe, Chetan Puri, Rashi Katade
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The cognitive load assessment developments have been spread across a wide field that includes educational applications of online lectures and VR language learning, as well as professional high-stakes application in dental simulation, driver monitoring, and simultaneous interpretation. Using sophisticated machine learning algorithms, such as the Support Vector Machines (SVM), the Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA), and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), researchers have obtained high classification with an accuracy of 88-92% when they use multimodal physiological features. These models succeed in processing complex data streams of eye-tracking and other sensors to give objective and continuous measures of mental effort as opposed to the restrictions of fixed, subjective questionnaires. Nevertheless, the discipline has serious challenges, the chief one being the heterogeneity of data on various experimental designs, highly sensitive privacy issues with biometric data, and the ongoing problem of establishing ecological validity beyond controlled laboratory settings. Therefore, the subsequent research projects should focus on creating unified multimodal data sets and powerful, adaptive systems in real-time. These innovations are paramount in helping to deploy ethical AI which is not only able to detect but also reduce cognitive overload to have the end result of promoting personalized and resilient learning conditions.

title: Smart and Affordable Battery Monitoring Using ESP8266, secured by AWS and Powered with Blockchain Integration
authors: Lingesh Kumar G S, Kannan Ramakrishnan, M.Mohamed Munavar Hussain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The challenge this project addresses is the high cost and inefficiency of traditional Battery Monitoring Systems, which are unsuitable for low-cost, embedded applications. The problem lies in the limited accessibility of accurate, secure, and affordable monitoring solutions for rechargeable battery packs. An Arduino Uno-based BMoS with the integration of low-cost sensors and IoT capabilities can ensure efficient monitoring of voltage, current, and overall battery health. The rationale of this study is to develop and validate an effective, secure, and intelligent system capable of real-time monitoring and predictive analysis on a rechargeable battery pack. The proposed system comprises Arduino Uno interfaced with current and voltage sensors, an LCD display for local monitoring, and an ESP8266 module for IoT-based data logging on ThingSpeak. Data analysis is enhanced through MATLAB using Support Vector Machine algorithms, while blockchain and AWS integration ensure data integrity and robust security. The novelty will be in the unified integration of IoT, machine learning, and blockchain within a single framework of BMoS.

title: Privacy-Preserving Deep Learning for Multi-Tenant CRM Workflows: Integrating Homomorphic Encryption across Cloud Databases
authors: Abhaar Gupta, Tarun Vishwanath Chincholi, Supreet Nagi, Balaje Prasath Manoharan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platforms increasingly integrate deep learning models to support predictive analytics, personalization, and automated decision-making. In cloud-based, multi-tenant environments, these capabilities introduce significant privacy risks, as sensitive customer data from multiple tenants is processed on shared infrastructure. Conventional security mechanisms, such as encryption at rest and in transit, fail to protect data during model training and inference, where exposure risks are highest. Homomorphic encryption offers a promising approach by enabling computation directly on encrypted data, preserving confidentiality throughout the analytics lifecycle. However, integrating homomorphic encryption into deep learning workflows presents substantial challenges related to system architecture, performance overhead, and operational complexity. This paper examines privacy-preserving deep learning for multi-tenant CRM workflows through the lens of practical enterprise deployment. It analyzes architectural patterns for integrating homomorphic encryption across cloud databases, explores workflow-level design considerations, and evaluates trade-offs between privacy guarantees and system efficiency. By focusing on real-world CRM pipelines rather than purely cryptographic abstractions, the study provides actionable guidance for designing scalable, compliant, and privacy-aware deep learning systems in modern cloud environments.

title: Design and Application of a Smart Meter Real-Time Electricity Consumption Monitoring System Based on Big Data and Cloud Computing
authors: Zhenglei Zhou, Zhou Yang, Juntao Pan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: Under the background of new power system construction, smart meters place higher demands on real-time monitoring and analysis capabilities. This paper constructs an analysis method integrating big data processing and cloud computing architecture to meet the real-time electricity consumption monitoring needs of smart meters. Experimental results show that the method reduces the average end-to-end processing latency to 418ms and increases the throughput to 572 records/second ${ }^{-1}$; under continuous operation, the system availability reaches $99.3 \%$, and the abnormal event detection latency is stable in the 70–82s range. At the business application level, the alarm effectiveness rate increases to $93.6 \%$, the anomaly handling lead time reaches 22.3 minutes, and the need for manual intervention is significantly reduced. The constructed real-time electricity consumption monitoring method for smart meters demonstrates strong advantages in real-time performance, stability, and business value, and can provide effective technical support for the refined operation and intelligent management of power systems.

title: FPGA-Based Embedded ML Accelerator for Healthcare IoT Devices
authors: Jijumon Rajendran, Harishankar Sajeev Pillai, Dr. Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Health care is shifting very fast to wearable and portable medical devices like ECG monitors, pulse oximeters, and glucose meters. Such devices enable continuous real-time monitoring and quick diagnosis. Unfortunately, these are powered by low-power microcontrollers that have limited computational resources, thereby making it difficult to deploy large ML models. Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) makes it possible for AI models to function in such limited devices effectively by pruning and quantization. Nevertheless, in case the biomedical signals are complicated, TinyML may still face challenges in maintaining the required speed and energy efficiency.This work presents a simulated FPGA accelerator that supports TinyML models for healthcare IoT devices. The FPGA unit is the one that handles the parallel operations, thus, inference is getting faster and the device is becoming more energy-efficient. Performance metrics like latency and power are being measured through PYTHON analytics simulation and hardware-aware optimization.The theoretical investigation of the topic indicates that the adoption of FPGA-style acceleration can lead to a huge efficiency improvement when compared to CPU-only TinyML execution.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Geo-Tagged Traceability for Ayurveda Herbs: Ensuring Authenticity from Collection to Formulation
authors: Nandini K, Sharanu Mitta, Yashas Goutham, Yashwanth N, Kavyashree I Pattan, Arjun Krishnamurthy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Fragmented supply chains and adulteration make the authenticity and quality of Ayurvedic herbs a challenge. This work describes a blockchain-enabled geo-tagged traceability framework developed for the secure capture of data across the herbal value chain. The system starts at the point of collection when farmers and wild collectors are registered with unique IDs and precise geolocations that are recorded immutably. Then, processing units receive their unique identities, while the details of traditional Ayurvedic practices such as drying, grinding, or roasting the herbs are recorded as verifiable events. The microbial load, heavy metals, and pesticide residue tests are independent laboratory evaluation results anchored to the respective herb batches. The various manufacturers will also be registered, and details regarding the production of powders, churnas, or tablet formulation are stored on the ledger. Each transition is governed by smart contracts, making the validation tamper-proof. Finally, the QR codes on the final product labels enable consumers to verify the provenance, processing information, lab results, and identity of the manufacturer in real-time. By integrating blockchain, laboratory data and consumer-facing verification, the proposed system delivers transparent and reliable traceability for Ayurvedic herbs, strengthening consumer trust, supporting regulatory compliance and enhancing global acceptance of Ayurvedic formulations all while preserving the essence of traditional practices.

title: Early Detection of Cystic Fibrosis Using Multimodal Deep Learning on Chest X-ray and Genetic Data
authors: Saravanan. M. S, Siva Rama Krishna Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is a complex multi-organ genetic disorder with a clear molecular pathology that is based on mutational change on the CFTR gene resulting in thick mucus and frequent infections. Early diagnosis of CF is essential to enhance life expectancy and management. This paper presents a multimodal deep learning model that combines radiography images and genetic mutation data to detect CF at an early stage. The use of a Vision Transformer (ViT) with X-rays of the chest to extract features and a deep feedforward neural network with Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane conductance Regulator (CFTR) mutation vectors are made. The fusion of the data is done at a joint embedding layer, and then the data is classified. The data consists of 8501 X-ray images obtained with ChestX-ray14 and CheXpert, and artificially generated genetic profiles with CFTR2 mutation data. The multimodal model attained better results (94.6% accuracy, 92.3% sensitivity and 96.1% specificity) than unimodal baselines. The fusion approach is further confirmed by comparative experiments involving CNN and LSTM model. The findings demonstrate the promise of multimodal Artificial Intelligence to assist in earlier and more accurate CF diagnosis. Future efforts will consist of practical applications in clinical settings and further expansion to longitudinal data analysis.

title: Real Time Malware Detection using Explainable AI and Deep Learning
authors: Thasleena V.A, D. Narmadha, G. Naveen Sundar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The increasing volume of cyber threats and the intricacy of malware demand the approaches to detection become more sophisticated, transparent and in real-time. Traditional signature-based methods are inadequately suited for dealing with polymorphic or zero-day attacks, creating an immediate need for intelligent and explainable reasons. This review intends to explore the potential of utilizing Deep Learning (DL) and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) in real-time applications for malware detection, providing a balance between accuracy and explainability. Many DL models, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), and hybrid structured networks, are successful at extracting complex information about API calls, binary data, and network logs. There are also XAI methods, such as SHAP and LIME that allow for model reasoning. There are frameworks, such as XRGuard and GRU-LSTM that report accuracies of 99.9% and transparency. Overall, by combining aspects of DL and XAI; the performance, explanation, accountability, and trust of the model is enhanced, enabling progress to a more intelligent, explainable, and proactive cybersecurity system that has the ability to keep up with the regular evolution of malware in changing digital environments.

title: Design and Simulation of a 6-Blade Circular Slot Bandpass FSS for 28 GHz
authors: Sapna B A, K Ramasamy, P. Kalaivani, Lakshman Kumar R, Bhupanithi G, Devan Saroram U G
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This is a representation of a usage of 5G technologies particularly in 28GHz which we focused in this project, where we faced some challenges in electromagnetic frequencies. To improve electromagnetic frequency we made a compact structure and it is also used for filtering unwanted signals effectively. FSS are planar periodic structures which control electromagnetic waves which are used for transmission and reflection based on frequency we used a 6 blade circular slot geometry design for a compact bandpass and it also resonate at 28GHz in 5G mmWave application, we used 5.0 mm×5.0mm which helps in enabling dense periodic arrangements. The material used is an FR4 substrate, it is a material that is low cost and also useful for mmWave frequencies. This material can provide a practical response at 28GHz. For simulation we used Ansys HFSS, set boundary condition to model FSS array which is measured using S-parameters those are S11 and S21 these are used for evaluation. The insertion loss used to get transmission efficiency is considered by S21 and for reflection, study matching and rejection is done in S11 at 28GHz frequency. Where the FR4 demonstrate high losses we still demonstrate a clear and usable bandpass behaviour with the help of geometry. By using these materials we get a clear response within the cost range and also performs at high frequency, providing guidance for affordable filtering for future technologies.

title:
Demonstration of Tapered Fin Vertical GaN FinFET for Achieving < 0.01 mΩ.cm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    ON Resistance
authors: Ancy Michel, Angelin Delighta A, Navin M George, D. Nirmal
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Gallium Nitride emerged as a promising material for power electronics applications. Although, reducing channel resistance while sustaining the electrostatic control is the challenging one. In this work, a tapered fin vertical FinFET is proposed and analysed its DC characteristics. Two sample devices with different tapered angles are used and evaluated its electrical performance using TCAD simulator followed by a comparison with the conventional rectangular FinFET. The tapered FinFET demonstrated an improved-ON current of 6.8 kA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> with a specific ON resistance of 0.008 mΩ.cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and a breakdown voltage of 42 V. The ON-state performance is enhanced due to the increased conduction area at the fin base. This balanced-ON state performance and breakdown voltage make the device suitable for low power electronic applications.

title: Cloudburst AI: Orchestrating SRE-Driven AI Inference Across Multi-Cloud and Edge Zones
authors: Rishiraj Kohli, Mourya Chigurupati, Sivarama Krishna Akhil Koduri, Nagarjuna Nellutla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) workloads are increasingly deployed across heterogeneous infrastructures consisting of public clouds, private clouds, and edge computing environments. However, achieving reliable, cost-efficient, and scalable inference across these distributed environments remains challenging. This paper presents Cloudburst AI, a Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)-driven orchestration framework that automates AI inference placement, reliability governance, observability, failure resilience, and operational performance across multi-cloud and edge zones. The framework introduces reliability-centric orchestration policies, autonomous failover intelligence, adaptive routing based on latency and availability, and error-budget driven execution to ensure sustained Service Level Objectives (SLOs). Experimental results demonstrate reduced latency variance, improved availability, minimized failure blast radius, and cost aware inference stability. Cloudburst AI establishes a unified paradigm for AI SRE, bridging DevOps reliability engineering with scalable inference execution.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Optimization of AlN/GaN MOSHEMTs Performance: Predicting Device Characteristics Based on Geometric and Material Parameters
authors: M Manikandan, Ajayan J, K.Bikshalu, G Dhivyasri, Sreejith S, Padmavathi M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Machine Learning in the optimization of AlN/GaN MOSHEMTs (Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor High Electron Mobility Transistors) to be used in the high-power and RF switching applications. Using simulated data, the research evaluates the effect of the critical geometric and material parameters on the DC and RF properties of the device, including barrier thickness (t<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">b</inf>), oxide thickness (t<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ox</inf>), and the gate length (L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf>). The machine learning models, such as regression algorithms and neural networks, are used to estimate the influence of such parameters on performance measures, such as transconductance (G<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>), drain current (ID), saturation current (IDsat), and frequency (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>). A large set of simulations of devices with different dielectric materials (ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) and scaling effects is used to train the models. The findings give information about the correlation between the structural characteristics and device behaviour, thus facilitating the optimization of the design efficiently, and can be used as a potent instrument in furthering the production of the next-generation high-performance GaN-based transistor.

title: A Study on TMD as Channel Materials in Enhancing FET Characteristics
authors: Syed Mustafaa M, Kavitha V P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Short channel effects (SCEs), weaker electrostatic control and increased leakage current at advanced technology nodes significantly restrict the continual downscaling of silicon based field effect transistors (FETs). Two-dimensional (2D) transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), notably MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, WS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> have gained substantial attention as alternative channel materials because of their atomically thin body and favorable electrostatic characteristics. This study presents a comprehensive TCAD based examination of TMD channel FETs concentrating on their impact on key device parameters such as subthreshold swing (SS), drain induced barrier lowering (DIBL), threshold voltage stability and leakage current. Material properties acquired from density functional theory (DFT) computations are included into physics based TCAD and NEGF simulations to accurately describe carrier transport and electrostatics in ultra scaled devices. Simulation results consistently reveal that TMD based FETs have greater gate controllability and enhanced suppression of SCEs compared to conventional silicon equivalents, particularly at nanometer scale gate lengths. Furthermore, sophisticated device topologies such as gate all around and edge gated configurations significantly increase electrostatic integrity. The combined TCAD data demonstrate that TMD channel materials enable aggressive device scaling while preserving acceptable switching behavior making them potential options for future low power and nanoscale FET technologies.

title: Forecasting Agricultural Commodity Prices using LSTM–GRU Ensemble Networks: Evidence from Indian Crop Markets
authors: S. Dineshkumar, A.Immaculate Mercy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Agricultural commodity price forecasting plays a crucial role in increasing the incomes of farmers and sustaining the Indian national economy. Proper prediction of prices helps farmers make proper decisions at the right time of selling or storing their produce, thus maximizing their profit and reducing financial risk. A ensemble deep learning price prediction model consisting of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks was developed. Model training and testing are performed on the commodity price information acquired by the World Food Programme (WFP) Economic Market Monitor of the different Indian states. The LSTM element is a good representation of long-term temporal dependencies, whereas the GRU is an effective short and midterm price variation model with a lower computational cost. The ensemble structure combines trends of the global market and domestic price changes. The experimental outcomes indicate a predictive ability, with MAE = 0.3077, RMSE = 0.9283, and R 2 = 0.9912. Moreover, the trend movement accuracy of the model is 81.07, which proves the ability of the model to predict the price direction in a reliable manner and is important in decision-making in agricultural markets. The suggested strategy would offer a valid and viable forecasting model that could enable farmers, traders, and policy makers to plan and manage agricultural markets effectively.

title: AI-Driven Virtual Assistant with Immersive Experience
authors: Ravi Maddinapudi L, Sujatha C, Sree Niranjanaa B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Intelligent Virtual Assistants (IVAs) are advancing with multimodal AI, yet existing systems still struggle with contextual reasoning, emotion awareness, and natural interaction inside immersive environments. This research presents a hybrid IVA framework that integrates speech recognition, emotion analysis, and generative reasoning with a photorealistic 3D avatar for real-time XR interaction. The system combines Azure Cognitive Services for intent classification with a domain-adapted Mistral LLM for deeper contextual understanding, supported by a lightweight vision module for facial emotion detection. A viseme-synchronized avatar rendering pipeline produces lifelike conversational behavior, enabling seamless multimodal interaction through speech, gaze, and expressionsExperimental evaluation shows that the hybrid model improves intent accuracy, response coherence, and user engagement compared to traditional IVA architectures. The results demonstrate that integrating cognitive AI with immersive avatar-based visualization enhances realism and usability in training, technical support, and interactive assistance scenarios. This work provides a scalable foundation for next-generation, emotion-aware virtual assistants capable of delivering more human-like and context-adaptive interactions in XR environments.

title: Constraint System Extension and MultiDimensional Comparative Analysis of Power System Unit Combination Models
authors: Xiangqian Cheng, Yuhao Wu, Bingxi Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: Unit scheduling represents the core optimization task in the operational phase of modern power systems. The primary objective of unit optimization is to minimize operational costs by determining the start-up/shutdown status and output levels of thermal power units within a specified timeframe, while simultaneously meeting load demands and adhering to various constraints. It is evident that unit combination optimization falls within the realm of large-scale mixed-integer programming, where computational complexity increases exponentially with system scale. While traditional optimization algorithms can deliver viable solutions, they must balance modeling depth with practicality for engineering applications. Therefore, this paper constructs a classical unit portfolio optimization framework and conducts research in two phases. First, based on economic dispatch principles, a classical unit portfolio model is established. This model incorporates unit fuel costs and start-stop switching costs into the objective function while strictly defining five core constraints: power balance, upper and lower limits on unit output, minimum start-stop time, ramping rate, and start-stop logic. Traditional optimization tools such as Gurobi or CPLEX are employed for modeling and solving. Subsequently, the classical model is extended by incorporating constraints for Direct Current (DC) transmission capacity limitations, N-1 security contingency, rotating reserve capacity, and security inertia. The same solvers are used to solve the extended unit model. Differences between the classical and extended models are analyzed across three dimensions: operational costs, constraint satisfaction, and unit dispatch strategies. The research results validate the engineering rationality of the extended model. While ensuring the safe and stable operation of the power system, it generates unit dispatch plans that better align with actual grid operation requirements. This establishes a solid classical model foundation for the subsequent introduction of permanent magnet motor unit matching models.

title: Deep Learning and Multimodal AI for Healthcare Data Analysis and Patient Monitoring
authors: Manju George
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The traditional diagnostic methods employed within healthcare settings are usually based on a single-modality data approach. This results in fragmented clinical understanding. Therefore, this study attempts to overcome this limitation by exploring the application of deep learning with multimodal artificial intelligence to provide improved healthcare data analysis. A unified approach has been proposed that incorporates heterogeneous data sources to provide comprehensive understanding. The approach has been validated using a dataset containing 418 instances, which has been obtained from the Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care database. The implementation has been carried out within a Python environment using advanced neural network models that are supported by TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, along with performance evaluation using Scikit-learn. The results obtained within this study have shown that multimodal data fusion results in improved predictive accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity. This indicates that deep learning has been successful in capturing complex relationships among heterogeneous data sources. This shows that artificial intelligence-based continuous patient monitoring systems have the potential to provide improved diagnostic capabilities, which will be helpful in developing personalized healthcare services within modern healthcare settings.

title: Enterprise Secure AI Execution Engine: A Zero-Trust Security Gateway for Enterprise LLM Systems
authors: Kandula Krishna Chaitanya Reddy, Tiriveedhi Chandra Lekha, Thrisha P, Medam Arjun, K Radhika, P Naresh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Organizations are increasingly adopting Large Language Models (LLMs) for automated decision-making and employee support and secure handling of sensitive organizational data. The systems enabled by these technologies deliver substantial business productivity gains. The systems create major security problems because their implementation enables users to conduct prompt injection attacks and data exfiltration and privilege escalation and adversarial manipulation and policy override attacks. LLM systems operate through natural language interactions which create particular security problems because standard security methods cannot stop attackers who use designed prompts to access LLM systems. The paper introduces an Enterprise Secure AI Execution Engine which functions as a Zero-Trust AI Security Gateway that protects enterprise LLM deployments through its security framework. The proposed architecture combines multiple security mechanisms which include Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Data Loss Prevention (DLP) and adversarial prompt detection and deterministic tokenization to create a security system that enforces stringent access protection rules. The system considers all incoming user data to be untrusted and it requires several security checks before permitting model operation. The system demonstrated its capability to identify adversarial prompts while safeguarding sensitive data with minimal effect on processing efficiency. The results demonstrate the system effectively blocks major security threats while it maintains strong defenses against unauthorized data extraction attempts. The proposed framework establishes a secure deployment architecture for enterprise LLM systems and it works best for organizations that need to protect their most sensitive operations.

title: A Dual-Module Vision–Language AI Framework for Intelligent Decision Making in Smart Agriculture
authors: Suhas Bhat, Soumya Nandan Mishra, Sucheta Kolekar, Deepak Parashar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Providing timely and correct agricultural support to the farmers remains a major problem since not many people can access qualified knowledge and early diagnosis of the crops. A smart agriculture assistant powered by AI can allow farmers to get access to insights by both text-based and image-based agricultural query in a single interface. Image preprocessing and visual analysis are automated to process plant or crop images that could contain evidence of potential disease or crop conditions, whereas text queries are read to detect intent by the farmer in terms of treatment, fertilization, and prevention. The knowledge and recommendation repository is centralized and provides crop specific, disease symptoms and advisory information to aid in informed decision making. The system combines visual and written know-how to come up with easy-to-understand and farmer-friendly explanations and recommendations that are actionable. This strategy enhances the accessibility of agricultural advice, early intervention and leads to increased productivity and sustainable agricultural activities.

title: Smart Agriculture System for Tea Plant Disease Detection and Targeted Pesticide Recommendation
authors: J. Suji Priya, L. Bharath, M. Prince Chakkaravarthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Tea leaf cultivation has significant importance in the agricultural economies of many developing countries. However, tea leaf productivity is influenced by diseases like blister blight, red rust, bird’s eye spot, etc., and environmental factors like leaf scorch. Conventional disease detection techniques often include manual inspection, which is a time-consuming and labor-intensive process. Moreover, this technique may also lead to inaccurate results. In this research work, a novel approach has been proposed to develop a tea leaf disease detection and pesticide recommendation system using advanced deep learning concepts. In this work, the YOLO12 framework has been implemented to detect tea leaf diseases. This framework utilizes the advantage of both lightweight and strong YOLO models to detect tea leaf diseases in real-time with high accuracy. In this research work, a knowledge-based recommendation system has also been integrated to provide recommendations to the users based on the diseases detected. In this work, the proposed model has been trained using a diverse dataset of tea leaf images. An accuracy of 96.7% has been achieved by the proposed model, proving its capability to generalize accurately. In this work, a user-friendly web interface has been implemented to make the system accessible to users.

title: Dual-Camera Screen-Space Reflections
authors: Xinyu Du, Suguru Saito
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Screen-space ray tracing (SSR) are widely adopted in real-time rendering due to their favorable balance between visual quality and performance. However, because SSR relies on single screen-space information, it is limited to surfaces such as water and floors and cannot correctly handle front-facing reflective surfaces where the reflected objects may lie outside the camera view. To address this limitation, we propose a dualcamera screen-space ray tracing method(DCSSR). By introducing a secondary camera and adding one additional one set of Gbuffers, our approach enables objects located behind the viewer to be mirrored onto frontside specular surfaces while retaining the efficiency advantages of SSR. Furthermore, the method remains lightweight and is particularly suitable for image-based distributed rendering systems.

title: A Novel Hybrid Deep Learning and Machine Learning Framework for Medicinal Plant Classification Using Multi-Feature Fusion
authors: Anil Kumar Thandlam, T.Veerraju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Accurate identification of medicinal plant species is essential for applications in traditional medicine, pharmacognosy, agriculture, and biodiversity conservation. However, variations in leaf shape, texture, illumination, and background complexity make automated classification highly challenging. This study proposes a novel hybrid deep learning and machine learning framework that integrates end-to-end convolutional models, transfer learning, handcrafted descriptors, and ensemble classification techniques for robust medicinal plant recognition. The framework begins with a baseline CNN model, followed by advanced transfer learning using ResNet50, DenseNet121, and VGG16, achieving accuracies of 69.5%, 96.2%, 94.16%, and 89.30%, respectively. To further enhance discriminative capability, six complementary feature sets—two transfer learning embeddings (ResNet50 and VGG16) and four handcrafted descriptors (HOG, pHash, SIFT, ORB)—are extracted and concatenated into a unified multi-feature representation. Multiple machine learning classifiers are evaluated on the fused features, with Logistic Regression achieving 98.91% accuracy, outperforming Random Forest and XGBoost. Finally, a soft-voting ensemble of Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost delivers the best performance with 99.46% accuracy, 0.9954 F1-score, and excellent micro-averaged ROC-AUC. Experimental results confirm that the proposed multi-stage hybrid architecture significantly improves classification accuracy compared to standalone CNN or TL models. The framework demonstrates high robustness and scalability, providing a reliable solution for automated medicinal plant classification.

title: ALTRIOT: An IoT-based Intelligent System for Assisting and Monitoring of Alzheimer’s Disease
authors: Madumidha S, Kamalnath S K, Kumar B, Mukeshwaran A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that has a profound effect on the memory, intellectual, and daily living skills of elderly patients, resulting in a loss of autonomy and increased reliance on caregivers. Early diagnosis and continuous monitoring are essential for improving patient safety, quality of life, and slowing disease progression. Conventional diagnosis methods are highly dependent on clinical diagnosis, imaging, and regular monitoring, which are often time-consuming, costly, and inadequate for continuous health monitoring in a living environment. This paper introduces ALTRIOT, an Internet of Things (IoT)-based intelligent system for continuous health monitoring and early diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease. The proposed system combines biomedical sensors for health monitoring of physiological parameters like heart rate and body temperature, motion and distance sensors for activity monitoring and fall detection, embedded processing for local data analysis, and wireless communication for remote health monitoring. Moreover, a GPS-based location tracking module is also included to handle wandering, which is one of the most significant safety issues for Alzheimer’s patients.The ALTRIOT system is capable of analyzing the sensor data and sending real-time alerts during abnormal conditions, thus allowing caregivers to respond accordingly. The experimental results show that the proposed system improves the accuracy of real-time monitoring, reduces the burden on caregivers, and improves patient management. The outcome of the study reveals that the IoT-based intelligent monitoring system, ALTRIOT, is a reliable, scalable, and cost-effective solution for Alzheimer’s disease care.

title: Securing Organization and Employees: AI-Powered Cyber Security System for Malware Defense System
authors: Venkateswaran Radhakrishnan, S. Baghavathi Priya, B. Buvaneswari, Theetchenya S, Hamid Jadad, Chithik Raja Mohamed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The most harmful virus on the planet is ransomware and various related malware. Many countries were attacked with this malware. It is a high-risk threat to individual users, corporations, industries, and government organizations. If any computer or network has been compromised/infected with malware, the users’/organizations’ data will be encrypted, deleted, modified, or used by other people for their benefit. The cybercriminal who created this software, such as ransomware, requests money (ransom) in exchange for the decryption key that will allow individuals/organizations to access their encrypted files. Ransomware malware has different types; it depends on the effects on the user data on their system. It is very challenging for any person or organization to defend against this malware. This malware employs social engineering techniques such as, but not limited to, phishing emails, OS weakness and lack of anti-viruses, fake Wi-Fi connections, downloading files, and installed software and application vulnerabilities. Due to the advancement of information technology, users, companies, and government organizations are moving forward with their operations in the digital system. In this case, everyone should be aware of malware and how it affects their day-to-day transactions. To safeguard against these malware, they must ensure that data security is given top priority along with high-speed infrastructural facilities. The proposed design will solve all these kinds of malware or attacks to keep all PCs in the network in a safe zone.

title: Calculation of High-frequency Transformer Core Loss Considering the Temperature Characteristics of Soft Magnetic Materials
authors: Lei Wang, Xiaokai Wang, Wenle Song
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: High-frequency transformers (HFTs) usually operate under non-sinusoidal excitation, and harmonics cause additional losses in the transformer, resulting in an increase in the transformer’s temperature. Therefore, it is crucial to conduct calculations considering the temperature-dependent core losses of the transformer. For this reason, firstly a dynamic magnetic properties testing platform was established, and the magnetic properties of three types of soft magnetic materials (SMMs): ferrite, amorphous alloy, and nanocrystalline were compared and analyzed. Secondly, based on the measured loss values of the materials at different temperatures, a calculation method for core losses considering the temperature characteristics of SMMs was proposed. Finally, three-dimensional finite element simulation models of HFTs with core made of these three types of SMMs were built. The results show that the loss differences in different parts of the core vary significantly with and without considering the temperature characteristics.

title: Unsupervised Wearable Biosignal Anomaly Detection for Stress Monitoring using WESAD Dataset
authors: Haritha J, Logeswaran K, Sriram R, Umesh A
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal wearable biosignal anomaly detection system utilizing the WESAD dataset. A sensor fusion pipeline is implemented combining Heart Rate Variability (HRV) from the Blood Volume Pulse (BVP), skin conductance features from Electrodermal Activity (EDA), and motion patterns from accelerometer (ACC) data. Using an unsupervised Isolation Forest model, the detection of physiological stress and impact anomalies is targeted. A n d an accuracy of 82.02 % and an F1-score of 0.53 were achieved on Subject 10 (S10), demonstrating the viability of lightweight anomaly detection for real-time edge deployment. Then the Results are compared against state of the art supervised methods, and the trade-offs of unsupervised approaches in resource-constrained environments are discussed.

title: VERA: A Blockchain-Backed, Auditable ML–Human Framework for News Verification and Misinformation Mitigation
authors: Prabin Devkota, Aayush Mainali, Riwaj Adhikari, Sudha Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The paper introduces a model called VERA (Verifiable Evidence, Reputation, Accountability), a comprehensive, end-to-end, trustworthy news verification and misinformation reduction system that combines provable-first content processing with auditable hybrid (ML + human) judgments and incentive-compatible governance. VERA splits verification into 5 layers: (1) ingestion and Claim-Evidence Graph construction with cryptographic content identifiers; (2) a verification engine storing ModelCards and RunCards on-chain and capturing structured human rationales; (3) a hybrid ledger/storage layer with a permissioned consortium blockchain with content-addressable storage and periodic public checkpoints; (4) incentive, reputation, and governance (reputation-bound staking, slashing, topic-sensitive reputation vectors, quadratic voting with crisis controls); and (5) access and UX layer. The design addresses four endemic shortcomings present in the prior literature: the lack of incentive alignment, the absence of privacy guarantees, the auditing of ML-human lineage of decisions, and feasible deployment constraints (cost/latency/interoperability).

title: Graph Signal Processing-based EEG Analysis with Quantum Machine Learning for Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Pentapati Kalyan Babu, Chandrabhotla Sridhara Syam, Jata Nikhilesh, Ballipadu Rishyan, Gubbala Adi Shankar, M.Srikanth
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The timely intervention in Alzheimer disease (AD) requires early detection, which is difficult to achieve in conventional machine and deep learning models because the brain networks have a complex functional connectivity and nonlinear dynamics. To mitigate this shortcoming, this paper advances a topology-conscious framework of spectral graph signal processing (GSP) and quantum machine learning (QML) to EEG-based early AD detection. Phase-locking value (PLV) was used to model multichannel EEG data of 162 (AD, mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and healthy controls) as functional networks of connectivity. Features of spectral graphs that are obtained through graph fourier transform can maintain the topology of a network and are encoded into a variational quantum classifier to improve nonlinear separability. The proposed method has accuracy of 94.6, sensitivity of 93.2, specificity of 95.8 and an AUC of 0.968, which is better than the conventional models. The analysis of ablation proves the complementary roles of the GSP and QML. The framework shows that it has a high potential to be used as a scalable and efficient solution to next-generation EEG-based neurodiagnostic.

title: Electric Field Engineering and Breakdown Voltage Enhancement in Dual Field-Plate AlGaN/GaN HEMTs: A Modelling and Simulation Approach for High-Power VLSI Systems
authors: Padmavathi M, Manikandan M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: The article gives the detailed & exhaustive analyses & the simulations w.r.t. Aluminum Gallium Nitrides (AlGaN), Gallium Nitrides (GaN), the higher electronical mobility dependent transistors abbreviated as (HeMT) which is involving an new dual fielded type of plates [FP] structures. In spite of the GaN device which offer good break-down volts & the saturation limits of the velocity in comparision to the Si element, they are suffering from large electric field which get crowded near the edges of the gate, thus it would lead to pre-mature break-down on the current & voltage collapses. The research presented here is proposing an source type of connected dual field plate designed for re-distributing the peak of the electric field voltage. By the usage of a calibration model developed by mathematical means & high level simulation using sim tool, the demonstration of a vital improvements in the breakdown volts [VBR] and the Baliga Figs of Merit [BFoM]. The sim results are indicating a 468percentage of increase in the break-down volts from 220 to 1250 volts & a suppressing of the collapse of the current (i) phenomenon, thus the developed device would be stable to a very high level of operations, which would suit higher powered applications in the switching process of the 5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> Generation of Power Amplifier.

title: Architecture and Implementation of O-RAN-based Local Breakout Service for Wi-Fi Devices
authors: Wan-Ru Yao, Jui-Tang Wang, Yu-Hung Cheng, Arturas Medeisis
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: This study proposes a 5G local breakout (LBO) service for Wi-Fi devices, integrated into the O-RAN architecture and combined with edge computing to achieve local offloading within 5G private networks. The proposed IoT Networking Delivery (IND) system utilizes routing table updates and tunnel switching techniques to implement local breakout for Wi-Fi devices. By directing specific types of traffic from the local network directly to corresponding services, this approach effectively reduces transmission latency and alleviates the burden on the 5G core network. Experimental results demonstrate that the IND system significantly improves performance; compared to traditional LBO methods via the User Plane Function (UPF), the IND system reduces the average Round-Trip Time (RTT) by approximately 3 ms. Under a high-load scenario of 6,000 packets per second (PPS), the system further demonstrates its efficiency by reducing latency by up to 4 ms. These findings indicate that the proposed architecture provides a low-latency and cost-effective solution for multi-access integration in 5G networks.

title: Causality-aware Dual Graph Approach for Multi-cloud Networking Forecasting
authors: Yongcan Luo, Jiahao Zheng, Shuxin Qin, Tianyi Chen, Dapeng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multi-cloud networking enables enterprises to orchestrate applications across heterogeneous cloud platforms, offering improved scalability, resilience, and cost-effectiveness. However, complex interactions among distributed cloud resources introduce intricate interdependencies, particularly through inter-VM traffic flows and shared network pathways. Current forecasting methods for system metrics often overlook these fine-grained traffic dynamics and the causal relationships inherent in multi-cloud environments, failing to capture how resource usage in one VM can influence others. In this paper, we propose a causality-aware dual-graph neural network designed explicitly for multivariate system metric forecasting in multi-cloud settings. Our framework employs an adaptive dual-graph architecture to simultaneously model spatial dependencies among metrics across different VMs and temporal dependencies within individual VM metrics. To explicitly encode directional causal relationships, we construct a Transfer Entropy-based causality matrix optimized through Bayesian search. A causally guided fusion module integrates these representations using contrastive and triplet learning, aligning latent spaces to emphasize causality-aware structures. Extensive experiments on real-world multi-cloud traces demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms strong spatio-temporal and Transformer-based forecasting baselines, achieving relative error reductions of at least 8% (across MAE/RMSE/MAPE) in the 15-VM setting.

title: Physics-Constrained Pose Refinement of Stacked Objects via a Hierarchical Support Graph
authors: Daohui Liu, Chen Sun, Zheng Gong, Jiong Yang, Xingjian Liu, Yu Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Physics-constrained refinement is a technique for correcting inaccurate 6-D pose estimates. A key challenge, however, is to identify which estimates to refine when a physically implausible state, such as inter-penetration, occurs between multiple objects. This is because the implausible state may stem from a single inaccurate estimate or from all of them. To resolve the ambiguity of which pose to refine, existing methods anchor the process to a single, predefined support, such as a tabletop. However, this method does not apply to stacked scenarios, where objects lack direct contact with this predefined support. To address this limitation, we introduce a method that infers physical supports for each object by constructing a hierarchical support graph. Specifically, this method first validates pose estimates against a primary support, such as a tabletop, and then uses the validated estimates as second-level supports for objects stacked on them. Based on the support relations defined in this graph, a joint cost function is formulated to drive the joint refinement of all estimated poses to physically plausible states. To prevent errors in the hierarchy from propagating, a visual consistency term is included in the cost function, allowing the refined poses to remain grounded in visual information. Experimental results confirmed that our approach is capable of refining pose estimates in various stacked scenes, with its performance validated on the T-LESS benchmark and a custom dataset.
