title: Eliminating the Bit Error Floor of Polarization Modulation by Using Wireless Friendly Building Materials
authors: Gang Yu, Yixin Zhang, Xin Dong, Wenji Xi, Lingyou Zhou, Yu Zheng, Jiliang Zhang, Jie Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: In this paper, we commence with the first principles of wave propagation and polarization characteristics during reflection and transmission, and then propose a ray tracing algorithm that can fully capture polarization properties for simulated paths. Simulation results reveal that conventional cross-polarized ratio (XPR) models for multipath components (MPCs), which assume a consistent average value, do not align well with reality. Additionally, the assumption of a constant XPR tends to either overstate or understate the impact of depolarization. This misrepresentation, combined with inaccurate modeling of depolarization in traditional channel models, leads to degraded performance of polarization shift keying (PolarSK) in wireless communication systems. Thirdly, the Average Bit Error Probability (ABEP) performance of different PolarSK modulation configurations is analyzed based on the data from the proposed ray tracing model under various system configurations. These analyses show that indoor building materials with strong depolarization effects lead to irreducible ABEP floors, which cannot be resolved by increasing diversity gain or adjusting constellation diagram. Finally, in order to mitigate these ABEP floors, we introduce an iterative algorithm designed to minimize the floor effect of the ABEP in PolarSK by simultaneously optimizing the complex dielectric constant and thickness of each layer in the multi-layer building material. Numerical results indicate that, for a target ABEP on the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup>, the proposed algorithm achieves a substantial signal to noise ratio (SNR) gain of up to approximately 25 dB, and for different constellation diagrams and different MIMO systems, the ABEP floors are completely eliminated within the considered SNR range, without any SNR penalty. At the same time, we provide a detailed explanation of the physical causes behind the occurrence of the ABEP floor effect. Overall, we establish building wireless performance (BWP) as a fundamental and quantifiable design objective for indoor environments and, by focusing on indoor polarized wireless channels, provide practical design guidelines while revealing that their achievable performance is inherently constrained by building material characteristics and architectural structures, thereby enabling wireless friendly indoor building and channel design toward 6G.

title: Adaptive Disinformation Diffusion on Networks With Heterogeneous Node and Link Dynamics
authors: Alice Nanyanzi, Sridhar Radhakrishnan, Kash Barker, Venu Sirisanagandla, Weinan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: This article introduces a novel adaptive diffusion model for disinformation propagation that captures temporally evolving, history dependent influence between node pairs. Unlike classical diffusion models, such as independent cascade, threshold, and compartmental models, which assume fixed or memoryless transmission probabilities, our approach assigns each directed link a time-varying influence strength <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\theta_{ij}(t)$</tex-math></inline-formula>. This parameter represents the dynamic influence strength from source <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$i$</tex-math></inline-formula> (disinformed node) to target <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$j$</tex-math></inline-formula> (susceptible node), modulating how past exposures shape future adoption likelihood. The influence strength evolves through state-dependent reinforcement and decay: sustained exposure from an actively disinformed node strengthens <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\theta_{ij}(t)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, while inactivity or countervailing signals induce relaxation. At the node level, belief accumulates through a leaky thresholded response to aggregated neighbor influence, so adoption emerges from cumulative exposure rather than isolated contact. This coupled mechanism enables influence strength, susceptibility, and belief to co-evolve over time. As a result, the model captures diffusion patterns that are difficult to reproduce within static transmission frameworks, including delayed activation, nonlinear reinforcement, echo-chamber amplification, and saturation shaped by structural bottlenecks. By systematically varying adaptive parameters, we demonstrate a spectrum of diffusion regimes ranging from slow persuasion to abrupt tipping and structurally constrained spread. Overall, the proposed model provides a mechanistic and history-informed representation of disinformation diffusion and offers a foundation for improved detection and intervention strategies.

title: New Approach to Small-Signal Parameter Extraction for GaN HEMTs up to Ku-Band
authors: Deepti Mongia, Dhananjay Rana, Kirti Bansal, Subhash Chander, Devendra Verma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: A new small-signal modeling approach applied to GaN HEMTs is presented. This approach addresses the challenges associated with numerical optimization of equivalent small-signal parameters using broadband S-parameter data, namely sensitivity to initial conditions, selection of starting values for extrinsic pad capacitances, problem of local minima in optimization algorithms and non-uniqueness of extracted small-signal parameters. The validity of the proposed small-signal modeling approach and equivalent circuit is verified by comparing the simulated wide-band small-signal S-parameters with measured S-parameters of indigenous 0.25 µm AlGaN/GaN HEMTs of 2×150 µm and 10×175 µm sizes.

title: RTL to GDS Implementation of a RISC-V SoC with Integrated Timer using Openlane Framework
authors: Gavaskar K, Shivappriya S N, Ishwerya V, Kanagadharshan V, Elango S, Anbumani V
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This paper describes the development and physical execution of a 32-bit RISC-V System-on-Chip (SoC) that incorporates a timer with programming unit utilizing a fully open-source RTL-to-GDSII design pipeline. Due to its open, modular, and license-free nature, the RISC-V architecture has emerged as a viable alternative to proprietary CPU designs. In order to solve these problems, a memory-mapped clock that can produce frequent signals for real-time management is combined with a tiny RISC-V core that supports the RV32I core instruction set. The entire SoC is built with the Open Lane streamlining structure and the Sky130 130 nm CMOS process design kit. Operational validity is tested using cycle-precise simulations, which ensure suitable command processing and dependable interrupt management. Logic synthesis generates an area-saving gate-level architecture translated to Sky130 standard cells. The final GDSII output shows that a fully operational RISC-V SoC can be built with only freeware tools, demonstrating the potential of open hardware design for embedded and industrial applications.

title: When AI Manages Memory: Q-Learning Based Adaptive Page Replacement
authors: Rehan George Mathew, S L Richris, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: As modern computing workloads have drastically increased in data-intensiveness, heterogeneity, and latency-sensitivity, memory management has become the primary source of bottlenecks in the operating system’s performance. Although page replacement algorithms such as First-In-First-Out (FIFO), Least Recently Used (LRU), and CLOCK are considered outdated, they are still mostly used because of their simplicity and predictability. Nevertheless, the predetermined decision logic of these algorithms render them unable to manage the dynamically changing access patterns which, for instance, could be normal for cloud analytics, containerized microservices, and machine learning inference systems.The present study is a clear and understandable RL system for adaptive page replacement based on Q-Learning. The system keeps metrics—page-fault rate, reuse distance, and working set size—performing one single run from the three algorithms FIFO, LRU, and CLOCK it chooses the best policy. Through online temporal-difference learning, it gradually adjusts its decision to a workload variation and hence, it supports the operation in a self-adaptive mode that is not affected by kernel latency constraints.The Q-Learning model introduced is a good example of a fully transparent system as compared to deep neural models. This is because it has a short, human-friendly Q-table that clearly shows the connection between workload states and the replacement actions. The system is thus theoretically evaluated to have decision complexity of O(1), to use a small memory, and to have less than 3% computational overhead. The results rank reinforcement learning as a feasible, understandable, and a compromise solution between static heuristics and black-box data-driven models, therefore, enabling the next generation of real-time memory management in modern operating systems to be almost a reality.

title: Logofy: A Prompt-Based Intelligent Logo Generator for Non-Designers
authors: Niranjan Desai, Shreyas Patil, Tharun V S, Parasnath D, Prof. Vaishali Y Parab
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Generative AI has revolutionized creative content, but many tools remain inaccessible to non-experts. The project presents a full-stack AI-powered logo generator that converts natural language prompts into original, professional logos instantly. Built with Hugging Face diffusion models and a modern tech stack (Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, Firebase), it offers an intuitive interface that requires no design or prompt engineering skills. This platform democratizes branding for individuals and small businesses, providing high-quality design on demand. Beyond logos, it serves as a scalable model for AI-driven creativity in marketing, product design, and personalized content creation.

title: Real-Time Classroom Engagement and Interaction Monitoring Using YOLOv5
authors: M. Gokuldhev, Muhammed Sinan P, Krishna Pratap Dwivedi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This research demonstrates a highly developed vision-based monitoring framework of the classroom that com-bines YOLOv5 object detection with DeepSORT for tracking multiple objects in monitoring student interaction and engagement in real time. The general idea is to develop a non-intrusive, computerized system that would automatically analyze the behavior of the class from behavioral as well as emotional clues obtained from live video streams. The developed model detects each student, follows the activity of each student during the session, and categorizes the level of engagement based on visual cues such as the direction of attention, facial expressions, and participation gestures.To facilitate interpretability, an interactive analytics dashboard is created that presents engagement measures, student and group performance, and temporal sequences of classroom attentions. The visualization allows educators to derive actionable insights on detecting disengaged students and adapting their teaching strategy in real time. The architectural approach is focused on **edge computing deployment** such that real-time computing can be done locally on devices with efficient execution without using any cloud facility, ensuring data confidentiality and minimal latency.A custom-annotated classroom dataset was developed and collected with great care in order to train and test the model, emulating various student behaviors, learning patterns, and classroom conditions through realistic but controlled scenarios. Experimental results show that the new system can reach high accuracy, strong robustness, and real-time responsiveness and can be a realistic solution to current smart classrooms as well as educational analytics applications.

title: Image Encryption Technology Based on Fractal Image Compression Algorithm
authors: Karan A, Karthickkameshwar S, Dhandapani Samiappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Image security and efficient storage have become critical challenges with the exponential growth of digital multimedia. The computational cost for traditional methods of encryption, especially with large image datasets, is high and significantly affects storage efficiency. In this work, fractal image compression is integrated with encryption techniques to make images secure and compact. First, a fractal image compression algorithm is applied to the proposed system for reducing the size of an image without significant quality degradation, followed by a robust encryption mechanism in order to protect sensitive information. The foremost aim is to ensure high-level security with the view of providing quick, efficient storage and faster transmission of images across networks. Its novelty lies in that fractals could leverage the self-similarity property to support simultaneous compression and encryption with very minimal processing overhead compared to conventional methods. Experimental results demonstrated that the system achieved superior compression ratios alongside strong cryptographic protection, suitable for secure multimedia transmission over bandwidth-constrained environments.

title: Low Latency Drowning Prevention: Design and Implementation of a Smart Safety Belt with Autonomous, Sensor-Fusion-Triggered Inflation
authors: Parimelazhagan S, Thirumoorthy S M, Karthikeyan M V, Tephillah S, Silva Deena J
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Drowning has been one of the leading causes of accidental death among the aquatic environments, especially in unsupervised or open-water areas. The paper describes the design and execution of a wearable smart safety belt, called Rescue Air, which is a detection system of possible drowning incidents, and it is composed of a water-immersion sensor, an MPU6050 inertial measurement unit (IMU), and GPS positioning. Detection algorithm is decided by rule driven decision logic and prolonged immersion time and abnormal movement patterns. When the distress conditions are confirmed, a solenoid operated 33g CO 2 cartridge inflates an airbag of 16.8L to offer about 160N of buoyancy. The system was tested in controlled experimental conditions in order to quantify latency of detection and reliability of its functioning. Findings show that the suggested prototype is capable of deploying the airbag in less than 2 seconds of the distress signal being received. The system is developed on a low-power microcontroller platform, and also provides the manual override features together with the inherent safety protection of the system to ensure safe functioning.

title: Sketch-Based 3D Anime-Style Character Generation with Geometry Guidance
authors: Kai Niu, Bofei Huang, Haoran Xie
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Multi-view image generation is crucial for 3D reconstruction, yet diffusion methods often lack strict perspective and tend toward near-orthographic views. This may lead to inaccurate camera pose estimation with severe artifacts and misalignment. Especially for anime-style images, geometry details cannot be faithfully reconstructed, such as faces, hair, and clothing. We propose a novel sketch-based 3D generation framework for 3D anime-style characters from a sketch using a feed-forward reconstruction stage built on synthesized multi-view images. A coarse geometry produced by a 3D generation model constrains the reconstruction stage to recover accurate geometry while preserving texture quality. For our constructed anime character dataset, the proposed method outperforms single-model baselines with high user satisfaction, highlighting the promise of combining generation with reconstruction for anime-style 3D reconstruction.

title: Design and Testing of Anti-Pumping Circuit for High-Voltage Circuit Breaker
authors: Chaoyu Chen, Qingbiao Zhan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: High-voltage circuit breakers play a critical role in the power system, and their anti-pumping circuit is an important protective measure to prevent the circuit breaker from repeatedly “closing opening” fault cycles due to the continuous existence of closing commands or contact adhesion. On the basis of analyzing the design requirements of circuit breaker control circuits, this article compares and studies the working principles, structural characteristics, and engineering applicability of traditional circuit breaker mechanism box antipumping circuits and innovative design protection device antipumping circuits. Through practical cases and experimental data analysis, the key impact of the matching between the action time of anti-pumping relays and the opening and closing time of circuit breakers on circuit reliability was revealed, and engineering suggestions were proposed to follow the principle of “single anti-pumping” and avoid using two sets of anti-pumping circuits together. At the same time, the system elaborates on the testing methods and common problem handling of antipumping circuits, providing technical reference and practical guidance for improving the operational reliability of highvoltage circuit breakers.

title: Automated Testbed for Repeatable Evaluation of Ultra-Wideband Localization Performance
authors: Alexander Kemptner, Julian Karoliny, Hannah Brunner, Andreas Gaich, Michael Neubauer, Fjolla Ademaj-Berisha, Filippo Casamassima, Walther Pachler, Shrief Rizkalla, Harald Witschnig, Andreas Springer, Hans-Peter Bernhard
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Testing Ultra-Wideband (UWB) systems is challenging, as multiple devices need to coordinate over lossy links and the systems' behavior is influenced by timing, synchronization, and environmental factors. Traditional testing is often insufficient to capture these complex interactions, highlighting the need for an overarching testbed infrastructure that can manage devices, control the environment, and make measurements and test scenarios repeatable. In this work, we present a highly automated testbed architecture built on Robot Operating System Version 2, integrating device management with environmental control and measurement systems. It includes an optical reference system, a controllable Autonomous Guided Vehicle to position devices within the environment, and time synchronization via Network Time Protocol (NTP). The testbed achieves a Root Mean Squared Error of 4.8 mm for positioning repeatability and 0.493° for the orientation, and our NTP-based synchronization approach achieves a timing accuracy of below 1 ms. All testbed functionality can be controlled remotely through simple Python scripts to allow automated orchestration tasks such as conducting complex measurement scenarios. We demonstrate this with a measurement campaign on UWB localization, showing how it enables repeatable, observable, and fully controlled wireless experiments.

title: Development of an Information-based Supply Chain Optimization System for Abaca Farmers and Traders
authors: Nancy P. Jacosalem, Jocelyn O. Balolot, Jade G. Cuartero, Michelle C. Elape, Jeanette M. Eborda, James Cloyd M. Bustillo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The abaca fiber industry in Barangay Culiram, Talacogon, Agusan del Sur, is a vital economic driver for local farmers and traders. However, the sector is currently hindered by fragmented supply chain processes, reliance on manual record-keeping, and restricted market access. This study addresses these challenges through the development of the E.A.S.Y. ABACA system, an information-based web optimization platform designed to automate farmer profiling, enhance production monitoring, and improve market transparency. Utilizing the Waterfall Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), the system was engineered using ReactJS, Node.js, and MySQL. Data were gathered through a mixed-methods approach involving 150 stakeholders, including farmers, traders, and agricultural officers. Evaluation results yielded an average weighted mean of 4.28 ("Very Good") on a five-point Likert scale across all system objectives. The findings demonstrate that the platform significantly improves data accuracy, facilitates informed decision-making, and fosters a more equitable and competitive agricultural economy by providing real-time market pricing and efficient supply chain oversight.

title: Machine Learning-based Stroke Detection using CT and MRI Imaging
authors: Hariharan R, Ruthrapathi M, Sanjay K, Srikesh S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Differentiating between ischemic and hemorrhagic stroke is important, as both are major causes of death and disability. With the development of medical imaging, machine learning (ML) is also applied to stroke detection and triage. This work focuses on recent advances in the machine learning methods used for stroke detection that appeared from 2020 till 2025 in CT/MRI images. In this work, we analyze and compare the evolution of deep-learning-based architectures, such as CNN, U-Net, transformers, and hybrid ensembles, from classical classifiers with handcrafted modalities. This review is based on how to categorize lesion types, localize hemorrhage, and isolate ischemic infarctions, as well as combined imaging strategies. Some recent advances like transfer learning, low-power CNNs for edge applications, uncertainty-aware ideas, and explainable AI for improved clinical interpretation are also discussed. But there still exist many challenges, including domain shifts across scanners, multi-center validation limited in scale, interpretability lacking in a model, and easy association with patient records under the privacy constraint. This review details methodological advancements and unresolved challenges associated with the above-mentioned methodologies. We view the ability to recognize strokes with ML as a key enabler that can help emerging needs like real-time, robust, and equitable diagnostic support systems toward ultimately beginning to deploy AI in acute stroke management.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Energy Demand Forecasting for Sustainable Hospital Infrastructure: A Predictive Framework for Operational Efficiency and Carbon Footprint Reduction
authors: K. M. Zubair, Gazi Arman Islam, Atkea Fauzia Eva, Muhammad Shayan Nafees, Md. Maniruzzaman, Sudoy Kumer Ghosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The growing power usage in clinical institutions, necessitated by unpredictable patient times and heavy usage of medical equipment, requires a high level of predictive models of sustainable operation. This paper is a machine learning forecasting approach to predicting the daily hospital energy demand incorporating environment parameters, occupancy data and equipment availability. The historical data were extensively preprocessed by outlier-detection, Min-Max-normalization and missing-value imputation. Recursive Feature Elimination was made to determine important predictors, which made it possible to compare the algorithms of Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Ridge Regression. Gradient Boosting had a better result of MAE of 0.103 kWh and R2 of 0.489 indicating that the gradient boosting is better suited to capture the temporal and operation dynamics. The analysis performed on a scenario basis showed that possible energy savings could be 12 percent by means of optimized scheduling of HVAC and load control. The model allows the preemptive resource distribution, which lowers the cost of operation and carbon intensity of healthcare infrastructure. Findings confirm the practicability of forecasting based on machines learning in real-time energy management, which underlies decarbonization agendas and sustainable operations of the facility in resource-restricted settings.

title: Predictive Maintenance of Brushless DC Motors using IoT-Enabled One-Dimensional CNN
authors: J. Punitha Nicholine, Vijayavivinya M, Sivayazhini P, Sivaranjani R, D. Suresh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Brushless DC (BLDC) motors are widely used in modern industrial applications due to their high efficiency and reliability; however, unexpected bearing failures and cyber threats in connected systems can lead to significant downtime and operational losses. To address these challenges, this work proposes an Internet of Things (IoT)–enabled intelligent framework for real-time fault diagnosis and cyber anomaly detection in BLDC motors. The system employs tri-axial accelerometers to continuously acquire vibration signals under varying speed and load conditions, reflecting realistic industrial environments. The collected data are transmitted through an IoT communication layer to enable centralized monitoring and real-time analysis. To enhance data quality, vibration signals undergo preprocessing steps including noise filtering, normalization, and segmentation into fixed-length windows. A one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D CNN) is then designed to automatically extract discriminative features directly from raw vibration signals without manual feature engineering. The model is trained using labeled data representing healthy operation, inner bearing faults, outer bearing faults, and malicious data injection. Efficient training is achieved using adaptive optimization and regularization techniques to ensure robustness and generalization. The proposed framework is evaluated using unseen test data across different operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high classification accuracy, stable precision and recall, and low inference latency, outperforming traditional machine learning methods. The low computational complexity and fast response time confirm the suitability of the system for real-time deployment in IoT-based environments. Overall, the proposed framework effectively supports predictive maintenance by enabling early fault detection, reducing unplanned downtime and maintenance costs, and improving system resilience against cyber anomalies. The solution is well suited for smart manufacturing, automated production lines, and Industry 4.0-based motor monitoring applications.

title: TechnoBot Pro: Design and Implementation of an AI-Enabled Interactive Smart Service Robot
authors: S. Kavitha, K.B. Raghavi, S.C. Sabarna, S. Sri Siva Sangari
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of TechnoBot Pro, an AI-enabled interactive smart service robot developed for automated reception and assistance applications. The proposed system integrates embedded hardware control, deep learning–based speech interaction, contactless water dispensing, and environmental monitoring into a unified robotic platform. The architecture follows a dual-controller approach comprising an ESP32-based customized PCB for real-time hardware control and a Raspberry Pi unit for artificial intelligence and human–machine interaction processing. The robot employs a CNN–RNN hybrid deep learning model for automatic speech recognition, enabling intelligent two-way communication with users. Convolutional Neural Networks extract spectral features from speech signals, while Recurrent Neural Networks model temporal dependencies for accurate speech-to-text conversion. Natural language understanding and response generation modules allow the robot to interpret user intent and provide appropriate responses through neural text-to-speech synthesis. In addition to AI-based interaction, the system includes mobile application-based navigation control, ultrasonic sensor-based human detection for automated greeting, and an IR sensor-controlled contactless water dispensing mechanism. A dedicated LCD module displays real-time temperature data measured through an embedded temperature sensor, ensuring independent environmental monitoring. The proposed system demonstrates a practical implementation of AI-driven service robotics using cost-effective embedded hardware. TechnoBot Pro provides a scalable, portable, and intelligent solution suitable for deployment in smart institutional environments such as colleges, hospitals, and corporate offices.

title: An Intelligent DVFS-Enabled VLSI Framework for Ultra-Low-Latency IoT Systems
authors: S. Kirthika Shree, Aravind T, R. Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) applications requires energy-efficient Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) architectures capable of supporting dynamic workloads with minimal communication delay. This study proposes an Adaptive Latency-Driven Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (ALD-DVFS) algorithm to improve the performance and energy efficiency of IoT nodes. The proposed method dynamically adjusts supply voltage and operating frequency based on real-time system parameters such as communication latency, traffic intensity, and computational workload. By continuously monitoring these metrics, the algorithm selects an optimal voltage–frequency pair that minimizes end-to-end delay while reducing dynamic and leakage power consumption. Unlike conventional static power management techniques, the adaptive approach enables rapid scaling during heavy traffic conditions and energy-saving operation during idle or low-load periods, ensuring reliable quality-of-service (QoS). The architecture is designed with lightweight control logic to reduce hardware overhead, making it suitable for resource-constrained IoT devices. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ALD-DVFS approach significantly reduces latency and power consumption compared with traditional fixed-frequency and static DVFS methods..

title: Evaluator@Pi: A Secure Web-Based Upward Evaluation System for Research Institute Leadership Assessment
authors: Mohd Rafizi Rahmad, Ihsan Mohd Yassin, Norazah Abdul Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This paper presents the design, implementation, and deployment of Evaluator@PI, a secure web-based upward evaluation system developed for Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) to enable grassroots staff to provide structured feedback on research institute leadership. The system employs a three-tier architecture built on PHP 8.2 and MySQL 8.4, incorporating security measures including bcrypt password hashing, PDO prepared statements for SQL injection prevention, and HTTPonly session cookies. The relational database schema follows an Entity-Attribute-Value (EAV) pattern across six normalized tables with referential integrity enforced through foreign key constraints. The system was pilot-tested at UiTM's Research and Innovation Department (JPI) for the 2025 evaluation cycle, spanning 21 departments with 157 registered users and collecting 1,144 rating responses from 44 evaluators assessing 15 managers. Results indicate high instrument reliability with Cronbach's alpha values exceeding 0.94 across all three evaluation categories, an overall mean rating of 4.41 out of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5(S D=0.72)$</tex>, and a Net Promoter Score of 27.3. This paper details the system architecture, database design, security framework, and user interface design, alongside a statistical analysis of the pilot deployment data.

title: Quantum-Inspired Digital Watermarking for Forensic Evidence Authentication using BB84 Protocol
authors: V. S. Gopthreya, Sreelakshmi R Syam, Kurunandan Jain, Prabhakar Krishnan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Digital media has always been prone to attacks like unauthorized modification, tampering, interception etc. Current watermarking schemes rely on classical key distribution schemes and hence are vulnerable to attacks like eavesdropping and quantum attacks. A method to integrate BB84 based key generation and DCT based embedding is proposed in this paper. The symmetric keys are generated using BB84 scheme and these keys are then used to encrypt the forensic metadata before embedding. Both key exchange process and watermark verification stages are secured. Under standard forensic processing—JPEG compression (QF 30-90), Gaussian noise(variance 0.01), and 30% cropping—watermarks remained detectable with PSNR exceeding 42 dB and SSIM above 0.98.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a 2.45 GHz Circular Patch Microstrip Antenna for Wireless Sensor Networks and IoT Applications
authors: Sourav Patra, Arpita Pawar, Deep Patil, Yash Mitkari, Sreedevi Nair, Sreekala Nair
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The exponential surge in wireless infrastructure, specifically within the realms of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Wireless Personal Area Networks (WPANs), has driven the requirement for radiating components that are small, low-profile, and economical. This study presents the systematic development, simulation, and performance evaluation of a Circular Patch Microstrip Antenna (CPMA) tailored for the 2.45 GHz Industrial, Scientific, and Medical (ISM) spectrum. Utilizing the Ansys High-Frequency Structure Simulator (HFSS), the antenna is constructed upon an FR-4 epoxy substrate. The primary objective is to maximize essential parameters such as radiation efficiency, gain, and impedance matching. Simulation data indicates a resonant return loss of –12.80 dB, which signifies a substantial reduction in signal reflection. Additionally, the proposed design exhibits a Voltage Standing Wave Ratio (VSWR) of 1.12 and provides an operational bandwidth of 80 MHz, adequately encompassing standard Bluetooth and Wi-Fi channels. With a peak gain of 4.5 dBi and a consistent broadside radiation pattern, this antenna architecture is a strong candidate for integration into miniaturized smart sensor nodes and wireless modules.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for Missing Meteorological Data Estimation in Nuclear Applications
authors: O.Sampath, C.Venkataswamy, B.Jonah Eric Franklin, S. Fayaz, G.Sri Charan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The proper and efficient operation of nuclear power plants is subject to proper meteorological information which is accurate and continuous. A typical feature though of sensor failures or transmission issues is loss of recording of values, especially of those that are of importance to both water availability and operation of the cooling system, such as the depth of the snowpack. It looks at how machine learning (ML) can be used to forecast missing data on the depth of the snowpack through predictors that are based on meteorology that include the temperature of the air, the humidity of the air, the velocity of the wind, and the quantity of sunlight. A comparison of three models, which are Linear Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF) and XGBoost Regressor, is performed. The best accuracy with the largest R 2 of 0.8641, MSE of 12.9442 and MAE of 2.6472 was XGBoost model, not only higher than LR (R 2 = 0.0031), but also higher than RF (R 2 = 0.8550). These results demonstrate that ensemble methods are the most appropriate to model non in an environmental data. The proposed solution offers an efficient solution to the lack of meteorological data imputation that enhances the safety and reliability of the activities of sources of nuclear energy.

title: IoT-based Wearable Smart Ambient Observation System for Elderly Monitoring
authors: Sivanesan Perumal, Raj Anand Sundaramoorthy, V. Ganesh Karthikeyan, Aarthee Selvaraj
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Recent health crises have highlighted the critical need for continuous monitoring of elderly individuals, particularly those over the age of 60, to improve their quality of life and reduce hospitalizations. This research proposes a smart Ambient Assisted Living (AAL) system to enhance the safety and well-being of elderly people, especially those over 60. The system uses a multi-sensor network comprising motion, touch, temperature, humidity, and current sensors to monitor daily activities and detect anomalies. By analyzing user behavior and environmental conditions, the system identifies potential health risks and enables timely interventions. To optimize system performance, a data reduction algorithm is implemented to eliminate redundant data, thereby improving efficiency. The refined data is transmitted to the cloud and processed using MATLAB and ThingSpeak platforms enable advanced prediction and analysis by facilitating data visualization, analysis, and machine learning, which allow the identification of patterns and trends in the data. The system generates alerts when monitored values exceed predefined thresholds, indicating potential health risks. Notifications are sent to family members and healthcare professionals via email or SMS through the IFTTT (If This Then That) protocol, ensuring a prompt response and intervention. The proposed system demonstrates exceptional performance, with 96.2% security, 88.5% energy efficiency, and 99.98% reliability, significantly contributing to the safety and well-being of elderly individuals. By leveraging IoT technology, data analytics, and machine learning, this innovative solution has the potential to revolutionize elderly care, reduce hospitalization risks, and improve overall health outcomes.

title: Intelligent Intrusion-Resilient Load Balancing in SDN using Machine Learning for Adaptive Traffic Rerouting
authors: Vanitha U, Ajin Ribia P, Dharshini M, Charlotte Infantia M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Software-Defined Networking (SDN) offers centralized control and customizable traffic control, although relying on a single controller renders it highly susceptible to DDoS attacks, as well as intelligent traffic flooding, which could bring the whole network to its knees. The load- balancing mechanisms that exist today respond at the point of degradation and cannot distinguish between malicious and legitimate flows in adversarial situations. To address this shortcoming, this paper presents an Intelligent Intrusion-resilient Load balancing (IIR- LB) model that combines real-time anomalous detection with real-time adaptive rerouting. The algorithm uses an Isolation Forest algorithm along with a CNN -RNN hybrid deep-learning model to learn and adapt both spatial and temporal dynamics of traffic and detect malicious flows correctly. When threats are identified, an adaptive routing engine employs A* optimization with Contraction Hierarchy to choose scalable low- latency routes, so that the network operation would not be interrupted by the attacks. Experimental analysis shows that IIR-LB is a highly effective throughput improvement, latency reduction, and the recovery of a controller overload as well as balanced traffic allocation during large-scale intrusions. The proposed architecture provides an autonomous traffic management and intelligent threat detection system, thus offering a self-healing and attack-sensitive SDN architecture that can be applied to a next-generation secure network infrastructure.

title: Augmented Reality with Agentic AI Framework for Brain Activation Stimulating Analysis Associated in Drug-Resistant Epilepsy Hodologic Guided Thalamic structure
authors: N.Deepa
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Refractory epilepsy patients who are ineligible for resistive surgery often rely on deep brain stimulation (DBS) targeting thalamic nuclei to suppress seizure activity. However, the absence of consensus on optimal stimulation sites and the lack of adaptive control limit the clinical efficacy of existing DBS systems. This study proposes an Agentic Augmented Reality–driven Thalamocortical Neuromodulation System that leverages hodological mapping to deliver patient-specific, adaptive stimulation. The system employs Agentic Artificial Intelligence comprising cognitive, sensory, actuator, and visualization agents. Using EEG, fMRI, and diffusion tensor imaging (DTI), the framework models thalamocortical connectivity through Graph Neural Networks and optimizes stimulation parameters via Reinforcement Learning. A real-time AR interface projects a 3D thalamocortical map, allowing clinicians to visualize seizure onset zones, agent-suggested stimulation targets interactively. Health Experimental evaluation across focal epilepsy demonstrated that hodological matched, agent-controlled stimulation achieved an 87.2% reduction in seizure frequency and an interictal discharge suppression rate of 92%, outperforming static DBS (58.4%) and non-agentic AI systems (73.1%). This results a response latency below 2 seconds and improved clinical interpretability scores by 41% through AR-based visualization. These findings highlight the potential of Agentic AI–augmented AR neuromodulation to advance personalized, adaptive, and interpretable therapies for refractory epilepsy by aligning thalamic stimulation with patient-specific hodological signatures.

title: CyberSentinel: A Comparative Agent-based Study of Proximal Policy Optimization and A* Search for Network Intrusion Containment
authors: Prahalika Nivarthi, Agastya Nambiar, Sahil Nambiar, Pooja Gowda
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: CyberSentinel, an Artificial Intelligence (AI) agent, is developed to simulate network intrusion detection and containment within dynamic computer networks. The system represents network structure by simulating a topology using the Mesa framework consisting of interacting agents (Node, Security, and Intruder). The project is carried out in two successive phases where the first phase involves the use of classical AI techniques, incorporating rule based Security Agent behavior, which is integrated with the A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∗</sup> search algorithm to find and isolate the compromised nodes. The second stage extends the model by incorporating Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) through a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) agent. This combination enables the system to have the best balance between proactive patrolling and reactive containment; the system keeps on calculating the most effective paths to containment; on the other hand, high level strategic decisions are taken by PPO. Results reveal that this PPO-enhanced model has very high containment rate as well as resilience to intrusion propagation to a greater extent than the heuristic-only use of the A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∗</sup> algorithm.

title: Real-Time Cloud VM Performance Prediction using DTW and GRU Networks
authors: K. Subba Reddy, Bheemagundam Noorbi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The problem that cloud computing environments faces is not easy to adequately monitor the performance of VM due to the changing workloads and resource contention. Accurate VM performance prediction is also important to resource allocation and increased system efficiency. This paper introduces CloudProphet a machine learning tool used to predict the performance of a virtual machine in real time based on DTW and Pearson correlation to select features. DTW is used to identify temporal similarities in the time-series data, whereas Pearson correlation identifies considerably relevant runtime measures. Three models are evaluated, including LSTM with no feature selection based on DTW, LSTM with an enhancement of features by DTW and GRU with both DTW and correlated features. As per experimental results on a real world cloud dataset, the GRU model can achieve the highest prediction accuracy of 99.3 percent exceeding other methodologies. It has improved performance and is attributed to the effective combination of temporal alignment and feature significance. The suggested system also supports the improved forecasting of the performance of applications and VM, thereby, supporting the efficient utilization of resources within cloud settings.

title: NAVIS - Design and Development of a CubeSat for Maritime Data Acquisition System
authors: Abishek R P, Dinesh R, Hariprassat K, Thyla B
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This project presents a compact 1U CubeSat (10×10×10 cm) tailored for maritime surveillance and safety services. It integrates real-time imaging and environmental sensors to track vessel movement, observe the air quality and obtain real-time data and time in Indian Standard Time (IST) format. The payload includes ESP32-CAM, MQ-135 gas sensor, BMP280 for atmospheric data, NEO-6M GPS, and MPU6050 for orientation. It is powered by the 18650 Lithium-Ion battery, and it supports LEO simulation and high-altitude trials. Communication is enabled via RA-02 LoRa SX1278 (433 MHz) module between the CubeSat and the ground station. Unlike drones, CubeSats offer autonomous, long-duration monitoring and modular scalability, making them ideal for evolving maritime services.

title: Multi-Cloud Program Management For Large Scale Deployment
authors: Siva Sai Krishna Suryadevara, Somesh Nagalla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Multi-cloud strategies are one of the enablers that have gained urgent importance in large-scale enterprise implementations to provide better resilience, independence, and optimization of their performance. There is, however, a great challenge associated with the governance, cost control, security compliance, interoperability, and service-level assurance of managing large-scale programs across heterogeneous cloud platforms. The paper introduces a multi-cloud program management scheme that is comprehensive and capable of assisting the scaling, reliable, and policy-oriented deployment of cloud environments, which are globally distributed. The framework proposed would combine orchestration as a centralized system, standardized policies of governance, automated monitoring of compliance and performance-aware placement of workloads to ensure that technical performance is aligned to strategic business goals. The major dimensions in program management such as alignment of portfolios, risk management, governing costs, vendor integration, and control over operations are taken care of in a structured manner using the layered architecture. The framework also includes analytics-based decision support to facilitate resource optimization as well as continuous improvement in dynamic multi-cloud ecosystems. Through experimental assessment and case study-inspired industry scenarios, it has been shown that the proposed solution leads to much better deployment agility, cost-effectiveness, service reliability and cross-cloud interoperability than the traditional single-cloud and ad-hoc multi-cloud management practice. The findings have demonstrated the relevance of program management in the practicality of the multi-cloud architectures to large-scale and mission-critical deployments.

title: An Explainable NASNet Architecture for Reliable Detection and Interpretation of Synthetic Images
authors: Farooq Sunar Mahammad, Rameswara Reddy Boddu, Thanikanti Sudhakar Babu, S.A. Poojitha, Pragati Priyadarshinee, B.V Chandra Sekahar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The development of realistic-looking synthetic images has increased enormously mainly because of the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technologies, which has created concerns about digital manipulation, identity theft, and disinformation. The study found issues such as limited understanding of hidden generative models and decreased power. This study indicates a NASNet-based explainable deep learning framework for precise AI-generated image detection and interpretation. To enhance the decision transparency, first we have collected dataset from kaggle with70k real images and 70k synthetic images and sent to proposed framework that combines explainability mechanisms with feature extraction. While compared to state-of-the-art methods, experimental evaluation on a large dataset of real and synthetic images demonstrates superior performance, achieving higher accuracy 98.55%, precision, recall, and F1-score. Reliability for achievable forensic applications is increased by the explainable visualization results, and these offer interpretable insights into model forecasts.

title: FAS-MBCA: Face Anti-Spoofing using Multi-path Bi-directional Cross Attention Fusion
authors: Dhimas Arief Dharmawan, Made Vidyatma Adhi Krisna, Anto Satriyo Nugroho, Andrey Ferriyan, Bambang Yuwono, Jaconiah Widiwasita, Aqsha Jauzaarafa’ Setya Hadi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Face Anti-Spoofing (FAS) is a critical component for securing face recognition systems against diverse presentation attacks. multi-modal FAS approaches leverage complementary cues from multiple sensors; however, existing methods often adopt single-path fusion or uni-directional interaction schemes, which may introduce modality hierarchy and limit comprehensive cross-modal feature refinement. In this paper, we propose FAS-MBCA, a structured multi-path bi-directional cross-attention framework for tri-modal face anti-spoofing. FAS-MBCA explicitly constructs a complete pairwise interaction topology among RGB, depth, and infrared modalities, enabling reciprocal feature-map refinement across all modality pairs. By modeling six-direction cross-modal interactions within a lightweight CNN-based architecture, the proposed framework enhances multi-modal synergy while maintaining computational efficiency. We evaluate FAS-MBCA on multiple widely used benchmarks, including CASIA-SURF (MmFA), CASIA-CeFA, and WMCA, under both intra-dataset and cross-dataset protocols. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FAS-MBCA consistently outperforms single-modal baselines and na¨ıve fusion strategies, and achieves competitive or superior performance compared to recent multi-modal methods. Efficiency analysis in terms of model parameters and GFLOPs further indicates a favorable accuracy–efficiency trade-off. These results validate that explicitly modeling complete bi-directional cross-modal interaction at the feature-map level provides an effective and practical solution for multi-modal face anti-spoofing. The source code of FAS-MBCA is publicly available at https://github.com/nascafas2025/FAS-MBCA.

title: 6G-Enabled Quantum-Energy-Aware Multi-Agent Multi-Modal Scheme for Underwater Acoustic–RF Networks
authors: Altaf Hussain, Shuaiyong Li, Tariq Hussain, Muhammad Salman Pathan, Theyazn H.H. Aldhyani, Eatedal Alabdulkreem
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Wireless networks with limited deployment constraints are critical for emergency response, marine monitoring, and mission-centric operations. Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks (UASNs) communications are severely constrained by high propagation delay, limited bandwidth, and energy scarcity. This paper proposes a 6G-Enabled Quantum-Aware Energy-Efficient Cooperation (6G-QAEEC) multi-agent underwater acoustic-radio frequency (RF) network framework for reliable multimodal underwater communication. The proposed architecture enables cooperative intelligence among submerged sensor nodes, Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) relays, and 6G-enabled surface/Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) gateways. A Nearest Node Verification (NNV) discovery protocol is introduced to prune unstable links during route formation, reducing redundant flooding and improving routing stability. To further enhance network efficiency, three cooperation mechanisms are developed: adaptive relay rotation with signal amplification, duty-cycled neighbor re-verification, and opportunistic optical burst communication for local high-rate exchanges. Quantum-aware optimization at the 6G edge accelerates routing and resource scheduling decisions under dynamic channel conditions. Simulation results show that the proposed framework improves packet delivery ratio, reduces energy consumption per bit, lowers routing overhead, and decreases end-to-end delay compared with baseline schemes. Overall, the proposed multi-agent multimodal quantum-aware framework enhances reliability, energy efficiency, and spectral utilization in next-generation underwater networks.

title: Measured Energy Consumption for AI-based Precoding
authors: Pengyu Cong, Chenyang Yang, Shengqian Han, Nan Li, Qi Sun
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper presents the first measurement-based study on the energy consumption of artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms for radio access networks (RAN), aiming to investigate whether the integration of AI leads to higher energy consumption than traditional non-AI algorithms. We use the multiuser precoding problem as a representative example, where AI is widely recognized for its potential in supporting the growing deployment of antennas for 6G systems. Our assessment examines three AI algorithms using the convolutional neural network (CNN), edge graph neural network (EGNN), and communication model-based graph neural network (MGNN), in comparison with two non-AI algorithms, specifically the weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) and regularized zero-forcing (RZF) algorithms. Through extensive measurements of energy and power consumption, we examine how efficient architecture designs of deep neural networks (DNNs) affect energy consumptions, and provide a quantitative comparison between measured results and estimates from existing power estimation models. The results indicate that the MGNN, which is the most efficient architecture among the three DNNs, consumes substantially less energy than the non-AI algorithms for large-scale systems, while the CNN consumes significantly more energy for both training and inference compared to the MGNN. Moreover, utilizing GPUs does not always lead to higher power consumption compared to CPUs, the power consumption of CPUs should be taken into account when GPUs are used, and DRAM contributes little to total power consumption. We demonstrate the shortcomings of existing utilization-based and FLOPs-based models in estimating energy consumption, calling for the development of more accurate models to facilitate green AI designs for RAN.

title: A 13-bit 70-MS/s SAR-Assisted Cyclic ADC With Charge-Reused 2-bit/Cycle Operation
authors: Rucheng Jiang, Han Wu, Kian Ann Ng, Chne-Wuen Tsai, Jerald Yoo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This article presents an energy-efficient 13-bit high-speed SAR-assisted cyclic ADC. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1^{\text {st}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> stage 6-bit SAR ADC coarsely quantizes the input signal. The proposed charge-reused 2-bit/cycle cyclic ADC doubles the conversion speed with 50% reduction in capacitor number. Owing to the comparator noise suppression circuit and mismatch robustness of the cyclic ADC, an interstage gain of only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> is required, which significantly relaxes the amplifier design and improves its total harmonic distortion (THD). Fabricated in 40nm 1P8M CMOS, the proposed SAR-assisted cyclic ADC achieves 13b resolution with SNDR and SFDR of 66.5dB and 80.1dB, respectively, with a Nyquist-rate input at 70MS/s without calibration while consuming 0.88mW power, yielding FoM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub> and FoM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">W</sub> of 175dB and 6.9fJ/conv., respectively. The ADC demonstrates a sub-1dB SNDR variation across the measured 9 chips, presenting the robustness against variations.

title: Crash-Risk-Aware Integrated Predictive Control in Emergency Conditions for Intelligent Vehicles
authors: Yu Zhang, Fuwei Wu, Yechen Qin, Mingming Dong, Shaoyang Shi, Ke Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Crash-avoidance systems in current intelligent vehicles (IVs) require accurate crash risk estimation and optimization of multiple control inputs. However, the uncertainties in vehicle states caused by the inherent noise in onboard sensors lead to increased errors in spatiotemporal crash risk calculations, which, in turn, affect the optimization of control inputs. To address these challenges, this article introduces a crash-risk-aware integrated predictive control (CRIPC), which computes crash positions and time-to-collision with uncertainties (UTTC) between vehicles under nonideal conditions by constructing an extensive elliptical vehicle geometry model to quantify the uncertainties arising from sensor noise. Additionally, CRIPC utilizes UTTC and the crash-point position between vehicles to dynamically adjust the crash-risk-aware repulsive field, which is subsequently used to define the objective function for CRIPC optimization. Validation results from real-vehicle experiments and driver-in-the-loop (DiL) simulations demonstrate that CRIPC effectively quantifies the motion uncertainties of surrounding vehicles, significantly enhancing crash avoidance capabilities while maintaining vehicle stability. Furthermore, CRIPC shows robustness to variations in road conditions and system uncertainties, meeting the real-time requirements of practical applications.

title: On the Availability Risks of Production LLM Services under Unbounded Inference_supp1-3689535.pdf
authors: Hongzi Zhu
publish: N/A
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become foundational components in a wide range of applications, including natural language understanding and generation, embodied intelligence, and scientific discovery. As their computational requirements continue to grow, these models are increasingly deployed as cloud-based services, allowing users to access powerful LLMs via the Internet. However, this deployment model introduces a new class of threat: denial-of-service (DoS) attacks via unbounded reasoning, where adversaries craft specially designed inputs that cause the model to enter excessively long or infinite generation loops. These attacks can exhaust backend compute resources, degrading or denying service to legitimate users. To mitigate such risks, many LLM providers adopt a closed-source, black box setting to obscure model internals. In this paper, we propose ThinkTrap, a novel input-space optimization framework for DoS attacks against LLM services even in black-box environments. The core idea of ThinkTrap is to first map discrete tokens into a continuous embedding space, then undertake efficient black-box optimization in a low-dimensional subspace exploiting input sparsity. The goal of this optimization is to identify adversarial prompts that induce extended or non-terminating generation across several state-of-the-art LLMs, achieving DoS with minimal token overhead. We evaluate ThinkTrap across multiple commercial, closed-source LLM services and observe that it can consistently induce abnormally long outputs and noticeable response-side degradation under black-box access. To further quantify the system-level impact of the attack, we conduct controlled experiments on private LLM deployments, where ThinkTrap reduces service throughput to as low as 1% of its original capacity and, in extreme cases, induces complete service failure due to resource exhaustion.

title: FedACE-Sec: A Secure Asynchronous Federated Learning Framework for Consumer Electronics with Proactive Threat Hunting
authors: Rui Guo, Jindi Shi, Fangyan Zhu, Yinghui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Next-generation consumer electronic devices in intelligent systems are exposed to increasingly sophisticated security threats, as privacy-sensitive data distributed across numerous devices remain vulnerable to malicious attacks and compromise. Centralized data collection for anomaly detection model training is neither secure nor compliant with modern privacy regulations. To address these challenges, we propose FedACE-Sec, an asynchronous and secure federated learning framework that integrates homomorphic encryption and digital signatures. It enables consumer electronics devices to collaboratively train a global anomaly detection model while safeguarding local data privacy, and employs digital signatures to prevent malicious model updates, thereby ensuring both privacy preservation and trustworthy proactive threat detection. Moreover, the framework integrates a buffer-based asynchronous aggregation mechanism that enabling clients to disconnect immediately after uploading their updates. Experimental results on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets show that the proposed framework provides enhanced security over the original FedCFA and improves model accuracy by 1.75%. Additionally, the framework effectively mitigates privacy leakage and malicious attacks. Ablation experiments further confirm the contribution of each component to security, efficiency, and overall model performance.

title: Field-to-Wire Coupling Model for Arbitrarily Routed Shielded Cables Above Ground
authors: Bing Li, Jiayue Xing, Wenxuan Huang, Yaoyao Li, Weiping Tong, Zongfei Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: In this article, transmission line (TL) theory is applied to the modeling of shielded cables with arbitrary routing paths above ground, enabling efficient and accurate prediction of field-to-wire coupling effects. The external electromagnetic field is first modeled either analytically (such as via plane wave illumination) or numerically (such as through nonuniform complex fields), and the cable path is discretized for field sampling along its trajectory. Afterward, a segmented TL-based external-loop circuit model is established, and the current distribution in the external loop is obtained through a recursive chain parameter algorithm. With an appropriate transfer impedance model, a segmented TL-based internal-loop circuit is subsequently formulated to determine the induced terminal currents. The accuracy and efficiency of the proposed model are verified through three representative full-wave simulation cases involving tubular-shield coaxial and multiconductor cables with both straight and arbitrarily routed (Gaussian-modulated helical) paths, including dielectric-coated, lossy, and segmentwise nonuniform shielding configurations, driven by analytical plane wave and numerically simulated fields. Furthermore, the proposed method was validated through radiated susceptibility experiments in a gigahertz transverse electromagnetic cell using three braided-shield coaxial cables arranged along straight and parabolic paths.

title: SoAD: Safety-Oriented Value Estimation for Enhanced Closed-Loop End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Yinfeng Gao, Deqing Liu, Yupeng Zheng, Qichao Zhang, Da-Wei Ding, Dongbin Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: End-to-end (E2E) autonomous driving systems, which map sensory inputs directly to vehicle planning, have garnered attention for harnessing the potential of data-driven methodologies in motion planning. However, current methods face two limitations that undermine their safety performance in closed-loop driving tasks. First, the predominant imitation learning (IL) paradigm overlooks long-term safety beyond predefined planning horizons, potentially guiding the ego vehicle into hazardous states. Second, the lack of reliable online evaluation mechanisms limits real-time responses to safety risks. To overcome these challenges, we propose SoAD, a safety-oriented E2E framework that integrates long-term safety awareness into planning. SoAD is distinguished by a reinforcement learning (RL)-based value estimation module to quantify the safety of planned trajectories, and a vector world model (VWM) to generate interaction-aware future rollouts. During training, the system benefits from value-guided fine-tuning (VFT) that optimizes the planning distribution to favor safer trajectories. In closed-loop deployment, a planning rescoring (PRS) mechanism is designed to perform reliable online evaluation by combining ego-conditional predictions from the VWM with corresponding safety value estimates. Experimental results on the Bench2Drive closed-loop benchmark demonstrate the state-of-the-art (SoTA) performance of SoAD, achieving a 66.40% improvement in driving score (DS) compared to the vectorized scene representation for efficient autonomous driving (VAD) baseline, while zero-shot evaluation on the driving in occlusion simulation (DOS) benchmark further highlights its strong generalization ability.

title: Nanomaterial-Based Capacitive Sensor for Dust Type Differentiation on Surfaces_supp1-3690478.pdf
authors: Abdullah Abdulhameed
publish: N/A
abstract: Current surface dust sensors offer advanced measurement capabilities, especially in determining dust concentration and accumulation ratio using light-scattering mechanisms. However, these sensors are limited in their ability to identify the type or geographical origin of dust particles. Leveraging advances in nanomaterials, we report the fabrication of nanomaterial-based dust sensors capable of differentiating dust samples from different sources under controlled dust loading on the sensor surface. Furthermore, a readout circuit is designed and implemented on a PCB to form an end-to-end sensing platform. Dust samples collected from sand dunes, house roofs, construction sites, and desertified soil are characterized using SEM, XRF, FTIR, and XRD. The results revealed that the dust samples have different morphological structures and elemental distributions, which contribute to their dielectric constants. The sensor capacitance ranged from 1.75 pF to 4.08 pF, and the sensor sensitivity ranged from 8.12% to 118.57%, respectively, as a function of dust type and accumulation level, which varied from 20 mg to 100 mg. In addition, the readout circuit reported a voltage sensitivity of 26-99% at a low dust loading of 20 mg, depending on the dust type. The proposed surface dust sensor has the potential to be integrated into solar panel systems to optimize their performance by enabling automated cleaning initiation when critical dust accumulation thresholds are reached.

title: A Real-time Detection Method of Spark Discharge in High Voltage Switchgear Based on Improved YOLOv10 and Multi-scale Feature Fusion
authors: Liming Li, Qianwen Chen, Wentao Ma, Ying Li, Yongyu Wang, Bo Liu, Liwei Song
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Spark discharge is liable to occur during the long-term operation of high-voltage switchgear, which often induces electrical failures and fires. Traditional detection methods suffer from limitations including low detection accuracy, high false alarm rate, and poor real-time performance. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel spark discharge detection framework integrating an improved YOLOv10 algorithm and a High-Resolution Network (HRNet), with a key innovation of a global-local attention fusion module. By integrating HRNet’s four-stage bidirectional cross-connection architecture, the framework realizes continuous interaction and complementarity between multi-scale features. A comprehensive spark discharge dataset was constructed using a multi-sensor array, providing reliable data support for model training and generalization verification. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves 98.9% accuracy on the validation dataset. At a resolution of 1920×1080, the false alarm rate is as low as 0.02, and the frame rate reaches 90 frames per second, realizing the synchronous improvement of detection precision and real-time performance. In practical deployment on 300 high-voltage switchgear samples under actual operating conditions, the model achieves an average detection accuracy of 99.2% with a 3D positioning error of 3.5 cm. This method significantly improves the detection accuracy and real-time performance of spark discharge, providing reliable technical support for the safety monitoring of high-voltage switchgear. The proposed method not only offers a reliable technical solution for the active safety protection of high-voltage switchgear, but also provides theoretical reference and engineering insights for the algorithm design of weak-feature target detection in similar industrial scenarios.

title: A Lightweight Multi-Head Attention Network with Hierarchical Cross-Modal Fusion for Planetary Gearbox Fault Diagnosis
authors: Qingyuan Zheng, Haitao Zhuang, Zuo Wang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: As critical components in transmission systems, planetary gearboxes require precise intelligent fault diagnosis to ensure optimal performance. Most existing methods rely solely on individual vibration signals for training, a practice that leads to the dual problems of insufficient data diversity and over-parameterized models. This paper presents a novel framework that integrates multi-modal sensor fusion with a lightweight multi-head attention mechanism to effectively address these challenges. Firstly, to enhance discriminative capability, multi-axis vibration measurements (x and y-direction) from the planetary gearbox are fused with encoder data to capture complementary dynamic characteristics. Then, a hierarchical cross-modal fusion method with a gating mechanism is introduced to enhance feature reliability and richness while reducing dimensionality. Within the proposed hierarchical architecture, we first perform intra-vibration feature consolidation to remove spectral redundancy, followed by cross-domain fusion with encoder signals to amplify discriminative characteristics. Finally, to reduce computational complexity, redundant information is reduced by applying average pooling to the multi-head attention inputs. Additionally, the value vector in the conventional attention mechanism is replaced with the input itself to decrease the number of parameters. Extensive experimental validation under varying operating conditions demonstrates that the proposed method simultaneously enhances fault diagnosis accuracy and significantly reduces computational demands.

title: Dynamic Whole-Body Dancing With Humanoid Robots: A Model-Based Control Approach
authors: Shibowen Zhang, Jiayang Wu, Guannan Liu, Helin Zhu, Junjie Liu, Zhehan Li, Junhong Guo, Xiaokun Leng, Hangxin Liu, Jingwen Zhang, Jikai Wang, Zonghai Chen, Zhicheng He, Jiayi Wang, Yao Su
publish: IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine
abstract: This paper presents an integrated model-based framework for generating and executing dynamic whole-body dance motions on humanoid robots. The framework operates in two stages: offline motion generation and online motion execution, both leveraging future state prediction to enable robust and dynamic dance motions in real-world environments. In the offline motion generation stage, human dance demonstrations are captured via a motion capture (MoCap) system, retargeted to the robot by solving a Quadratic Programming (QP) problem, and further refined using Trajectory Optimization (TO) to ensure dynamic feasibility. In the online motion execution stage, a centroidal dynamics-based Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework tracks the planned motions in real time and proactively adjusts swing foot placement to adapt to real-world disturbances. We validate our framework on the full-size humanoid robot Kuavo 4Pro, demonstrating the dynamic dance motions both in simulation and in a four-minute live public performance with a team of four robots. Experimental results show that longer prediction horizons improve both motion expressiveness in planning and stability in execution.

title: Eddy-Current Losses Analysis of Edge Burr-Affected Laminations in Single-Phase Wound Core of Transformer
authors: Chenqingyu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: The interlaminar short circuit under the impact of edge burr is one of major fault of transformer core, which might locally damage the core and make the transformer operate with defection, further affect the service performance. The correlative research primarily focuses on fault simulation or experiment to laminated core at various short-circuit positions, burr lengths, material types and so on. However, the numerical method to evaluate the eddy-current losses with local interlaminar fault is relatively rare, especially for transformer core of larger capacity. Therefore, in our study, an analytical formula to estimate the eddy-current losses by respective resistance of lamination is proposed, where the non-uniform distribution of electromagnetic quantity is sufficiently considered to determine the boundary condition of eddy-current field. Meanwhile, the contact coefficient measurement of electrical steel is adequately prepared for losses computation of contact resistance. Subsequently, the losses experiment to defective samples affected by artificial burr is developed to validate the effectiveness of proposed method, where the result shows a good precision in a variety of excitations.

title: HFedAR: Resource Allocation for Adaptive Retransmission-Based Hierarchical Federated Learning in Open RAN_supp1-3682775.pdf
authors: Hongchao Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: The flexible architecture of the open radio access network (O-RAN) provides effective support for the deployment of federated learning (FL). However, existing FL schemes in wireless networks often suffer from packet errors, which lead to reduced model test accuracy and increased training delay. In this paper, we propose a robust FL scheme in O-RAN, named adaptive retransmission-based hierarchical FL (HFedAR), which improves model test accuracy and reduces training delay. Specifically, HFedAR first analyzes the similarity of local gradients using a clustering algorithm to associate users with edge servers. Subsequently, we employ an adaptive retransmission mechanism for both edge and global aggregation, thereby facilitating rapid convergence of FL on non-independent and identically distributed data. Considering the limitations of spectrum resources and user energy, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem to minimize FL training loss and delay. Due to the implicit nature of the objective function, the coupling of decision variables, and network dynamics, it is difficult to solve the problem through traditional convex optimization and machine learning algorithms. Therefore, we derive a convergence upper bound for HFedAR and present a two-stage resource allocation algorithm. The algorithm can jointly make optimal user power and computing resource allocation decisions in the first stage, and user scheduling, retransmission selection, and radio resource block allocation decisions in the second stage. Extensive simulation results on the MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets demonstrate that the HFedAR scheme significantly improves model test accuracy by 3.5% and 6.5% and reduces training delay by 40.1% and 34.2% compared with existing FL benchmarks.

title: Novel Bushing With Adaptively Field Grading Structure: Simulation and Mechanism
authors: Zhiwen Huang, Gang Sun, Yifan Zhou, Ya Sun, Zhikang Yuan, Jun Hu, Jinliang He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Bushing has become a critical component endangering the safe functioning of power transmission and limiting its development to higher voltage and power levels. The main technical bottleneck that restricts high voltage bushing is the uneven distribution of electric fields due to its “plug-in” structure. In this paper, a novel adaptive field grading (AFG) bushing with a simple multi-layered structure based on nonlinear materials is introduced. A frequency-domain steady-state simulation method considering the field-dependent dielectric and loss characteristics of nonlinear materials to design the AFG bushing is proposed. Referring to the size of the AC transformer capacitive bushing, the finite element models of the 40.5 kV AFG bushing are established, and the electric field distributions are obtained. Optical electric field sensors are used to test the surface electric field distribution of the bushing samples, validating the accuracy of the simulation method. Combining simulation and experimental test results, the influence of doping concentrations and lengths of the field grading layer on the electric field distribution is studied, and the grading mechanism of nonlinear material is summarized. The results demonstrate the potential of nonlinear materials to relieve electric field concentration in bushings and provide a solid basis for its design and application.
