title: Sensorless Control of LC-Filtered PMSM Drives With An Easy-Tuning Adaptive Full-Order Observer
authors: Chao Li, Shaobin Li, Yongxiang Xu, Jibin Zou, Wentao Zhang, Shuo Chen, Zhixun Ma, Xiang Wu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: In permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives, an LC filter helps sinusoidalize the motor terminal voltage, improving operational longevity. However, its inclusion increases model complexity, requiring a high-dimensional gain matrix for observability and complicating observer design. To overcome this issue, a novel adaptive full-order observer (AFO) is proposed for estimating stator current, stator voltage, extended back electromotive forces (EEMFs), rotor position, and speed in LC-filtered PMSM drives across medium- to high-speed ranges. Based on a frequency-domain dynamic error model, the introduced pole-placement method achieves stable pole assignment with only one tuning parameter. By formulating an error function that captures the effect of speed estimation error on pole placement, the unstable region of the system is effectively reduced through proper observer design. An improved speed adaptation law based on the gradient descent algorithm is also presented, with its stability rigorously proven via Lyapunov analysis in accordance with LaSalle's invariance principle. The resulting speed adaptation loop uses only one tuning parameter, ensuring asymptotic stability and ease of implementation. Furthermore, the influence of parameter variations on estimated states is analytically expressed, allowing quantitative error analysis and robustness assessment. Experimental results validate the proposed AFO design and parameter sensitivity analysis for sensorless control of LC-filtered PMSM drives.

title: Research on Temperature Error Compensation of Fiber Optic Gyroscope Based on Viscoelastic Dynamic Mechanism
authors: Qiongnan Shi, Jiajun Ma, Zhuo Zhang, Bin Long, Shan Xiao
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: Under complex varying-temperature conditions, fiber optic gyroscopes are prone to zero-bias drift, a major factor limiting their measurement accuracy. The traditional Shupe model considers only the instantaneous temperature gradient and fails to account for thermal hysteresis. Furthermore, most existing models predominantly employ linear elasticity assumptions, neglecting the viscoelasticity and stress relaxation characteristics of the fiber coil composite. This leads to insufficient compensation accuracy under dynamic temperature changes. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a thermo-mechanically coupled dynamic error compensation method based on the standard linear solid model. It innovatively integrates viscoelastic dynamics with the thermal hysteresis effect of FOGs to establish a physics-based model. By constructing a state-space model that incorporates temperature field distribution and stress relaxation mechanisms, and by introducing relaxation time constants to quantitatively characterize material memory effects, the proposed method effectively overcomes the oversimplified material assumptions in traditional models. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively eliminates thermal hysteresis effects, achieving notable compensation performance. The accuracy of the FOG-98 under temperature variations improved from 0.065°/h to 0.025°/h, representing an enhancement of 61.5%. These results verify the method's effectiveness and superiority in complex temperature environments.

title: Multi-domain Feature Integration based Trusted Partial Multi-view Incomplete Multi-label Learning
authors: Jie Wen, Jiaying Zhou, Xiaohuan Lu, Youliang Tian, Zheng Zhang, Li Shen, Yong Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Partial Multi-view Incomplete Multi-label Learning (PMvIMlL) focuses on the challenging multi-view multi-label learning tasks characterized by partially missing views and labels in real-world data. Although many methods have been proposed in recent years, most methods suffer from three distinct yet critical limitations. First, they lack effective mechanisms to quantify predictive uncertainty under dual missing settings, often yielding overconfident and unreliable predictions. Second, existing representations rely exclusively on spatial-domain features, overlooking the rich complementary information available in the frequency domain, which substantially limits their discriminative power. Third, prevailing fusion approaches fail to account for cross-view opinion divergence, frequently resulting in suboptimal fusion quality when handling discordant perspectives. To address these issues, we propose MFIT, a Multi-domain Feature Integration based Trusted Partial Multi-view Incomplete Multi-label Learning framework. Our method comprises two core components: a Feature Enhancement module via Frequency-Domain Integration (FE-FDI) and an Evidential Fusion module with Cross-view Decision Consistency (EF-CDC). FE-FDI enriches representations by incorporating global frequency patterns while preserving spatial characteristics. EF-CDC addresses the sensitivity issue of multi-view decision conflicts in the fusion process by enhancing cross-view decision consistency. Comprehensive evaluations on six datasets demonstrate that MFIT achieves substantial and consistent performance improvements, validating its effectiveness in enhancing both prediction accuracy and decision reliability.

title: PAPR Reduction in AFDM via Pre-Chirp Selection Assisted Active Constellation Extension and Pilot Position Optimization
authors: Anjie Zhang, Qi Zheng, Qiang Li, Yao Ge, Miaowen Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The recently proposed affine frequency division multiplexing (AFDM), which is based on multi-chirp waveforms, has demonstrated reliability and robustness in doubly selective fading channels. However, similar to other multi-carrier schemes, AFDM suffers from high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR), which may cause signal distortion in power amplifier and performance degradation. To address this issue, this paper proposes, for the first time, an optimal pilot positioning (OPP) algorithm for pilot frames and a selective pre-chirp parameter assisted active constellation extension (SP-ACE) algorithm for subsequent data frames of AFDM systems. OPP selects the signal with the lowest PAPR by adjusting pilot symbol positions, while SP-ACE does so by tuning pre-chirp parameters and constellation points. Simulation results demonstrate that both algorithms achieve superior PAPR performance without sacrificing the bit error rate (BER) performance in the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) region of practical interest, and do not require the transmission of additional side information.

title: Research Status and Development Trends of Laser-Assisted Simulation of Dose-Rate Effects
authors: Ge Tang, Qiqi Liu, Jingrui Liu, Yao Xiao, Fuwang Zhang, Zhenkang Zhang, Xueying Wang, Shaobo Si, Yu Zhang, Suhan Tang, Mo Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Pulsed-laser simulation provides a repeatable, safe, and effective complementary approach to large radiation facilities for studying transient ionizing dose-rate effects in semiconductor devices and integrated circuits. This review traces the development of laser-assisted test facilities over the past five decades and summarizes how wavelength selection, pulse-width control, beam/spot conditioning, and coordinated laser and X-ray/γ-ray platforms have expanded accessible test conditions. We discuss the physical basis of laser-radiation correspondence, from carrier generation and transport to circuit-level photocurrent phenomena, including rail-span collapse, and identify three dominant accuracy drivers: device/circuit structure and biasing, laser parameters, and temperature/thermal effects. Three conversion factor (CF) extraction approaches—carrier generation rate, peak photocurrent equivalence, and charge collection equivalence—are compared in terms of assumptions, uncertainty, and validity range. Among them, charge collection equivalence remains applicable in saturation regimes where peak-based metrics fail. Looking ahead, further progress in laser-assisted dose-rate simulation will depend on broader-band laser sources, such as UV-capable wide bandgap laser-assisted simulation systems, more reproducible CF establishment workflows, and AI-assisted electrical response modeling (e.g., technology computer aided design (TCAD) and finite-element analysis) for high-dimensional parameter studies.

title: An Ultra-wideband Miniaturized Sinusoidal Microwave Photonic Receiving Antenna for Optical Frequency Comb Undersampling Receiving Systems
authors: Yuhang Song, Yumo Tian, Shuguo Xie, Yan Yang, Rongguang Feng
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a miniaturized sinusoidal microwave photonic receiving antenna (Sin-MPRA) for optical frequency comb (OFC) undersampling receivers operating from 1 to 18 GHz. Through the parallel configuration of the radiating structure and loading with an absorbing cavity, along with the embedding of a thin-film lithium niobate optical electric-field sensor (OES) featuring an exponentially tapered electrode, the antenna impedance is sufficiently tuned to directly achieve optimal voltage division, thereby eliminating the need for a high-profile, standalone impedance transformer. Capacitive edge loading enhances low-frequency response, while optimized copper-wire feed ensure near-lossless voltage transfer. The prototype achieves an equivalent system gain exceeding 0 dBi across the band, with a peak of 13.34 dBi, despite its compact size of 0.24 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</sub> in diameter and 0.04 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</sub> in height. Multi-signal reception in an OFC undersampling system validates its potential for wideband spectrum sensing on space-constrained platforms.

title: Drones in Automated and Robotized Internal Logistics: a Review of Optimization and Control Techniques
authors: Mojtaba Porghoveh, Raffaele Carli, Mariagrazia Dotoli
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Drones have emerged as powerful tools for automation, real-time monitoring, and data collection, and their use in logistics continues to expand. In internal logistics, covering warehousing, inventory management, and intra-logistics tasks, drones offer opportunities to improve operational efficiency, accuracy, and cost-effectiveness. Despite these advantages, deploying drones in enclosed and cluttered indoor environments without GPS requires reliable control and mission-level optimization strategies. This paper reviews recent approaches to drone mission control and optimization for internal logistics applications over the past decade. Drone applications are organized into three categories: inventory management, surveillance and inspection, and intra-logistics operations. For each category, we discuss key challenges spanning mission-level decision problems such as task allocation and path planning, and execution-level requirements such as navigation, obstacle avoidance, and flight control. We compare classical, robust, and adaptive control methods with emerging learning-based approaches, highlighting their advantages, limitations, and implementation challenges. Finally, we identify open issues and outline research directions related to swarm coordination and human–drone interaction, which are important for scalable deployment in modern internal logistics environments.

title: Machine Learning Models for Emotion Prediction Using Eye-Gaze Features
authors: Sharon Zachariah, Harshitha P G, Shreya Paul, Vishaka Biju, Meenakshi Malhotra, Deepshik Sharma, Arun Sasidharan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Eye-gaze behavior provides rich, non-intrusive cues that reflect underlying cognitive and emotional processes, placing it as one of the most promising modalities for affective computing for emotion prediction. This work investigates four different machine learning models-namely, Random Forest, CatBoost, XGBoost, and a Multi-Layer Perceptron classifier to predict five emotional states-anger, disgust, fear, sadness, and happiness-using eye gaze-derived features. Emotion prediction is usually achieved using facial features or speech analysis. Eye gaze features analysis will be complementary in emotion prediction. The six most relevant oculometric features include average position, dispersion of gaze, pupil dilation, and saccade speed. The experimental results obtained show a significant difference in performance among the models tested. The highest accuracy obtained by the MLP classifier is 97.62%, followed by 95.41% by XGBoost, 94% by CatBoost, and 90.26% by Random Forest. The results also bring out the relative strengths of gradient boosting and deep neural models in capturing complex emotional signatures embedded in gaze dynamics. In general, the study also illustrates that eye-gaze features are robust enough to classify emotions reliably and the MLP model has better discrimination capability. These comparative insights shall guide future design for gaze-based affective computing systems.

title: An Explainable AI System for Breast Cancer Classification with Tumor Localization and Hormonal Risk Interpretation using EfficientNet B2
authors: S.Nagavarshini
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Breast cancer is among the top causes of death among women in all parts of the world, and thus early and accurate diagnosis is essential to increase chances of survival. The proposed work offers a deep learning-based model of the automated classification of the breast cancer on the basis of the mammograms in combination with the locus and growth investigation of the tumor. A pre-trained EfficientNetB2 model is further adjusted to tumor classification including benign and malignant also, with the accuracy rate of approximately 95 percent and a high Area Under the Curve (AUC) of prediction, demonstrating strong prediction power. Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), to make the model easier to interpret, a heatmap that represents key locations affecting prediction is generated. These heatmaps are then subjected to tumor segmentation, contour and area estimation to be able to visualize tumor localization and determine tumor growth patterns. Also, a basic risk stratification tool using Area of tumor is presented to classify cases in low, medium and high-risk groups. The system also includes a primitive visualization of hormonal factors to connect exaggerations with potentially biological effects.

title: Improving Semi-Supervised Contrastive Learning via Entropy-Weighted Confidence Integration of Anchor-Positive Pairs∗
authors: Shogo Nakayama, Masahiro Okuda
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Conventional semi-supervised contrastive learning methods assign pseudo-labels only to samples whose highest predicted class probability exceeds a predefined threshold, and then perform supervised contrastive learning using those selected samples. In this study, we propose a novel loss function that estimates the confidence of each sample based on the entropy of its predicted probability distribution and applies confidencebased adaptive weighting. This approach enables pseudo-label assignment even to samples that were previously excluded from training and facilitates contrastive learning that accounts for the confidence of both anchor and positive samples in a more principled manner. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves classification accuracy and achieves more stable learning performance even under low-label conditions.

title: AI-Powered Missing Child Detection and Retrieval Using Deep Face Matching Algorithms
authors: Santhiya K, Sivasankari A, M. Varshana Devi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The rising cases of missing children have become a burning international problem that requires fast and smart systems of identification. The current paper proposes a new solution, AI-Powered Missing Child Detection and Retrieval, designed to help police officers with missing children using deep learning and facial recognition technologies to locate missing children in the most effective way possible. There are two Multi-Task Cascaded Convolutional Networks (MTCNN) that are used in the proposed system to detect every face accurately and the FaceNet that is used to obtain the high-dimensional facial embeddings, which separate unique facial characteristics. Images posted by the users are cross-tabulated with a secured database of missing children and upon crossing a similarity threshold, the system sends real-time notifications to the authorities and registered guardians. The platform also has a location-based tracking and a feedback-based loop of learning that allows improvement of the model with time. The proposed system helps save a lot of time in search, identify more, and make data-driven decisions in emergency cases by utilizing artificial intelligence and computer vision. The framework illustrates an efficient, socially effective, and scalable solution which will help in bringing back missing children in a safe manner.

title: AI-Driven Honeypot-based Intrusion Detection System for Intelligent Cyber Threat Analysis
authors: Sathiya Priya S, Ved Lanjewar, Rigzen Chospal, Stanzin Dorjey, M.Malleswari, Bharathi.B
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Cyber-attacks are now more sophisticated than ever; they take place more frequently than ever; and they highlight some of the limitations of traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS). The limitations of IDS have been demonstrated by the growing number of zero-day attacks and advanced persistent threats, which are extremely difficult to accurately detect using traditional methods of IDS. Honeypot-based IDS offer the opportunity to provide proactive security solutions through the establishment of "honeypot" systems that lure in attackers, allowing for the collection and analysis of thorough and precise data on their behaviours. This paper presents an artificial intelligence-based honeypot IDS which takes advantage of and integrates intelligent deployment of honeypots, as well as machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) technologies to provide near-real-time detection and analysis of cyber threats. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), and long short term memory (LSTM) models will be used to evaluate and analyse the behaviour of network traffic as well as the interaction of attackers with honeypots. The user is proposing to use a combination of behavioural anomaly detection and adaptive learning methods to build a system capable of detecting previously known and previously unknown attack vectors with extremely low numbers of false positives. An experimental evaluation of the proposed system using publicly available, standard intrusion detection datasets as well as actual live honeypot traffic demonstrated an accuracy rate of 96.8% for detecting intrusions, a false positive rate of 2.1%, and an F1 score of 96.2%. The real-time functionality showcases the average detection latency time for the system, which is 172 milliseconds, giving the user ample time to rapidly respond to an incident and generate a compliance report.

title: Data-Driven Vendor Performance Analytics in Cloud SCM
authors: Prasad Sundaramoorthy, Soumya Remella, Kiran Konakalla, Mihir Dhakan, Sudheekar Reddy Pothireddy, Quazi Taif Sadat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The fast-adaptive implementation of cloud-based Supply Chain Management (SCM) systems has created enormous amounts of non-homogeneous operational information that allow organizations to redefine vendor assessment in ways other than conventional cost-based metrics. In this paper, the author introduces a vendor performance analytics model of Cloud SCM based on the application of the latest analytics and machine learning methods so as to provide objective, scalable and real-time data on supplier performance. The proposed solution combines the multi-dimensional vendor indicators such as delivery reliability, cost compliance, quality conformance, service responsiveness and risk exposure into a single cloud analytics pipeline. Through predictive modelling and trend analysis, the framework allows identifying inefficiencies of vendors and likely disruption of the supply chain early. Experimental analysis performed in cloud-hosted SCM data sets proves greater accuracy in ranking vendors, greater level of decision transparency and a major decrease of risks in procurements in opposition to the traditional approach of utilizing scorecards. The findings reveal the utility of data-driven analytics in strategic vendor selection support, continuous performance monitoring, and continuous supply chain operation in dynamic cloud environments.

title: Predictive Modelling for Market Behaviour and Trading
authors: Vrishin Gaikwad, Yash Srivastava, M. Kanchana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: One of the major challenges associated with stock market predictions is their high volatility, non-linearities, and noisy time series data. To overcome these challenges, this study proposes a comparative analysis method for short-term stock market direction predictions. The methodology compares the performance of sophisticated sequence-based deep learning architectures, such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Transformers, with a conventional ensemble machine learning method using XGBoost. To accomplish this, a system is designed using data preprocessing, technical indicator-based feature engineering, supervised sequence learning, and a meta-learning ensemble method. Based on the experimental results using a wide range of data, including over 400 Indian stocks, deep learning architectures have shown conservative bias towards the majority class, resulting in poor recall, while failing to capture significant temporal patterns. On the other hand, the XGBoost method showed excellent performance, achieving high accuracy of 0.633 and area under the ROC curve of 0.686. Therefore, gradient boosting methods have shown high effectiveness in learning non-linear relationships between technical indicators, while deep learning methods failed to overcome low signal-to-noise ratio issues.

title: Design and Implementation of an AR Shooting Game System Integrating Body Movements and Voice Input
authors: Riko Suwabe, Masasuke Yasumoto, Keisuke Utsu
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: We present the design and implementation of an Augmented Reality (AR) application centered on the integration of body movements and voice input, aiming to clarify the insights and challenges of such interaction and to explore its potential applications. As an implementation example, we developed the shooting game system “Voisho.” In this system, when a player speaks, the spoken content is generated in real time as 3D text and fired as projectiles into the AR space. By combining this with aiming and evasion through body movements, the system realizes a novel interaction that requires the simultaneous execution of voice and physical actions. Observations of participants who played the game at an exhibition suggested the potential to enhance players' sense of presence and agency. Furthermore, the system has potential applications in domains that demand active physical and cognitive engagement, such as education and rehabilitation.

title: Comparison between the LSTM and Hierarchical LSTM Architectures in Improving Brain Tumor Detection in MRI data
authors: Yaakulya Sabbani, S Venkata Achuta Rao, Sk. Mahaboob Basha, M Senthil Kumar, Mahaveerakannan R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The MRI data of brain tumors needs to be segmented with accuracy and reliability to enhance outcomes of patients. Recurrent deep learning models and many others are effective with the temporal and spatial medical imaging data. This research classifies brain cancer with the use of both a traditional LSTM network and HLSTM structure using a Kaggle MRI dataset. The HLSTM framework was however tested together with the baseline LSTM model since it is a more complex hierarchical model and was anticipated to capture more complex hierarchical correlations among the feature representations. The performance of the models was calculated with ROC-AUC, F1, Accuracy, Precision, and Recall to complete it. The accuracy of LSTM networks in the context of classification is quite reasonable but studies indicate that HLSTM models can perform better than LSTM networks in most assessment measures. The hierarchical nature of the HLSTM aids in abstraction of tumor features to minimize cases where the false negativity is high, and cases where the sensitivity is high. The authors find that HLSTM is a strong and effective automated brain tumour detection technology that has significant clinical decision-support systems. When discussing the concept of the theoretical discussion, one can consider cntd & vd Human-In-The-Loop machine league based on the combination of HLSTM with xAI and attention mechanism to implement the field to the next level and enhance the diagnosis transparency and reliability. Their comparisons showed that medical imaging data can be analyzed with the help of hierarchical model. In the best case scenario, HLSTM, rather than LSTM, can enhance brain tumor detection and result in scalable and automated health care solutions. The model can be enhanced with attention, collective data of all photo groups, and clarifiable artificially intelligent to allow doctors to comprehend forecasts.

title: Advanced Classification of AI-Generated Images Through Transformers
authors: B. Manojna, T. Pavan Venkata Satya Sai Ajay, V. Eswar Kishore Raju, M. Naga Lokesh, N. Bhargav Sai Ram
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The prevalence of the artificial intelligence-driven image creation poses a threat to the digital confidence and content verification, along with the safety of the data. A deep learning model to classify real and AI-generation images is discussed in the paper which strongly classifies real and AI generation images when using three attention-based architectures i.e. MobileNetV3-Large with lightweight Efficient Channel Attention (ECA), ResNet-50 with Squeeze-Excitation (SE) and EfficientNetV2-S with cross-attention. The two models rely on the refinement of representation based on attention, and Gradient weighted Class Activation Mapping (Gradcam) provides interpretable heatmaps, which are useful to comprehend the parts of the decision-making. It has been implemented as flask-based web application, the front end of which is user friendly and enables real time uploading and classification of images. On the CIFAKE data, the networks ResNet 50 + SE have a competitive performance of 96.20 validation and MobileNet V3 large + ECA of 95.83 with higher computation efficiency. The results indicate that accuracy, efficiency, and explainability should be considered to identify synthetic contents in a trustworthy manner. This journal contributes to the practical solution to the issues of media verification, digital forensics, and security service applications where the distinction between authentic and AI-generated media is becoming a necessity.

title: IMM-SLT: Multimodal Fusion for Enhanced Sign Language Translation
authors: Kiruthika N, Thangamariappan P, Sivam V, Rokhit Ranjan V M, Sudharsan S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The paper presents the multimodal architecture of IMM-SLT, which is aimed at improving automatic Sign Language Translation (SLT) through video, pose, and face signals. The traditional SLT programs are based on visual image or movement of hands now, and it is only that; this makes their opportunity to capture the linguistic and grammatical richness of sign language very limited. Conversely, the sign language communication is intrinsically based on non-manual movements as well as on manual ones, i.e., facial expressions and body language. In order to overcome this shortcoming, the offered strategy model jointly estimates spatial-temporal video distribution, skeletal posture data, and facial expression data in a single deep learning system. Video frames are handled to find motion and appearance, pose estimation methods are completed to model organized joint movements, and facial analysis models are utilized to find the expressive non-manual expressions. The integration of these heterogeneous characteristics by means of an effective multimodal fusion strategy is done in a way that these characteristics are synchronized and integrated across time so as to facilitate further semantic interpretation of sign gestures. The multimodal representation that results is converted into natural language text through a sequence-to-sequence translation model. As experiments show, the proposed IMM-SLT module is able to not only cut down on the time spent in translation by about 23% in comparison with the video-only methods but also improve the computational efficiency, as well as generate a higher accuracy in the translation itself, especially with regard to complex signs that need facial and grammatical nuances to render. These results point to the significance of using multimodal integration to translate the sign language effectively, and indicate the relevance of the suggested system in the context of assistive communication and inclusive human-computer interaction.

title: Design and Implementation of an Embedded Offline Real-Time Sensor Network for Aquaponics Monitoring
authors: Mohammad Farid bin Saaid, Haris Murshidi bin Mat Isa, Mohd Khairi bin Nordin, Adizul bin Ahmad, Mohamad Farid bin Misnan, Norashikin M. Thamrin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Aquaponics is a key technology for sustainable food production, yet its broader adoption is constrained by challenges in scalability, real-time process control, and nutrient management. This study presents the design, implementation, and evaluation of an embedded, offline real-time sensor network for aquaponics monitoring. Instead of focusing on active automated control, the primary objective was to evaluate system performance, scalability, and nutrient dynamics through high-frequency sensor-based data acquisition. The system featured a cocopeat-based grow bed cultivating Pak Choy and was tested under varying Tilapia stocking densities to simulate scalable conditions. Powered by an Arduino Mega 2560 microcontroller, an array of sensors (pH, TDS, NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, NPK, and temperature) provided continuous data logging at 5-second intervals to a local SD card, ensuring operational independence from network connectivity. Results show that the offline monitoring framework effectively tracked critical water quality parameters, verifying the biofiltration system's capacity to adapt to a 200% increase in fish biomass while maintaining stable ammonia levels. A direct correlation was established between increased fish density and higher concentrations of TDS and macronutrients (Phosphorus and Potassium), leading to a statistically significant increase in plant growth. The findings demonstrate that affordable, microcontroller-based offline monitoring technologies can significantly enhance the data-driven management and scalability of aquaponics, offering a robust and accessible solution for urban and commercial agriculture.

title: Predicting Food Market Trends With Regression Models
authors: Madduru Sresta, K.Meghaneswari, S Pushparani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Many items have a limited shelf life and Even poor inventory management can result in significant waste and loss for the organization Reliable demand projections have become critical, especially in the food industry. Recent work on handling time-dependent data has shown significant gains for several machine learning approaches. The proposed work analyzes the impact of different factors on demand, extracts the characteristic features that may have an influence, and suggests a comparison analysis of regressors to anticipate the number of orders using the "Food Demand Forecasting" dataset made public by Genpact. Random Forest Regressor, Cat Boost Regressor, and Ensemble Model as a part of Machine Learning and Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM) Bi-directional Long-Short Term Memory (BILSTM) and Ensemble of LSTM and BILSTM Models as a part of Deep Learning are some of the regression Model investigated in this paper for increasing the accurate demand forecasting. Along with Explainable AI(XAI) framework includes in this model to determine the features that affect the target variable (number of orders) that helps the hotel management for maintaining the stock of raw materials for the upcoming weeks. The outcomes show the Innovative Deep Learning Ensemble Model Paradigm’s potential in predicting how it works better than deep learning and other machine learning methods. By integrating XAI framework that provides the reason for satisfying or not satisfying the need of hotel management subsequently to the customer.

title: FraudGraph-X: A Scalable Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network Framework for Real-Time Financial Fraud Detection
authors: S.P.Vidyassri, Somalaraju Sankar, Rakshana K, P.L.Mamishna, Shrutha Kieerthy, Kaashvi Madhavan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Real-time fraud detection in large-scale financial systems remains a critical challenge due to the dynamic, sparse, and imbalanced nature of transactional data. Traditional machine learning models and even some deep learning methods fail to generalize effectively across evolving fraud patterns, often trading off between scalability, precision, and latency. This paper presents FraudGraph-X, a novel Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework designed to detect financial fraud in real time. FraudGraph-X integrates two parallel branches: a GraphSAGE-based spatial branch that captures transaction relationships, and a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) branch that models sequential behavior. These branches are fused using a custom attention mechanism that highlights discriminative temporal-spatial features. The model is trained using a hybrid loss function comprising binary cross-entropy, focal loss, and temporal smoothness loss to address severe class imbalance and ensure consistent predictions across time. Evaluated on the Elliptic Bitcoin Transaction dataset, FraudGraph-X achieves an accuracy of 96.72%, precision of 94.85%, recall of 92.63%, and F1-score of 93.73%, outperforming state-of-the-art models like GCN, TGAT, and XGBoost. Moreover, the model delivers a real-time inference latency of 28.3 ms and a throughput of 1250 transactions per second, making it suitable for deployment in streaming environments. The results confirm that combining graph structure with time-aware learning significantly enhances detection accuracy and responsiveness. FraudGraph-X represents a scalable and interpretable advancement in fraud analytics for modern financial systems.

title: A Machine Learning-based National Startup Capital Readiness Scoring Platform: Design, Implementation, and Comparative Evaluation
authors: Md Ismail Jobiullah, Sakera Begum, Md Musa Ali, Md Mashfiqur Rahman, Md Shamsul Arefin Emon, Kaniz Fatima
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: This paper presents the design and evaluation of a National Startup Capital Readiness Scoring Platform that employs machine learning to objectively assess startup investment readiness. The platform integrates a multi-tier web architecture comprising a React-based frontend, Node.js backend, PostgreSQL database, and a modular AI/ML scoring engine with external NLP (Hugging Face) and geospatial (Geoapify) API integrations. Using the publicly available Crunchbase-derived Startup Success Prediction dataset (923 US-based startups, 1980s–2013), we comparatively evaluate Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost classifiers. XGBoost achieves the highest performance (85.9% accuracy, AUC-ROC = 0.91), followed by Random Forest (84.3%, AUC-ROC = 0.89) and Logistic Regression (79.5%, AUC-ROC = 0.84). SHAP-based feature importance analysis reveals that total funding, investor relationships, milestone achievements, and time-to-first-funding are the most significant predictors. The platform provides component-level feedback across eight readiness dimensions, serving as a data-driven tool for founders, investors, and policymakers.

title: Design and Implementation of a Lattice-Based Post-Quantum Secure Bootloader for RISC-V with QEMU-Based Validation
authors: Durga Lakshmi Mownish Bugatha, Rani Medikonda
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of RISC-V architectures in embedded, IoT, and safety-critical systems necessitates quantum-resistant secure boot mechanisms to counter harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks targeting classical RSA/ECDSA firmware signatures. This paper presents PQBoot-C, a complete 52 kB production-ready C implementation of a lattice-based post-quantum secure bootloader for RISC-V 64-bit processors featuring NIST-standardized Dilithium2 (ML-DSA) signature verification. PQBoot-C establishes a quantum-resilient hardware root-of-trust through a multi-stage PQChain verification protocol: SHA3-256 measured boot computes kernel integrity, Dilithium2 authenticates firmware against an embedded public key, and Physical Memory Protection (PMP) enforces hardware-isolated execution. Comprehensive QEMU RV64GCV emulation enables reproducible cycle-accurate profiling, demonstrating a bounded total secure boot cost of 1.43 million clock cycles, corresponding to 14.3 ms on a 100 MHz core and 1.43 ms on a 1 GHz core.Cross-platform validation confirms deployment readiness across QEMU virt, VisionFive2, and HiFive Unmatched platforms, with deterministic execution and full UART traceability. Security analysis demonstrates 128-bit IND-CCA quantum resistance with tamper-evident logging and constant-time Dilithium2 via masked pqm4 integration. The crypto-agile design supports future PQC migration (Falcon, SPHINCS+) while maintaining a sub-100 kB footprint suitable for resource-constrained embedded deployment. PQBoot-C provides the first open-source, QEMU-reproducible baseline for post-quantum RISC-V secure boot, bridging the critical research-to-deployment gap.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimization for Taxi Ride Sharing
authors: Anirudh A Zalki, Ayush Nag, Harshad S Magdum, Praveen U Alur, Uday Kulkarni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Ride-sharing systems have to assign drivers to different zones when adapting to variations in demand and travel times, and costs. Traditional rule-based approaches and static optimisation are not sufficient for handling such real-time challenges. This work It introduces PPO, which is a reinforcement learning-based framework. That utilizes the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm to enhance vehicle dispatch, dynamic pricing and driver positioning. The system uses time-ordered historical ride data to make predictions. demand at specific zones and times, creating a 2D zone-time demand matrix for reinforcement learning. A custom Gymnasium Environment simulates multi-zone operations, driver distribution, pooling capacity and cost-time limits. The PPO agent learns effective dispatch and pricing methods by interacting with the environment through a reward function that balances pooling efficiency, fleet distribution, passenger waiting time, and cost of trip. Tests using NYC Yellow Taxi data show meaningful improvements, with a mean journey time of 23.76 minutes, an average pooling rate of 0.93, an average reward of 1.193, and an average trip cost of $15.14. These results demonstrate that PPO is a scalable Data-driven solution for modern ride-sharing challenges.
